title: Visual: approval dashboard route is missing
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, viewport 1280x800. Expected: Compliance approval queue with pending submissions, status pills, and approve/reject affordances per specs/web-ui.md. Actual: /review renders the browse landing page; there is no pending submissions list, status pill, or approval action. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/05-review-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx implements only the registry browser and modal detail view, with no approval routes.
close_reason: Implemented: added /review approval dashboard route with pending queue, status pills, and decision actions

title: Visual: submission wizard route is missing
description: Flow: Submission at /submit, viewport 1280x800. Expected: upload/publish wizard with validation for required fields, oversize zip, and bad SKILL.md per specs/web-ui.md. Actual: /submit renders the browse landing page with no publish/upload entry point or validation states. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/04-submit-route.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx has no router or submission workflow UI.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-9i0; same missing publish/upload flow evidenced by /submit fallback.

title: Visual: dev stack cannot start for web inspection
description: Flow: dev stack startup for ASR web visual review. Viewport: not reached. Expected: docker compose API health at http://localhost:3001/api/health returns {"status":"ok"} and pnpm --filter @asr/web dev starts Vite on http://localhost:5173. Actual: pnpm is not available in the shell (zsh: command not found: pnpm), so the web dev server cannot start; API health also failed with curl exit 7 on localhost:3001 after docker compose up -d. Screenshot path: none, UI inspection could not begin. Root cause hypothesis: local dev environment or compose stack is missing required Node package manager/API service wiring for the documented visual-review workflow.
close_reason: Implemented: compose now starts Forgejo, a dev API health/mock registry service on :3001, and the Vite web app on :5173 using Corepack-managed pnpm; verified /api/health and browser rendering.

title: Wire entrypoint guard into Dockerfile.api ENTRYPOINT
description:
Parent story: asr-nbj
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#production-mode-enforcement (lines 178-187); specs/deployment.md (Dockerfile.api build)

Context:
The guard script only protects boots if the image runs it before node. asr-mcx.1
creates deploy/docker/Dockerfile.api with `CMD ["node",...]` and explicitly defers
the entrypoint guard to this story. This task edits that Dockerfile to install the
guard as the ENTRYPOINT so it runs ahead of the CMD.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/Dockerfile.api (edit; created by asr-mcx.1)
- deploy/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh (from asr-nbj task 1; COPYed into the image)

Change:
In the runner stage of Dockerfile.api: COPY deploy/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh to
/usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh, `RUN chmod +x` it, and add
`ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh"]` while keeping the existing
`CMD ["node","packages/submission/dist/index.js"]` so the guard runs before node.

Acceptance:
After `docker build -f deploy/docker/Dockerfile.api -t asr-api:dev .`,
`docker run --rm -e NODE_ENV=production -e AUTH_MODE=mock asr-api:dev` exits non-zero
and prints the FATAL message, while `docker run --rm -e NODE_ENV=production
-e AUTH_MODE=entra -e DATABASE_PATH=/tmp/w.db asr-api:dev` boots and answers 200 on
its liveness route.

Risks / out of scope:
Depends on asr-mcx.1 (the Dockerfile must exist) and asr-nbj task 1 (the guard
script). Do not change the CMD or duplicate the app build steps — this task owns only
the entrypoint wiring.

title: Add Docker entrypoint guard refusing prod+mock boot
description:
Parent story: asr-nbj
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#production-mode-enforcement (lines 178-187)

Context:
Hard rule: NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock MUST fail to boot. This is the
container-layer defence in depth on top of the app-level guard (Phase 1). A mock
identity in prod would let anyone assume any role — a launch-blocking security hole.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh (new)
- spec source: specs/submission-package.md:182-187

Change:
Create deploy/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh: shebang '#!/bin/sh'; then
`if [ "$NODE_ENV" = "production" ] && [ "$AUTH_MODE" = "mock" ]; then
echo "FATAL: AUTH_MODE=mock is forbidden in production" >&2; exit 78; fi`; then
`exec "$@"`. Mark the file executable.

Acceptance:
`NODE_ENV=production AUTH_MODE=mock sh deploy/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh true`
exits 78 and prints the FATAL message to stderr; `NODE_ENV=production
AUTH_MODE=entra sh deploy/docker/docker-entrypoint.sh true` exits 0.

Risks / out of scope:
Use POSIX sh (the runtime image may lack bash). Exit code must be non-zero (78 per
spec). Do not duplicate other env validation — only the prod+mock combination is
checked at this layer.
close_reason: Implemented: added POSIX Docker entrypoint guard that exits 78 for NODE_ENV=production with AUTH_MODE=mock

title: Add 0002 scan_results migration + repository keyed by (submissionId, contentHash)
description:
Parent story: asr-st5
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#scan-result-storage (lines 445-461); specs/types.md#scanning (lines 102-114); migration framework asr-7jd.2/.3 (0001 is submissions)

Context:
Every scan verdict must be durable, auditable, and joinable to its submission. The compliance reviewer's GET /submissions/:id/scan response (security-scanning.md lines 463-499) reads from this table. Keying on (submissionId, contentHash) lets a re-submission of identical content reuse/locate its prior scan and ties the row to the canonical hash.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0002_scan_results.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (import + push the 0002 entry, ascending id order)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/scanResults.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/scanResults.test.ts (new)

Change:
Migration `{ id: 2, name: 'scan_results', up }`: CREATE TABLE scan_results(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, submission_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES submissions(id), content_hash TEXT NOT NULL, scanner_image TEXT NOT NULL, started_at TEXT NOT NULL, completed_at TEXT NOT NULL, duration_ms INTEGER NOT NULL, verdict TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(verdict IN ('pass','block','review_required')), finding_count INTEGER NOT NULL, findings_json TEXT NOT NULL, tool_results_json TEXT NOT NULL, signature TEXT, created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))) plus `idx_scan_submission(submission_id)` and `idx_scan_subm_hash(submission_id, content_hash)`. Push into the `migrations` array after 0001. Add `export function insertScanResult(db, report: ScanReport): void` (serialising findings/toolResults to JSON) and `export function getScanResult(db, submissionId: string, contentHash: string): ScanReport | undefined` (row→ScanReport, parsing JSON back to the @asr/core shape).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/scanResults.test.ts: after runMigrations(db), insertScanResult(db, sampleReport) then getScanResult(db, sampleReport.submissionId, sampleReport.contentHash) deep-equals the inserted ScanReport (verdict, findings, toolResults round-trip); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Numbering is fixed: 0001 = submissions (asr-7jd.3), so this is 0002 — do not renumber. Depends on the migration runner (asr-7jd.2) and the submissions table (asr-7jd.3) for the FK. Import ScanReport from @asr/core (asr-btm); do NOT redefine it. The GET /submissions/:id/scan handler is a separate workflow/API task — only the repository here.

title: Implement runScanner runner returning a @asr/core ScanReport
description:
Parent story: asr-st5
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#api-integration (lines 419-443); specs/submission-package.md (src/scan/runner.ts line 42; SCANNER_IMAGE/SCANNER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS/SCANNER_SEVERITY_THRESHOLD lines 171-174); specs/types.md#scanning (lines 102-121)

Context:
The workflow's scan node (Flowcraft blueprint asr-82o — reference only) needs a host-side function that launches the scanner container against an already-extracted skill dir and hands back the canonical ScanReport for persistence and verdict branching. This is the boundary between the untyped in-container orchestrator and the typed @asr/core world.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/scan/runScanner.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/scan/runScanner.test.ts (new)

Change:
`export interface RunScannerInput { submissionId: string; contentHash: string; extractedDir: string }`. `export async function runScanner(input: RunScannerInput): Promise<ScanReport>` where `ScanReport` is imported from `@asr/core`. It `docker run --rm` the `SCANNER_IMAGE` (from env via asr-6ax.2) with `-v ${extractedDir}:/scan/input:ro`, a temp `:/scan/output`, and env `SUBMISSION_ID, CONTENT_HASH, SCANNER_IMAGE, SCAN_SEVERITY_THRESHOLD, SCAN_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, SCAN_SIGNING_KEY` and pass-through `VERACODE_API_KEY_ID/SECRET`; timeout = `SCANNER_TIMEOUT_SECONDS*1000 + buffer`. Parse stdout JSON, re-cross-check `computeVerdict(report.findings, threshold)` from `@asr/core` equals `report.verdict` (defence-in-depth), and verify the detached `signature` (HMAC-SHA256 over the report JSON sans signature, key = `SCAN_SIGNING_KEY`); throw if signature missing/invalid when a key is configured. Return the typed `ScanReport`. Inject the docker spawn (a `runContainer` arg defaulting to execFile) so the test can stub it.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/scan/runScanner.test.ts: with a stubbed container that returns a correctly HMAC-signed block report, `runScanner({...})` resolves to a ScanReport with `verdict==='block'` and the asserted shape; a tampered report (signature over different bytes) makes `runScanner` reject; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Import ScanReport AND computeVerdict from @asr/core — do NOT redefine either (depends on asr-btm). Do NOT persist to the DB here (task 04 owns scan_results). Do NOT wire this into the Flowcraft blueprint (asr-82o). zip extraction that produces extractedDir is asr-crh.1's job — runScanner takes the dir as input.
close_reason: Implemented: added signed scanner container runner returning canonical ScanReport

title: Implement in-container scan-orchestrator (parallel tools, SARIF aggregate, signed verdict)
description:
Parent story: asr-st5
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#scan-orchestrator (lines 162-358); ### Severity Mapping (lines 99-109); ### Blocking Rules (lines 110-120); ### Environment Variables (lines 82-98)

Context:
This is the in-container entrypoint that actually runs the tools. It runs Gitleaks/Trivy/Foxguard/Opengrep in parallel, aggregates each tool's SARIF, normalizes per-tool severity labels to critical/high/medium/low, computes the pass/block/review_required verdict, signs the report JSON, and emits it. It runs INSIDE the scanner image (no @asr/core import available there), so it carries its own self-contained types matching the canonical ScanReport shape.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/scanner/scan-orchestrator.mjs (new)

Change:
Port the spec's orchestrator (lines 168-356) as ESM. Tools run via `Promise.all`; each `runX` parses its SARIF with the shared `parseSarif`. Implement `mapSarifSeverity` (error→high, warning→medium, note→low) and the per-tool severity table (lines 103-108); Gitleaks findings forced to `high` (line 106). `computeVerdict`: critical→block; ANY gitleaks finding→block (secret hard-block, line 119); else high→review_required (respecting SCAN_SEVERITY_THRESHOLD); else pass. Opengrep skipped when no scripts/ or `OPENGREP_ENABLED=false`; Veracode skipped when `VERACODE_API_KEY_ID` unset (toolResults entry `skipped:true`). Emit a ScanReport with `submissionId, scanId(ULID), contentHash(env CONTENT_HASH), scannerImage(env SCANNER_IMAGE), startedAt, completedAt, durationMs, verdict, findings, toolResults: Record<tool,{exitCode,findingCount,skipped?}>` and add `signature` = HMAC-SHA256 hex over the canonical report JSON (key from env `SCAN_SIGNING_KEY`) when the key is set. Write `report.json` to OUTPUT_DIR, print the JSON to stdout, `process.exit(verdict==='block'?1:0)`.

Acceptance:
`node deploy/docker/scanner/scan-orchestrator.mjs` against a temp dir whose `.git`-free tree contains a file with a hardcoded `AKIA...` style key (Gitleaks SARIF present) prints JSON with `verdict==='block'` and `toolResults.gitleaks.findingCount>=1`; against an empty/clean dir it prints `verdict==='pass'`. Run as a small node test or `docker run` smoke; assert the stdout JSON parses and matches the expected verdict.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT import @asr/core here — this file is bundled into the image with no node_modules workspace. Keep the emitted field names byte-identical to the canonical ScanReport (specs/types.md lines 102-114) so the runner (task 03) can validate without reshaping. The host-side runScanner + DB storage are tasks 03/04. Custom Opengrep rule files are out of scope.
close_reason: Implemented: standalone scanner orchestrator with parallel tool execution, SARIF aggregation, verdict computation, optional HMAC signature, report emission, and node:test coverage for secret-block and clean-pass cases. Targeted test passes: node --test deploy/docker/scanner/scan-orchestrator.test.mjs. Repo gates attempted: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile passes; pnpm build fails in existing asr-999; pnpm test fails in existing asr-4y3.

title: Add pinned-version scanner Docker image (Gitleaks+Trivy+Foxguard+Opengrep)
description:
Parent story: asr-st5
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#scanner-docker-image (lines 121-161); ## Tool Stack (lines 15-38); ### Environment Variables (lines 82-98)

Context:
Code-containing skills are scanned in a dedicated, hermetic container so no scanner tool ever runs in the API process (the in-process plugin model was deleted, specs/security.md lines 120-122). This task builds the image that all four Tier-1/Tier-2 tools live in, with pinned versions so prod builds are reproducible (`:latest` is forbidden, security-scanning.md line 17).

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile (new)
- deploy/docker/scanner/rules/.gitkeep (new) — placeholder for the custom-rules dir COPYed at build (security-scanning.md line 147)
- deploy/docker/scanner/README.md (new) — records the resolved pins + the security-scanning.md line-17 pre-flight notes

Change:
Add a multi-stage `FROM node:22-slim` Dockerfile that installs Gitleaks (COPY --from a PINNED `zricethezav/gitleaks:<pin>`), Trivy (PINNED installer + `trivy --download-db-only`), Foxguard (`npm i -g foxguard@<pin>`), and Opengrep (PINNED release asset, not `/latest/`). NO `:latest` tags anywhere. Veracode is NOT baked in (Tier 3 optional, security-scanning.md lines 33-37). `COPY rules/ /opt/scan/rules/`, `WORKDIR /scan`, `COPY scan-orchestrator.mjs /opt/scan/`, `ENTRYPOINT ["node","/opt/scan/scan-orchestrator.mjs"]`. Resolve the placeholder version pins per the line-17 pre-flight checklist and record each chosen pin in README.md.

Acceptance:
`docker build -t asr-scanner:test -f deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile deploy/docker/scanner` succeeds, and `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh asr-scanner:test -c 'command -v gitleaks && command -v trivy && command -v opengrep && command -v foxguard'` exits 0 (all four binaries resolvable on PATH). No line in the Dockerfile matches `:latest`.

Risks / out of scope:
The scan-orchestrator.mjs body is owned by task 02 (this task only references it in COPY/ENTRYPOINT — the build needs the file present, so land 02 first or stub it). Do NOT add Veracode to the image. Do NOT pin to `:latest`. Custom rule authoring (rules/sast/*) is out of scope — only the dir + .gitkeep here.
close_reason: Implemented: pinned scanner image with Gitleaks v8.30.1, Trivy v0.70.0, Foxguard 0.8.1, and Opengrep v1.22.0. Acceptance docker build, PATH check, and no-latest grep pass; repo-wide build/test remain blocked by tracked unrelated beads asr-999 and asr-9qb.

title: Add in-process boot hard-fail for NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock
description:
Parent story: asr-k4m
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#production-mode-enforcement (lines 178-187); specs/security.md#dev-mode (lines 170-171)

Context:
Production must never accidentally boot with mock auth. The Dockerfile entrypoint enforces this externally, but the app must also refuse in-process so the guarantee holds when run outside the container (tests, local prod-mode runs). Per submission-package.md line 180 this in-process check lives in src/auth/entra.ts and runs at app boot.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/auth/entra.ts (extend: add assertAuthModeAllowed; sibling 02 creates the file)
- packages/submission/src/auth/bootGuard.test.ts (new)

Change:
In entra.ts add `export function assertAuthModeAllowed(env = process.env): void` that throws `new Error('FATAL: AUTH_MODE=mock is forbidden in production')` (string verbatim from submission-package.md line 184 / matches env superRefine in asr-6ax.2) when `env.NODE_ENV==='production' && env.AUTH_MODE==='mock'`. Wire the app composition (sibling that builds the auth-mounted app, or src/index.ts per asr-6ax.3) to call `assertAuthModeAllowed()` at module load before constructing middleware, so an uncaught throw produces a non-zero exit.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/auth/bootGuard.test.ts: `assertAuthModeAllowed({ NODE_ENV:'production', AUTH_MODE:'mock' })` throws /forbidden in production/; `assertAuthModeAllowed({ NODE_ENV:'development', AUTH_MODE:'mock' })` does not throw; `assertAuthModeAllowed({ NODE_ENV:'production', AUTH_MODE:'entra' })` does not throw. Story-level boot check: spawning the entrypoint with NODE_ENV=production AUTH_MODE=mock exits non-zero. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Overlap: this duplicates two existing guards on purpose — the zod env superRefine (asr-6ax.2) and the Dockerfile entrypoint `exit 78` (asr-nbj / hard rule). Keep all three message strings identical ('FATAL: AUTH_MODE=mock is forbidden in production') so logs match regardless of which fires first. Do not remove or relax the env-module refinement; this is the auth-layer belt-and-suspenders required by submission-package.md line 180. Do not change the Dockerfile here.

title: Add requireRole guard and role-matrix path exemptions
description:
Parent story: asr-k4m
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#role-matrix (lines 149-163); specs/security.md#role-based-access (lines 145-154)

Context:
Once an `identity` is on the context (mock from sibling 01 or Entra from sibling 02), each endpoint must be gated by the required role per the spec role matrix; unauthenticated requests return 401 and authenticated-but-wrong-role return 403. `/health`, `/version` and `/webhooks/*` are exempt (api.md lines 162-163). This guard is what individual route handlers (POST /submissions etc.) wire in to enforce the matrix.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/auth/requireRole.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/auth/requireRole.test.ts (new)

Change:
requireRole.ts: import `Identity, AuthVariables` from './types.js' and `apiError` from '../http/errors.js' (asr-igs.1). Export `requireRole(...roles: string[]): MiddlewareHandler` that reads `c.get('identity')`; if absent → `apiError(c,401,'authentication_required')`; if `identity.roles` intersects none of `roles` → `apiError(c,403,'insufficient_permissions',{ required: roles.join(',') })` (api.md lines 188-189; security.md line 153 SoD is enforced at handler level, not here); else `await next()`. Also export `EXEMPT_PATHS` = ['/health','/version'] plus a `isExemptPath(path: string): boolean` that returns true for those and any path starting '/webhooks/' (api.md lines 162-163), so the app composition can skip auth for them. Do NOT enforce the per-row `sub`-match constraints (those Additional-Constraint cells in the matrix are each handler's job).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/auth/requireRole.test.ts: a Hono test app where `c.set('identity',{sub:'u1',roles:['Compliance']})` then `requireRole('Compliance')` reaches the handler (200); same with roles:['Submitter'] returns res.status===403 and `await res.json()` deep-equals { error:'insufficient_permissions', required:'Compliance' }; with no identity set returns 401 { error:'authentication_required' }; `isExemptPath('/webhooks/forgejo')===true` and `isExemptPath('/submissions')===false`; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not implement separation-of-duties (submitter≠approver) here — that compares `sub` claims at the handler/HITL node (security.md line 153, separation_of_duties_violation enum is a different code). Mid-flow role loss (security.md lines 155-158) is a decision-submit-time check owned by the approve/reject handlers. Keep the exempt list byte-aligned with api.md lines 162-163; note api.md says `/health` while asr-6ax.3 currently mounts `/healthz` and asr-mcx.6 aligns them — reference /health per the matrix.

title: Implement Entra OIDC bearer validation (JWKS, aud, iss, roles)
description:
Parent story: asr-k4m
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#entra-id-oidc (lines 136-143); specs/api.md#entra-id-integration (lines 143-145)

Context:
In prod (AUTH_MODE=entra) every user-facing request carries an Entra ID bearer token that must be cryptographically verified before any role decision. This middleware verifies the JWT against the Entra JWKS, checks aud/iss, extracts the `roles` claim, and places the same `Identity` shape on the Hono context that mock auth (sibling 01) produces, so the role guard (sibling 03) is auth-mode agnostic.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/auth/entra.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/auth/entra.test.ts (new)

Change:
entra.ts: import `Identity, AuthVariables` from './types.js' (sibling 01). Add a JWKS-backed verifier using `jose` (`createRemoteJWKSet` + `jwtVerify`); add "jose":"^5.9.0" to packages/submission deps. Read `AZURE_TENANT_ID` and `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` from process.env (submission-package.md lines 159-160; spec uses AZURE_ prefix). JWKS URL = `https://login.microsoftonline.com/${AZURE_TENANT_ID}/discovery/v2.0/keys` (security.md line 139). Export `entraAuth(): MiddlewareHandler`: extract Bearer token from Authorization header (missing/malformed → `apiError(c,401,'authentication_required')` from src/http/errors.ts, asr-igs.1); `jwtVerify(token, jwks, { audience: AZURE_CLIENT_ID, issuer: \`https://login.microsoftonline.com/${AZURE_TENANT_ID}/v2.0\` })` (security.md lines 140-141); on failure → 401 authentication_required; on success set `c.set('identity', { sub: payload.sub, roles: (payload.roles as string[]) ?? [] })` (security.md line 142) then `await next()`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/auth/entra.test.ts: using a `jose`-generated test keypair, a token signed with correct aud+iss and `roles:['Compliance']` passes (handler sees identity.sub and identity.roles===['Compliance'], 200); a request with no Authorization header returns res.status===401 and `await res.json()` deep-equals { error:'authentication_required' }; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes (JWKS fetch stubbed/injected, no network).

Risks / out of scope:
Do not implement the prod+mock boot guard here in a way that conflicts with sibling 04 — if the guard ships in this same entra.ts file (per submission-package.md line 180), keep it as a separate exported check that 04 wires/asserts. Do not add the role matrix or requireRole here (sibling 03). Token refresh (security.md lines 159-163) is SPA/CLI client-side, out of scope. Note env-var naming: spec is canonical AZURE_TENANT_ID/AZURE_CLIENT_ID (not ENTRA_*); env module (asr-6ax.2) does not yet validate these, so read directly from process.env until the env schema is extended.

title: Add mock auth middleware injecting identity from MOCK_USER_* env
description:
Parent story: asr-k4m
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#dev-mode (lines 164-171); specs/api.md#entra-id-integration (lines 141-147)

Context:
In dev (AUTH_MODE=mock) the service must run without Entra ID by injecting a configurable mock identity, so contributors and the docker dev stack can exercise role-gated routes locally. This is the dev half of the auth middleware; the prod OIDC validator (sibling 02) and the role guard (sibling 03) consume the same `identity` it places on the Hono context.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/auth/types.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/auth/mockAuth.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/auth/mockAuth.test.ts (new)

Change:
types.ts: export `interface Identity { sub: string; roles: string[] }` (app-internal auth type — NOT a @asr/core canonical type). mockAuth.ts: export a Hono `MiddlewareHandler` factory `mockAuth(): MiddlewareHandler` that reads `MOCK_USER_SUB` and `MOCK_USER_ROLES` (CSV) from process.env (per submission-package.md lines 157-158), builds `{ sub: MOCK_USER_SUB, roles: MOCK_USER_ROLES.split(',').map(s=>s.trim()).filter(Boolean) }`, calls `c.set('identity', identity)` and `await next()`. Declare the context variable type via Hono `Variables` (`type AuthVariables = { identity: Identity }`) exported from types.ts so `c.get('identity')` is typed. No token validation in mock mode (security.md line 167).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/auth/mockAuth.test.ts: a Hono test app with `mockAuth()` mounted and env MOCK_USER_SUB='u1', MOCK_USER_ROLES='Submitter,Compliance' on a GET / handler that returns `c.json(c.get('identity'))` yields res.status===200 and `await res.json()` deep-equals { sub:'u1', roles:['Submitter','Compliance'] }; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not validate any bearer token here (that is sibling 02, prod path). Do not add the prod+mock boot guard here (sibling 04 owns it). The SPA mock-auth banner (security.md line 169) is a web-package concern, not this middleware. Keep `Identity` local to packages/submission — do not add it to @asr/core (it is not in the canonical type set).
close_reason: Implemented: mock auth middleware injects MOCK_USER_* identity with regression test

title: Add integration test driving code-path to published and md-only past-skip with mocked HITL+scan
description:
Parent story: asr-82o
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#pipeline (full code path + md-only branch, lines 13-62), #blueprint-sketch (lines 64-171), #state-persistence (lines 176-178)

Context:
This is the story-level integration test: it instantiates the assembled approvalPipeline and drives a real Submission through the engine end to end. It proves the code-containing path traverses every node to status=published using mocked HITL signals and a mocked scan node, and that the md-only branch reaches publish without ever entering questionnaire/scan/review. It guards against edge-routing regressions as later tasks (mutex/resume asr-fc6) build on the blueprint.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.integration.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add a vitest integration test that runs approvalPipeline via the Flowcraft engine with a mocked ForgejoClient registered in ctx.svc (openSubmissionPR->{branch:'submit/x',prNumber:1,headSha:'h'}, mergePR->{sha:'abc'}, publishArtifact->url, deleteBranch->ok), a mocked ctx.audit (records actions), and runScanner mocked (vi.mock '../scan/runner.js') to return a ScanReport with verdict:'pass'. Case A (code-containing): seed ctx files=['skill.md','run.py'] etc.; drive the engine, supplying HITL signals for questionnaire/confirmation/review (resume each pause with a valid response/decision); assert the run reaches the publish node, ctx.get('mergeCommit')==='abc', the visited-node order includes classify->push-to-forgejo->questionnaire->scan->confirmation->review->publish, and the audit recorded 'workflow.review.approved'. Case B (md-only): seed files=['skill.md','README.md']; drive the engine with NO HITL signals; assert it reaches publish, mergeCommit==='abc', and the visited-node set does NOT contain 'questionnaire','scan', or 'review' (it goes classify->push-to-forgejo->auto-approve->publish) and runScanner was never called.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.integration.test.ts: Case A asserts a code-containing submission reaches publish with mergeCommit 'abc' having visited questionnaire/scan/confirmation/review (HITL signals + scan mocked); Case B asserts the md-only submission reaches publish while questionnaire/scan/review were never visited and the mocked runScanner had zero calls; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
runScanner (asr-st5) and the HITL delivery endpoints (asr-kkd) are mocked/simulated here, not exercised live — this test owns the blueprint wiring, not those services. Do not hit a real Forgejo or SQLite — ForgejoClient is mocked and audit is stubbed. Real crash-resume across a restarted engine is asr-fc6, out of scope; this test runs one continuous engine instance.

title: Wire md-only auto-approve branch reusing the shared publish node
description:
Parent story: asr-82o
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#blueprint-sketch (auto-approve node 135-140; auto-approve edges 169-171), #pipeline (md-only branch, lines 15, 25-57)

Context:
The md-only submission must reach the SAME publish node without passing through questionnaire/scan/review. This task adds the auto-approve ComputeNode and the two conditional edges that route a 'md-only' classification from push-to-forgejo through auto-approve into the shared publish node, so both paths converge on one publish (no duplicated merge/upload logic). This depends on the spine (task 01) and is independent of the HITL nodes (task 02).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.ts (extend: add auto-approve node + md-only edges)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Chain onto the exported approvalPipeline:
- `new ComputeNode('auto-approve', { idempotent:true, async execute(ctx){ await ctx.audit('workflow.review.approved', { actor:'system', auto:true }); } })` (audit action from asr-pf0 AUDIT_ACTIONS).
- `.edge('push-to-forgejo','auto-approve', { when: (ctx) => ctx.get('classification') === 'md-only' })`.
- `.edge('auto-approve','publish')`.
The auto-approve edge and the code-path questionnaire edge (task 02) both branch off push-to-forgejo, mutually exclusive on classification; both converge on the single publish node defined in task 01.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.test.ts: the blueprint contains a node 'auto-approve' with idempotent === true, and the outgoing edges from 'push-to-forgejo' include exactly two targets — 'auto-approve' guarded by classification === 'md-only' and 'questionnaire' guarded by classification === 'code-containing'; the 'auto-approve' node has a single outgoing edge to 'publish'; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The md-only path STILL goes through push-to-forgejo + a real PR merge in publish — never write directly to main (specs/workflow.md line 15). Do not duplicate the merge/publishArtifact logic — reuse the publish node from task 01. The compute-only standalone orchestrator publishMdOnly (asr-6hl.3) is a separate Phase-1 specialisation; this blueprint branch is the Flowcraft equivalent and shares the publish node, not that function.

title: Add code-path HITL nodes (questionnaire 7d, confirmation 14d, review 30d Compliance) and scan node with edges
description:
Parent story: asr-82o
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#blueprint-sketch (HITL+scan nodes, lines 97-133; code-path edges 161-167), #timeout-and-expiry (lines 214-222), #separation-of-duties (lines 224-226)

Context:
The code-containing path inserts three HITL pause points and a scan compute node between push-to-forgejo and publish. The HITL nodes carry the spec's exact timeouts (questionnaire 7d, confirmation 14d, review 30d) and actor constraints (confirmation gated to the submitter; Compliance review forbidding the submitter for separation of duties). The scan node calls runScanner and short-circuits to rejected on a block verdict. This builds on the spine from task 01.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.ts (extend: add questionnaire/scan/confirmation/review/rejected nodes + code-path edges)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Chain onto the exported approvalPipeline (import HitlNode from 'flowcraft'; import type { ScanReport } from '@asr/core'; import { runScanner } from '../scan/runner.js' (asr-st5)):
- `new HitlNode('questionnaire', { prompt: () => ({ type:'questionnaire', questions: generateSecurityQuestions() }), validate: (responses) => responses.every(r => r.answer !== undefined), timeout: '7d' })`.
- `new ComputeNode('scan', { idempotent:true, async execute(ctx){ const report: ScanReport = await runScanner({ submissionId: ctx.get('submissionId'), workdir: ctx.get('workdir'), contentHash: ctx.get('contentHash') }); ctx.set('scanReport', report); if (report.verdict === 'block') ctx.jump('rejected'); } })`.
- `new HitlNode('confirmation', { prompt: (ctx) => ({ type:'scan-results', report: ctx.get('scanReport') }), allowedActors: (ctx) => [ctx.get<Submission>('submission').submittedBy], timeout: '14d' })`.
- `new HitlNode('review', { prompt: (ctx) => ({ type:'compliance-approval', submission: ctx.get('submission'), questionnaire: ctx.get('questionnaireResponses'), scanReport: ctx.get('scanReport'), versionDiff: ctx.get('versionDiff') }), requiredRole:'Compliance', forbiddenActors: (ctx) => [ctx.get<Submission>('submission').submittedBy], timeout: '30d' })`.
- `new ComputeNode('rejected', { idempotent:true, async execute(ctx){ await ctx.audit('workflow.review.rejected', { actor:'system' }); } })`.
Add code-path edges: `.edge('push-to-forgejo','questionnaire', { when: (ctx) => ctx.get('classification') === 'code-containing' })`, `.edge('questionnaire','scan')`, `.edge('scan','confirmation', { when: (ctx) => ctx.get('scanReport').verdict !== 'block' })`, `.edge('confirmation','review')`, `.edge('review','publish')`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.test.ts: the blueprint's HITL nodes expose the spec timeouts — questionnaire.timeout === '7d', confirmation.timeout === '14d', review.timeout === '30d'; review.requiredRole === 'Compliance'; calling review.forbiddenActors on a ctx stub whose get('submission') === { submittedBy:'u1' } returns ['u1'] and confirmation.allowedActors returns ['u1']; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
ScanReport.verdict values (pass/review/block) are owned by asr-btm — import the type, do not redefine. runScanner is asr-st5 (not yet decomposed); import it but mock it in the integration test (task 04). Do not wire the md-only auto-approve branch here (task 03). Keep separation-of-duties also enforced at the endpoint (asr-kkd) — this node-level forbiddenActors is one of the two layers (spec lines 224-226), not the only one.

title: Add approvalPipeline Blueprint skeleton with classify/push-to-forgejo/publish compute nodes
description:
Parent story: asr-82o
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#blueprint-sketch (lines 64-95, 135-157, 161-171), #pipeline (lines 13-62), #state-persistence (lines 176-178)

Context:
The Flowcraft approvalPipeline is the single declarative DAG that every submission flows through; both the md-only and code-containing paths share it. This task lays down the Blueprint and its three idempotent ComputeNodes (classify, push-to-forgejo, publish) plus the classify->push-to-forgejo edge, so the auto-approve branch (task 03) and HITL nodes (task 02) can attach to a working spine. ctx.svc resolves ForgejoClient from the DI container and ctx.audit is the closed-enum audit helper.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.test.ts (new)

Change:
Create `export const approvalPipeline = new Blueprint<Submission>('skill-approval')` (import Blueprint, ComputeNode from 'flowcraft'; import type { Submission } from '@asr/core'; import { ForgejoClient } from '@asr/core'; import { classifySkill } from '../zip/classify.js' (asr-crh.2)). Add three idempotent ComputeNodes:
- `classify` { idempotent:true } -> `const cls = classifySkill(ctx.get<string[]>('files')); ctx.set('classification', cls)` (cls is 'md-only'|'code-containing').
- `push-to-forgejo` { idempotent:true } -> `const forgejo = ctx.svc(ForgejoClient); const { branch, prNumber } = await forgejo.openSubmissionPR({ submissionId: ctx.get('submissionId'), manifest: ctx.get('manifest'), files: ctx.get('files'), autoApprove: ctx.get('classification') === 'md-only' }); ctx.set('branchName', branch); ctx.set('prNumber', prNumber)` (asr-she.3).
- `publish` { idempotent:true } -> `const forgejo = ctx.svc(ForgejoClient); const merge = await forgejo.mergePR(ctx.get('prNumber')); await forgejo.publishArtifact({ owner: ctx.get('manifest').author, name: ctx.get('manifest').name, version: ctx.get('manifest').version, zipBuffer: ctx.get('zipBuffer') }); await forgejo.deleteBranch(ctx.get('branchName')); ctx.set('mergeCommit', merge.sha); await ctx.audit('workflow.review.approved', { actor: 'system' })` (asr-she.4/she.5, audit action from asr-pf0 AUDIT_ACTIONS). Add `.edge('classify', 'push-to-forgejo')`. Leave HITL nodes, auto-approve node, scan node, and conditional edges to tasks 02/03 (export the partially-built Blueprint object so later tasks chain `.node(...)`/`.edge(...)` onto it in the same module).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.test.ts: importing approvalPipeline yields a Blueprint whose id === 'skill-approval' and whose registered node names include 'classify', 'push-to-forgejo', and 'publish'; each of those three nodes reports idempotent === true; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT redefine Submission/ScanReport/SkillClassification — import from @asr/core (asr-5k8/asr-btm/asr-crh.2 own them). The publish node here is the SAME node reused by both branches; the md-only auto-approve node and edges belong to task 03, the HITL/scan nodes to task 02. registry.json regeneration is the distribution epic, not here. Do not add a direct-write-to-main path — publish merges a PR (specs/workflow.md line 15).

title: Implement POST /submissions/:id/approve with separation-of-duties check to publish
description:
Parent story: asr-kkd
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#post-submissionsidapprove (lines 101-118); specs/api.md#role-matrix (lines 149-164); specs/workflow.md#separation-of-duties (lines 224-226)

Context:
The `review` HITL node requires a `Compliance` actor and forbids the submitter (workflow.md lines 122-132). Per spec the separation-of-duties check must ALSO run at the handler (workflow.md lines 224-226), not just the HITL node, so a stale-role decision is caught at submit time. Approving delivers the review signal, resuming the pipeline through `publish` to the published phase.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/approve.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/approve.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function registerApproveRoute(app: Hono): void` mounting `POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/approve`. Body: `{ comment?: string }`. Load the submission via the read repository (asr-igs.2); 404 `apiError(c,404,'submission_not_found')`. Compare caller `sub` to `submission.submittedBy`; if equal return `apiError(c,403,'separation_of_duties_violation')` (spec line 116). Otherwise deliver the `review`-approve HITL signal (story asr-82o) → `{ phase: 'published'; publishedAt; mergeCommit }`, emit `workflow.review.approved` via the audit helper (asr-pf0 `audit.emit`), and return 200 `{ status: { phase: 'published', publishedAt, mergeCommit }, publishedVersion, registryUrl }` per spec lines 107-112. Import `apiError` from `../errors.js`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/http/routes/approve.test.ts: POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/approve by a Compliance caller whose `sub` differs from `submission.submittedBy` returns 200 with `status.phase === 'published'`; POST by a caller whose `sub` equals `submittedBy` returns 403 and `(await res.json()).error === 'separation_of_duties_violation'`; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The `Compliance` role gate (403 `insufficient_permissions`, required `Compliance`, role matrix line 157) is enforced by the auth/role middleware in story asr-k4m — this handler enforces only the submitter≠approver SoD check (defence in depth, workflow.md line 226). Do NOT implement the Flowcraft engine or the publish/merge step (asr-82o); stub the signal in test. Do not redefine SubmissionStatus.

title: Trigger md-only publish from POST and add end-to-end test
description:
Parent story: asr-6hl
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#pipeline (classify -> md-only auto-approve, lines 14-62); specs/api.md#post-submissions (lines 62-80)

Context:
This closes the Phase-1 loop: after a successful POST that classifies md-only, the service runs publishMdOnly so the submission ends at published without human action. It also adds the end-to-end test that is the story's acceptance signal.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.ts (extend POST: invoke publishMdOnly for md-only)
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.publish.test.ts (new — e2e against a mocked ForgejoClient)

Change:
In the POST handler (asr-igs.3), after persisting an md-only submission, build the validated file list and call `publishMdOnly({ db, forgejo }, { submission, files, lockVersion: 0 })`, awaiting it in Phase 1 for deterministic GET results; surface failures as apiError(c,500,'internal_error'). For code-containing submissions leave status uploaded (full pipeline is asr-82o). Inject the ForgejoClient (default forgejoFromEnv()) so tests pass a mock.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/http/submissions.publish.test.ts: POST /api/v1/submissions with an md-only zip, using an injected mocked ForgejoClient, then GET /api/v1/submissions/:id returns status.phase==='published' with a mergeCommit set; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Phase 1 runs publish inline (awaited) for determinism; making it async/queued with crash-resume is asr-fc6. Do not run publishMdOnly for code-containing submissions. The live-Forgejo variant of this flow is exercised by the docker e2e (asr-mcx), not here.

title: Implement md-only auto-approve publish orchestrator
description:
Parent story: asr-6hl
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#pipeline (md-only/auto-approve path, lines 14-62, 135-171), #crash-recovery-resume (idempotency, lines 180-187); specs/git-integration.md#branch-strategy (lines 32-47)

Context:
The Phase-1 end-to-end signal: an md-only submission is published purely through compute steps (no HITL). This orchestrator runs the auto-approve path — open a PR (auto-approve label), merge it with the merge token, upload the package artifact, delete the branch, and flip the submission to published. It is the compute-only specialisation of the Flowcraft blueprint (the full code-path blueprint with HITL nodes is asr-82o).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/publishMdOnly.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/publishMdOnly.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export async function publishMdOnly(deps: { db: Database; forgejo: ForgejoClient }, input: { submission: Submission; files: Array<{ path: string; content: Buffer }>; lockVersion: number }): Promise<{ mergeCommit: string; packageUrl: string }>`: forgejo.openSubmissionPR({ submissionId, manifest, files, autoApprove:true }) (asr-she.3) -> { branch, prNumber }; mergePR(prNumber) (asr-she.4) -> { sha }; publishArtifact({ owner: manifest.author, name: manifest.name, version: manifest.version, zipBuffer: await packSkillZip(files) }) (asr-she.5 + 6hl-2) -> url; deleteBranch(branch) (asr-she.4); updateSubmissionStatus(db, id, lockVersion, { statusPhase:'published', statusJson: JSON.stringify({ phase:'published', publishedAt: new Date().toISOString(), mergeCommit: sha }) }) (asr-7jd.4). Every external call is idempotent (ForgejoClient tolerates 409/405/404). Return { mergeCommit: sha, packageUrl: url }.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/publishMdOnly.test.ts: with a mocked ForgejoClient (openSubmissionPR->{branch:'submit/x',prNumber:1}, mergePR->{sha:'abc'}, publishArtifact->url, deleteBranch->ok) and a temp sqlite holding a seeded md-only submission at lock_version 0, publishMdOnly resolves to { mergeCommit:'abc', packageUrl } AND getSubmissionById shows status_phase==='published'; assert mergePR and publishArtifact were each called once; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Auto-approve still goes through a real PR + merge — never write directly to main (specs/git-integration.md line 47). registry.json regeneration is asr-3d8/asr-p29 (distribution epic), not here. Code-containing path (questionnaire/scan/review) is asr-82o. Keep every external call idempotent so crash-resume (asr-fc6) can re-run safely.

title: Add canonical skill zip packer
description:
Parent story: asr-6hl
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (publishArtifact takes zipBuffer, lines 168-180); specs/workflow.md#blueprint-sketch (publish node, lines 142-157)

Context:
publishArtifact uploads a zip buffer to Forgejo's generic package registry. After a submission is extracted to disk and validated, the publish step repackages the skill files into a deterministic zip to upload as the artifact. Canonical, sorted packing keeps the artifact reproducible.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/zip/pack.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/zip/pack.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export async function packSkillZip(files: Array<{ path: string; content: Buffer }>): Promise<Buffer>` using yazl: sort entries by path ascending, addBuffer each with a fixed mtime (epoch 0) for determinism, end(), and collect the output stream into one Buffer.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/zip/pack.test.ts: packSkillZip([{path:'SKILL.md',content},{path:'manifest.yaml',content}]) returns a Buffer that yauzl re-reads to exactly those two entries in sorted order; packing the same input twice yields byte-identical Buffers; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Determinism matters (fixed mtime, sorted order) so the artifact and any future content-hash agree. Packs already-validated files only — extraction/validation is asr-crh. No Forgejo upload here (that is publishArtifact).

title: Add forgejoFromEnv factory and Forgejo env vars
description:
Parent story: asr-6hl
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#environment (FORGEJO_* vars, lines 161-166), #module-layout (src/forgejo/ line 40); specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 64-88)

Context:
The publish workflow needs a ForgejoClient built from service config. asr-she.1 defined ForgejoClient/ForgejoConfig in @asr/core; asr-6ax.2 created the zod env module but only with PORT/NODE_ENV/AUTH_MODE/FORGEJO_URL/DATABASE_PATH. This adds the remaining Forgejo env vars (optional, to keep asr-6ax.2 tests intact) and one factory so every caller constructs the client identically.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/env.ts (extend the zod schema)
- packages/submission/src/forgejo/index.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/forgejo/index.test.ts (new)

Change:
Extend the env zod schema with FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN, FORGEJO_MERGE_TOKEN, FORGEJO_OWNER, FORGEJO_REPO as z.string().optional() (keep them optional so the existing asr-6ax.2 dev-mode parse test still passes). Add `export function forgejoFromEnv(e = env): ForgejoClient`: assert the four FORGEJO_* + FORGEJO_URL are present (throw `Error('FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN is required')` etc. when missing), normalise baseUrl to end with /api/v1, and return `new ForgejoClient({ baseUrl, uploadToken, mergeToken, owner, repo, defaultBranch:'main' })`. Re-export ForgejoClient from @asr/core for service consumers.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/forgejo/index.test.ts: forgejoFromEnv with a stub env (FORGEJO_URL='http://forgejo:3000/api/v1', upload/merge tokens, owner='asr', repo='skills-registry') returns a ForgejoClient (instanceof) without throwing; calling it with FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN undefined throws /FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN/; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Never log token values. baseUrl must point at Forgejo's /api/v1; never reference github.com. Keep the new env vars optional so asr-6ax.2's parseEnv dev-mode test is unaffected. Branch-protection bootstrap (specs/git-integration.md lines 195-219) is a separate script, out of scope.

title: Implement GET /submissions/:id handler
description:
Parent story: asr-igs
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#endpoints (GET /submissions/:id, line 14), #role-matrix (lines 160-161)

Context:
Submitters and the web UI poll a submission's current workflow state by id (e.g. to watch an md-only submission reach published). This adds the read endpoint to the router POST created.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.ts (extend the submissionRoutes router)
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add GET `/:id` to submissionRoutes: getSubmissionById (igs-2); undefined -> apiError(c,404,'submission_not_found'); else rowToSubmission(row) and c.json(submission) (200) — id, manifest, classification, contentHash, status (full SubmissionStatus union), branchName?, prNumber?.

Acceptance:
vitest integration: after a POST /api/v1/submissions returns an id, GET /api/v1/submissions/:id returns 200 with that id and status.phase==='uploaded'; GET /api/v1/submissions/does-not-exist returns 404 { error:'submission_not_found' }; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Role-scoped visibility (own vs Compliance-all) is asr-k4m's middleware — Phase 1 returns the row under the mock principal. No list endpoint (GET /submissions) here (filter/paginate is a follow-up).

title: Implement POST /submissions handler
description:
Parent story: asr-igs
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#post-submissions (lines 62-80), #error-responses (lines 183-209); specs/workflow.md#pipeline (classify, lines 14-31)

Context:
The write-side entrypoint of the whole platform: accept a multipart skill.zip, safely extract it, classify md-only vs code-containing, parse SKILL.md into a canonical SkillManifest, compute the content hash, persist a submissions row, and return 201 with the submission id. Phase 1 needs the md-only path to continue (publish trigger is asr-6hl).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.ts (new — exports `submissionRoutes` Hono sub-app)
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (mount: app.route('/api/v1/submissions', submissionRoutes))
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.test.ts (new)

Change:
Build `submissionRoutes = new Hono()`. POST `/`: read multipart `file` via c.req.formData(), write to a tmp path, extractSafe (asr-crh.1) into a tmpdir; extractor throw -> apiError(c,400,'invalid_zip',{message}). classifySkill (asr-crh.2) the returned paths. Read SKILL.md and parseSkillManifest (asr-4bc.2); throw -> apiError(c,422,'invalid_manifest',{message}). Compute contentHash = sha256 over the sorted "relpath\0bytes" concatenation (canonical per specs/versioning.md; centralised later by asr-2fb). Build a Submission (id=ulid(), status {phase:'uploaded'}, submittedBy=env.MOCK_USER_SUB), insertSubmission, return c.json({ id, status, manifest, contentHash, createdAt }, 201). Mount the router in index.ts.

Acceptance:
vitest integration (app.request with a multipart FormData + a Blob zip fixture built via yazl): POST /api/v1/submissions with an md-only zip (SKILL.md + manifest) returns 201 with a ULID `id`, `status.phase==='uploaded'`, and `manifest.name`; POSTing a garbage (non-zip) blob returns 400 { error:'invalid_zip' }; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Per-skill mutex (pending_versions) + 409 version_in_progress are owned by asr-fc6 — do not add that table here. Full Entra/mock auth middleware is asr-k4m; Phase 1 reads the submitter sub from env.MOCK_USER_SUB. Do not trigger publish here (that wiring is asr-6hl). contentHash is inline now, replaced by the @asr/core primitive from asr-2fb later.

title: Add submission read + row-to-aggregate mapping to repository
description:
Parent story: asr-igs
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#submission (lines 50-81); code: packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.ts (insertSubmission/updateSubmissionStatus from asr-7jd.4)

Context:
GET /submissions/:id and the publish workflow must read a stored submission back as the canonical Submission aggregate, with its SubmissionStatus union rehydrated from status_json. asr-7jd.4 added the write helpers to this repo; this adds the read + row<->aggregate mapping so the HTTP layer never touches SQL.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.ts (extend; keep insertSubmission/updateSubmissionStatus)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `export interface SubmissionRow { id; manifest_json; classification; content_hash; submitted_at; submitted_by; branch_name; pr_number; status_phase; status_json; lock_version }`. Add `export function getSubmissionById(db: Database, id: string): SubmissionRow | undefined` (SELECT * FROM submissions WHERE id = ?). Add `export function rowToSubmission(row: SubmissionRow): Submission` (type from @asr/core) parsing manifest_json -> SkillManifest and status_json -> SubmissionStatus, mapping branch_name/pr_number -> branchName/prNumber. Extend insertSubmission so a full Submission persists (manifest_json, content_hash, status_phase, status_json all populated).

Acceptance:
vitest: after runMigrations + insertSubmission of a full md-only Submission, getSubmissionById(db,id) returns a row whose status_json round-trips through rowToSubmission to a Submission with status.phase==='uploaded' and manifest.name equal to the seeded value; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not change the 0001 schema (asr-7jd.3 owns it). status_json is the serialised SubmissionStatus union — keep phase tags byte-identical to @asr/core. No HTTP here.

title: Add unified API error envelope helper
description:
Parent story: asr-igs
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#error-responses (lines 183-209)

Context:
Every submission and registry endpoint must return errors in one closed-enum envelope so clients (web, CLI, MCP) branch reliably. Centralising this in one helper stops each route hand-rolling JSON+status pairs and keeps the ApiError enum single-sourced.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/errors.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/http/errors.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export type ApiError = 'authentication_required'|'insufficient_permissions'|'separation_of_duties_violation'|'submission_not_found'|'submission_in_progress'|'version_already_exists'|'version_in_progress'|'version_yanked'|'version_downgrade'|'invalid_zip'|'invalid_manifest'|'content_blocked'|'too_many_requests'|'audit_chain_broken'|'internal_error'` (verbatim from specs/api.md lines 188-203). Add `export function apiError(c: Context, status: ContentfulStatusCode, error: ApiError, extra?: { message?: string; details?: Record<string,string>; required?: string; retryAfterSeconds?: number }): Response` returning `c.json({ error, ...extra }, status)`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/http/errors.test.ts: apiError on a Hono test Context with ('invalid_zip', 400) yields res.status===400 and `await res.json()` deep-equals { error: 'invalid_zip' }; passing extra {message,required} includes them in the body; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Keep the ApiError union byte-identical to specs/api.md (clients depend on the closed set). No HTTP routes here — shared helper only. Per-endpoint status-code choice is each handler's concern.

title: Implement publishArtifact generic-package upload
description:
Parent story: asr-she
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 168-180)

Context:
After publish, the packaged skill.zip is uploaded to Forgejo's generic package registry so the CLI/MCP download path can fetch it by owner/name/version. This uses the raw Forgejo packages HTTP API (not Octokit) with the upload token (write:package).

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/forgejo.ts (add publishArtifact)
- packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `async publishArtifact(input: { owner: string; name: string; version: string; zipBuffer: Buffer }): Promise<string>`: derive `base` by stripping a trailing /api/v1 from cfg.baseUrl (`cfg.baseUrl.replace(/\/api\/v1\/?$/, '')`); build url `${base}/api/packages/${input.owner}/generic/${input.name}/${input.version}/skill.zip`; fetch PUT with headers Authorization `token ${cfg.uploadToken}`, Content-Type `application/zip`, body zipBuffer; throw `Error('publishArtifact ' + res.status)` on !res.ok unless res.status===409 (already uploaded); return the url.

Acceptance:
vitest with mocked fetch (vi.stubGlobal('fetch', ...)): publishArtifact issues a PUT to `<base>/api/packages/<owner>/generic/<name>/<version>/skill.zip` with an Authorization header starting 'token '; a 409 response does NOT throw and the url is returned; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Uses the UPLOAD token (write:package), not the merge token. The /api/v1 suffix must be stripped to reach /api/packages — do not double-prefix. download-URL resolution on the read side is a CLI/registry concern, out of scope.

title: Implement mergePR and deleteBranch with the merge token
description:
Parent story: asr-she
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 138-166); specs/security.md#token-scoping-forgejo (lines 183-191)

Context:
Publishing an approved skill merges its PR to main; only the merge token (merge-whitelist member) may do this — the upload token cannot (separation of duties). After merge the submit branch is deleted. Forgejo's merge takes Do + merge_message_field and returns merge_commit_sha.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/forgejo.ts (add mergePR + deleteBranch)
- packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `async mergePR(prNumber: number): Promise<{ sha: string }>` using this.merge: POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{index}/merge with Do:'squash', merge_message_field:`Approved and published (#${prNumber})`, delete_branch_after_merge:true; tolerate 405 (already merged); then GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{index} and return { sha: merge_commit_sha } (throw if absent). Add `async deleteBranch(branch: string): Promise<void>` using this.merge: DELETE /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/{branch}; tolerate 404.

Acceptance:
vitest with mocked Forgejo: mergePR issues the merge POST via the MERGE-token client (assert the request used the merge token / merge Octokit), returns the mocked merge_commit_sha, and a second call where merge returns 405 is tolerated (no throw); `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
mergePR/deleteBranch MUST use this.merge (merge token), never this.upload — using the upload token here defeats separation of duties. Branch-protection setup (branch_protections POST) is a separate bootstrap concern, out of scope.

title: Implement openSubmissionPR flow in ForgejoClient
description:
Parent story: asr-she
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 89-136), #branch-strategy (lines 33-47)

Context:
This is the write-path entrypoint the workflow calls for every submission (including MD-only auto-approve): create the submit branch, commit the skill files under skills/<author>/<name>/, open a PR labelled auto-approve or needs-review, and wait for Forgejo to compute mergeable. There is no direct push to main.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/forgejo.ts (add openSubmissionPR + private waitMergeable + prBody helper)
- packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `async openSubmissionPR(input: { submissionId: string; manifest: SkillManifest; files: Array<{ path: string; content: Buffer }>; autoApprove: boolean }): Promise<{ branch: string; prNumber: number; headSha: string }>`: branch = `submit/${submissionId}`; headSha = await createOrGetBranch(branch); for each file (in order) await putFile(branch, `skills/${manifest.author}/${manifest.name}/${file.path}`, file.content, submissionId); POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls with title `[Skill] ${manifest.name}@${manifest.version}`, head branch, base cfg.defaultBranch ?? 'main', body prBody(...), labels autoApprove ? ['auto-approve'] : ['needs-review']; await private waitMergeable(prNumber) polling GET /pulls/{index} up to 5s for a defined mergeable. Add prBody(manifest, submissionId, autoApprove).

Acceptance:
vitest with mocked Forgejo asserts the call ORDER for a 2-file submission: branch create, then file PUT #1, then file PUT #2, then PR create, then mergeable poll; the returned branch === 'submit/<id>' and prNumber matches the mocked PR; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
File commits MUST be sequential and in input order (assert order in the test). Do not merge here — merge is the merge-token task (asr-she follow-up). Uses SkillManifest from @asr/core (asr-5up).

title: Add putFile and idempotent branch creation to ForgejoClient
description:
Parent story: asr-she
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 96-119, 193); #known-forgejo--github-differences (per-file PUT /contents, line 60)

Context:
Forgejo has no git tree API, so multi-file commits are sequential per-file PUTs, and branch creation must be idempotent (a retried submission re-uses an existing submit/<id> branch). These are the primitives openSubmissionPR composes.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/forgejo.ts (add private methods to ForgejoClient)
- packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add private `createOrGetBranch(branch: string): Promise<string>`: POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches with new_branch_name + old_branch_name (cfg.defaultBranch ?? 'main') via this.upload; on a 409 catch, GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/{branch} and return its commit.id; otherwise return the created commit.id (head sha). Add private `putFile(branch, path, content: Buffer, submissionId): Promise<string>`: PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path} with a deterministic message `submit: <path> (<submissionId>)`, content base64-encoded, branch; return the new commit sha; tolerate 409 by fetching the current sha.

Acceptance:
vitest with a mocked Forgejo (nock or msw intercepting cfg.baseUrl): when the branch POST returns 409, createOrGetBranch issues the GET and returns its head sha; putFile issues a PUT to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/<path> on the given branch; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Keep the commit message format deterministic so retries are recognisable in Git history (specs/git-integration.md line 193). Sequential only — no parallel PUTs (Forgejo serialises commits on a branch). openSubmissionPR assembly is the next task.

title: Add ForgejoClient skeleton with dual Octokit clients
description:
Parent story: asr-she
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 64-88), #token-security (lines 222-229)

Context:
All git operations target Forgejo's /api/v1 via Octokit with a baseUrl override. The client holds two tokens — an upload token (branches/commits/PRs/package upload) and a merge token (merge/branch deletion) — so the upload identity can never merge to main (specs/security.md separation of duties). This skeleton establishes the dual-client construction; later tasks add methods.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/forgejo.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (replace the placeholder comment on line 3 with `export * from './forgejo.js';`)
- packages/core/package.json (add "@octokit/rest":"^21.0.0" to dependencies)

Change:
Add `export interface ForgejoConfig { baseUrl: string; uploadToken: string; mergeToken: string; owner: string; repo: string; defaultBranch?: string }` and `export class ForgejoClient` whose constructor builds `this.upload = new Octokit({ baseUrl: cfg.baseUrl, auth: cfg.uploadToken })` and `this.merge = new Octokit({ baseUrl: cfg.baseUrl, auth: cfg.mergeToken })` (both private readonly), storing cfg. Expose no HTTP methods yet.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts: constructing ForgejoClient with distinct upload/merge tokens yields two different Octokit instances, both configured with cfg.baseUrl (assert the constructed instances differ and carry the baseUrl); `rg -i 'github' packages/core/src/forgejo.ts` returns no host references; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
NEVER reference github.com or api.github.com — baseUrl is always the Forgejo /api/v1 host. No HTTP methods in this task. Do not log token values.
close_reason: Implemented: added ForgejoClient skeleton with separate upload and merge Octokit clients plus core tests

title: Add whitelist skill classifier (md-only vs code-containing)
description:
Parent story: asr-crh
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#classification-whitelist-approach (lines 84-104)

Context:
The Phase-1 auto-approve route depends on correctly labelling an upload md-only (docs/images/config only) vs code-containing (anything else). specs/security.md mandates a whitelist (not blacklist): unknown or extensionless files force the safer code path, which then requires scan + review.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/zip/classify.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/zip/classify.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `const CONTENT_ONLY_EXTENSIONS = new Set(['.md','.txt','.rst','.png','.jpg','.jpeg','.gif','.svg','.webp','.yaml','.yml','.json'])` and `export function classifySkill(files: string[]): SkillClassification` returning 'md-only' iff every file's lowercased extension is in the set, else 'code-containing'. A file with no '.' (no extension) is treated as not-content-only (code path). Import SkillClassification from @asr/core (added by asr-5k8).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/zip/classify.test.ts: classifySkill(['SKILL.md','img/logo.png','manifest.yaml']) === 'md-only'; classifySkill(['SKILL.md','hack.py']) === 'code-containing'; classifySkill(['SKILL.md','Makefile']) === 'code-containing' (extensionless); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Whitelist only — do NOT convert this to a blacklist of dangerous extensions (security regression per specs/security.md line 86). SVG <script> stripping and markdown XSS handling are separate downstream concerns. Keep the extension set byte-identical to specs/security.md lines 89-93.

title: Add yauzl-based safe zip extractor
description:
Parent story: asr-crh
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#zip-upload-hardening (#must-have-controls lines 13-26, #implementation-sketch lines 28-82)

Context:
Submissions are untrusted user archives; the extractor is the first line of defence against zip-slip, zip bombs, symlink and device-file attacks. specs/security.md mandates yauzl with explicit per-entry control and forbids unzipper. This is the MUST-HAVE control set from the threat model and drives Phase 1 auto-approve routing.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/zip/extract.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/zip/extract.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/package.json (add "yauzl":"^3.0.0" to deps; "@types/yauzl":"^2.10.0" and "yazl":"^2.5.1" to devDeps for test fixtures)

Change:
Implement `export async function extractSafe(zipPath: string, targetDir: string, limits = LIMITS): Promise<string[]>` per specs/security.md#implementation-sketch. LIMITS = { maxCompressedBytes 50*1024*1024, maxUncompressedBytes 200*1024*1024, maxFiles 500, maxDepth 5, maxPathLen 200 }. For each entry: NFC-normalize name; reject name.length > maxPathLen ('path too long'); reject /[\x00-\x1f‮‏]/ ('illegal chars'); reject path-traversal when resolve(targetDir,name) is not under resolve(targetDir)+sep ('path traversal'); reject relative-depth > maxDepth ('max depth'); reject symlinks via (externalFileAttributes >>> 16) & 0xa000 ('symlink rejected'); reject when ++fileCount > maxFiles ('max files'); accumulate uncompressedSize and reject > maxUncompressedBytes ('uncompressed size limit'). Accept the optional `limits` param so tests can trip caps cheaply. Return the list of extracted relative paths.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/zip/extract.test.ts: a zip built with yazl containing an entry named '../evil.txt' rejects with /path traversal/; a zip with 2 entries extracted with limits overriding maxFiles:1 rejects with /max files/; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
NEVER import unzipper (specs/security.md line 26 forbids it). Polyglot magic-byte sniffing is a follow-up refinement — this task delivers path/size/symlink/count controls only. Extract to a caller-provided targetDir (use os.tmpdir in tests) and clean it up in afterEach.

title: Add optimistic-lock guarded submission update helper
description:
Parent story: asr-7jd
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#concurrency-model (per-skill optimistic locking lines 144-148); specs/security.md#race-condition-prevention (lines 197-199)

Context:
Concurrent workflow steps / replicas must not clobber each other's submission writes. The lock_version column added in 0001 enables compare-and-swap updates so a stale writer is rejected rather than overwriting newer state — the primitive behind the per-skill mutex and 409 version_in_progress.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function insertSubmission(db, row)` (minimal seed helper inserting a row at lock_version 0) and `export function updateSubmissionStatus(db, id: string, expectedLockVersion: number, next: { statusPhase: string; statusJson: string }): boolean`. updateSubmissionStatus runs `UPDATE submissions SET status_phase=?, status_json=?, lock_version = lock_version + 1 WHERE id=? AND lock_version=?` and returns `info.changes === 1`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.test.ts: after runMigrations + insertSubmission at lock_version 0, updateSubmissionStatus(db,id,0,next) returns true and a second updateSubmissionStatus(db,id,0,next2) returns false (stale write rejected, no rows changed); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
This is the optimistic-lock primitive only — full submission CRUD and the SubmissionStatus serialisation strategy come with the API story (asr-igs). Do not throw on stale write here; return false so callers can map it to 409 version_in_progress.

title: Add 0001 submissions-table migration
description:
Parent story: asr-7jd
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#submission (lines 50-81); specs/audit.md#schema (FK target submissions(id) line 27); specs/submission-package.md#concurrency-model (lines 144-148)

Context:
submissions is the core write-side table the workflow drives and that audit_events FKs to. It must persist the Submission aggregate (specs/types.md) plus an optimistic-lock column for the per-skill mutex (concurrency model). audit_events itself is out of scope — owned by the Audit epic asr-62i.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0001_submissions.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (import and push the 0001 entry into `migrations`)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0001_submissions.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export a Migration `{ id: 1, name: 'submissions', up }` whose up runs CREATE TABLE submissions(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, manifest_json TEXT NOT NULL, classification TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(classification IN ('md-only','code-containing')), content_hash TEXT NOT NULL, submitted_at TEXT NOT NULL, submitted_by TEXT NOT NULL, branch_name TEXT, pr_number INTEGER, status_phase TEXT NOT NULL, status_json TEXT NOT NULL, lock_version INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0) plus indexes idx_submissions_content_hash(content_hash), idx_submissions_submitted_by(submitted_by), idx_submissions_status_phase(status_phase). In migrations/index.ts import the entry and push it (keep ascending id order).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0001_submissions.test.ts: after runMigrations(db), `db.pragma('table_info(submissions)')` includes columns id, manifest_json, classification, content_hash, status_phase, status_json and lock_version; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT create audit_events here (specs/audit.md#schema is owned by epic asr-62i). status_phase mirrors the SubmissionStatus.phase tag and the classification CHECK values mirror SkillClassification (specs/types.md) — keep both byte-identical. No repository/query logic in this task.

title: Add forward-only migration runner to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-7jd
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (src/db/migrations/ line 51 — "sequential .sql files; runner pinned to up-only")

Context:
Schema changes must be applied deterministically and exactly once, in order, with no down-migrations (the spec pins the runner up-only). This is the engine; individual migrations (submissions table, etc.) register against it.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.test.ts (new)

Change:
Define `export interface Migration { id: number; name: string; up(db: Database.Database): void }` and `export const migrations: Migration[] = []` (ordered by id; populated by later tasks). Add `export function runMigrations(db: Database.Database): void` that: creates `schema_migrations(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, applied_at TEXT NOT NULL)` if absent; reads the set of applied ids; for each unapplied migration in ascending id order runs `db.transaction(() => { m.up(db); db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_migrations (id,name,applied_at) VALUES (?,?,?)').run(m.id, m.name, new Date().toISOString()); })()`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.test.ts: push a one-off test migration into `migrations`, run runMigrations(db) twice on the same temp db, then assert `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM schema_migrations WHERE id = <testid>` === 1 (second run is a no-op); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
No down/rollback path — up-only by design. Do not auto-discover .sql from disk in this task; the in-code `migrations` array is the registry. Wrap each migration in a single transaction so a failure leaves schema_migrations consistent.

title: Add better-sqlite3 WAL connection module to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-7jd
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (src/db/index.ts line 50); specs/audit.md (SQLite via better-sqlite3, WAL)

Context:
All workflow and audit state lives in a single SQLite database opened in WAL mode (concurrency model, specs/submission-package.md lines 144-148). Every db consumer needs one canonical opener that sets pragmas consistently (WAL + foreign_keys) so connection setup is not duplicated per module.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/index.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/index.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/package.json (add "better-sqlite3":"^11.0.0" to deps, "@types/better-sqlite3":"^7.6.0" to devDeps)

Change:
Add `import Database from 'better-sqlite3';` and `export function openDb(path: string): Database.Database` doing `const db = new Database(path); db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON'); return db;`. Re-export the better-sqlite3 `Database` type (`export type { Database } from 'better-sqlite3';` or a type alias) for downstream modules to consume.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/index.test.ts: openDb on a temp-file path (node:os tmpdir + a random name) returns a db where `db.pragma('journal_mode', {simple:true})` === 'wal' and `db.pragma('foreign_keys', {simple:true})` === 1; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Use a temp FILE path, not ':memory:' (memory dbs report journal_mode 'memory', not 'wal'). No schema creation here — migrations own that. Close the db in the test's afterEach to avoid leaking WAL/-shm files.

title: Add /healthz route and Hono bootstrap to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-6ax
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#composition (lines 111-142), #module-layout (src/http/health.ts line 22)

Context:
Container Apps and the docker dev stack need a liveness endpoint, and the service needs an actual bootstrap that listens on the configured port. This replaces the stub index from the scaffold task with a real (minimal) Hono app and proves the prod-mode guard fires on boot (importing env first).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/health.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (replace stub)
- packages/submission/src/index.test.ts (new)

Change:
health.ts: export a Hono sub-app `healthRoutes` with GET / returning c.json({status:'ok'}) (200). index.ts: import { env } from './env.js' FIRST (so the prod+mock guard throws before listen), create `const app = new Hono()`, `app.route('/healthz', healthRoutes)`, and start the server via @hono/node-server `serve({ fetch: app.fetch, port: env.PORT })` (add "@hono/node-server":"^1.13.0" to deps). Export `app` for tests; guard the serve() call so importing the module in a test does not bind a port (e.g. only serve when run as the entrypoint).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/index.test.ts: `const res = await app.request('/healthz')` returns res.status===200 and `await res.json()` deep-equals {status:'ok'}. Manual: `AUTH_MODE=mock NODE_ENV=development pnpm --filter @asr/submission dev` then `curl -s localhost:3001/healthz` prints {"status":"ok"}.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add auth middleware or other routes yet (full composition lands incrementally). Importing env at the top is load-bearing: it makes a prod+mock boot exit non-zero (env throws) — do not move it below serve().
close_reason: Implemented: added Hono app bootstrap with /healthz route, guarded serve startup, dependency wiring, and regression test

title: Add zod-validated env module to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-6ax
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#environment (lines 150-176), #production-mode-enforcement (lines 178-187)

Context:
The service must fail fast on misconfiguration and must never boot in production with mock auth.
