Centralising env parsing in one zod-validated module gives every other module a typed `env` and is where the in-process production-mode guard lives. specs/submission-package.md line 176: src/env.ts "validates these with zod and refuses to boot if anything required is missing — fail-fast, never silently default in prod."

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/env.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/env.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function parseEnv(raw: NodeJS.ProcessEnv)` that parses raw through a zod schema covering at minimum PORT (z.coerce.number().default(3001)), NODE_ENV (z.enum(['development','production'])), AUTH_MODE (z.enum(['mock','entra'])), plus optional FORGEJO_URL and DATABASE_PATH passthrough. Add a superRefine that fails when NODE_ENV==='production' && AUTH_MODE==='mock' with message 'FATAL: AUTH_MODE=mock is forbidden in production'. Export a module-level `export const env = parseEnv(process.env)` that throws (uncaught → non-zero exit) on invalid config.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/env.test.ts: parseEnv({NODE_ENV:'production', AUTH_MODE:'mock'}) throws /forbidden in production/; parseEnv({NODE_ENV:'development', AUTH_MODE:'mock'}) returns env.PORT===3001 and env.AUTH_MODE==='mock'; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not pull secrets from Key Vault here (prod loader is a Phase 4 concern) — only parse the passed env object. Keep the prod+mock refinement: it is the in-process half of the production boot guard; the Dockerfile entrypoint half is asr-nbj (out of scope).
close_reason: Implemented: added zod-validated submission env parsing with production mock-auth guard and regression tests

title: Scaffold @asr/submission package manifest and tsconfig
description:
Parent story: asr-6ax
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#module-layout, #packagejson (lines 67-107)

Context:
Phase 1 needs a deployable Hono service that will host the submission API, registry read API, MCP server, workflow runner and audit chain. Nothing exists under packages/submission yet. This task creates the workspace package so later tasks (env, health, db, zip) have a home and `pnpm` resolves @asr/submission.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/package.json (new)
- packages/submission/tsconfig.json (new)
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (new, minimal stub)

Change:
Create packages/submission/package.json named "@asr/submission", "type":"module", main ./dist/index.js, scripts { dev: "tsx watch src/index.ts", build: "tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts", test: "vitest run" }, dependencies { "@asr/core":"workspace:*", "hono":"^4.6.0", "zod":"^3.23.0" }, devDependencies { "@types/node":"^22.0.0", "tsup":"^8.3.0", "tsx":"^4.0.0", "typescript":"^5.7.0", "vitest":"^4.0.0" }. Create tsconfig.json mirroring packages/core/tsconfig.json (ES2022 / ESNext / moduleResolution bundler / strict / declaration / outDir ./dist / include src/**/*). Create src/index.ts as a minimal stub `console.log('@asr/submission boot');` (replaced by the bootstrap task). Run pnpm install so the workspace links.

Acceptance:
`pnpm install && pnpm --filter @asr/submission build` exits 0 and produces packages/submission/dist/index.js.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not yet import auth/http/mcp/workflow/db modules in index.ts (they do not exist) — the full composition from specs/submission-package.md#composition lands incrementally. Match dependency versions to specs/submission-package.md#packagejson to avoid drift; better-sqlite3/yauzl/octokit deps are added by the tasks that need them.
close_reason: Implemented: scaffolded @asr/submission package manifest, tsconfig, and boot stub

title: Add Submission and SubmissionStatus union to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#submission

Context:
Submission is the write-side aggregate tracked through the pipeline; SubmissionStatus is the discriminated union the workflow engine and the GET /submissions/:id endpoint switch on. It embeds SkillManifest and (in scan-complete) ScanReport, so both must already be defined.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append; do NOT modify lines 1-52)

Change:
Append verbatim from specs/types.md#submission: export type SkillClassification = 'md-only'|'code-containing'; export interface Submission (id ULID, manifest: SkillManifest, classification, contentHash, submittedAt, submittedBy, branchName?, prNumber?, status: SubmissionStatus); and the full export type SubmissionStatus = discriminated union of 13 { phase: ... } members (uploaded, classifying, pushing-to-forgejo, auto-approved, questionnaire-pending, scanning, scan-complete{report:ScanReport}, user-confirmation-pending, compliance-review, approved, published, rejected, withdrawn).

Acceptance:
A vitest type-test (added in the verification task) constructs a Submission with status { phase: 'scan-complete', report } and exhaustively narrows SubmissionStatus by phase; `pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds.

Risks / out of scope:
Keep union member tags and field names byte-identical to the spec — the SQLite schema (asr-7jd) and API serialise these phase tags directly. No persistence logic here.
close_reason: Implemented: added canonical Submission and SubmissionStatus types to @asr/core with regression type coverage

title: Add scanning result types (ScanReport, ScanFinding, verdict enums) to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#scanning

Context:
ScanReport is the canonical shape returned by the external scanner container's runScanner(); SubmissionStatus and the workflow branch on ScanReport.verdict. These types must exist before the Submission union (which embeds ScanReport) and before the security-scan epic wires the container.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append; do NOT modify lines 1-52)

Change:
Append the scanning types verbatim from specs/types.md#scanning: export type ScanSeverity = 'critical'|'high'|'medium'|'low'; export type ScanTool = 'gitleaks'|'trivy'|'foxguard'|'opengrep'|'veracode'; export type ScanVerdict = 'pass'|'review_required'|'block'; export interface ScanFinding (tool, ruleId, severity, file, line, message, snippet?); export interface ScanReport (submissionId, scanId, contentHash, scannerImage, startedAt, completedAt, durationMs, verdict, findings, toolResults: Record<ScanTool,{exitCode;findingCount;skipped?}>, signature?).

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds and a consumer importing { ScanReport, ScanVerdict } from '@asr/core' type-checks (locked in by the verification task's type-test).

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement computeVerdict() — that pure function lives in @asr/core/scan-verdict and is owned by the security-scan epic. Types only here.
close_reason: Implemented: added canonical scanning result types and a core type regression test

title: Add SkillManifest + permissions/compatibility types to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#skill-manifest

Context:
SkillManifest is the parsed, validated shape of a skill's manifest.yaml and the root type every other flow (submission, scan, publish) references. It must exist exactly once in @asr/core before the SKILL.md parser (asr-4bc), ForgejoClient (asr-she), and the zip classifier (asr-crh) can consume it.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append after the legacy block ending at :52; do NOT modify lines 1-52)

Change:
Append the canonical manifest types verbatim from specs/types.md#skill-manifest: export type SkillKind = 'skill' | 'persona'; export type PersonaMode = 'inject' | 'delegate'; export interface PermissionsManifest (network, networkHosts?, filesystem: 'none'|'read-own'|'read-write-own', subprocess, environment: string[]); export interface CompatibilityManifest ('claude-code'?, codex?); export interface SkillManifest (name, version, author, description, tags, kind, persona_mode?, references?, entrypoint?, dependencies?, permissions, compatibility?).

Acceptance:
`rg "interface SkillManifest" packages` returns exactly one line (packages/core/src/types.ts) and `pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not delete/rename the legacy SkillMeta/Skill/InstalledSkill types (parser.ts:2 and the CLI import them; their removal is owned by CLI migration stories). No zod schema here — that is the manifest-parser story asr-4bc.
close_reason: Implemented: added canonical SkillManifest, permissions, compatibility, and persona types to @asr/core

title: Story: Production boot guard in Dockerfile.api entrypoint (refuse prod+mock)
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: ARCHITECTURE.md; .claude/CLAUDE.md (hard rule)

Goal: Enforce the hard rule that NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock MUST fail to boot, at the deploy/docker/Dockerfile.api entrypoint layer (defence in depth on top of the app-level guard owned by Phase 1). The entrypoint exits non-zero with a clear diagnostic before the Node process starts the server.

Acceptance: 'docker run -e NODE_ENV=production -e AUTH_MODE=mock asr-api' exits non-zero and prints a message naming the offending combination; 'docker run -e NODE_ENV=production -e AUTH_MODE=entra ...' starts and answers 200 on /healthz.

title: Story: Workflow interaction endpoints (questionnaire/scan/confirm/approve/reject)
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/api.md#workflow-interactions; specs/api.md#requestresponse-schemas; specs/workflow.md#separation-of-duties

Goal: Implement the five workflow endpoints on the submission service: POST /submissions/:id/questionnaire, GET /submissions/:id/scan, POST /submissions/:id/confirm, POST /submissions/:id/approve, POST /submissions/:id/reject. Each delivers the corresponding HITL signal into the Flowcraft engine, enforces the role matrix, and enforces separation of duties (submitter != approver) at the handler in addition to the HITL node.

Acceptance: integration tests show POST /questionnaire transitions phase to scanning; POST /approve by a distinct Compliance sub returns 200 with phase=published; POST /approve by the submitter returns 403 separation_of_duties_violation; POST /reject without a 10-500 char reason returns 400.

title: Story: Flowcraft approval pipeline blueprint (code-path HITL nodes)
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/workflow.md#pipeline; specs/workflow.md#blueprint-sketch

Goal: Define the Flowcraft approvalPipeline Blueprint in packages/submission with the code-containing path nodes (classify -> push-to-forgejo -> questionnaire[HITL,7d] -> scan -> confirmation[HITL,14d] -> review[HITL,30d,Compliance] -> publish) and the md-only auto-approve branch reusing the same publish node. Compute nodes are idempotent; HITL nodes carry the timeouts and allowedActors/forbiddenActors from the spec. ctx.svc resolves ForgejoClient; ctx.audit writes via the audit helper; the scan node calls runScanner.

Acceptance: an integration test instantiates the blueprint and drives a code-containing submission through every node to status=published using mocked HITL signals and a mocked scan node, and asserts the md-only branch reaches publish without questionnaire/scan/review.

title: Story: Entra ID auth middleware (mock dev / OIDC prod) + role matrix
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/security.md#entra-id-oidc; specs/api.md#authentication--authorization; specs/security.md#dev-mode

Goal: Implement Hono auth middleware that in prod validates the Entra bearer token (JWKS signature, aud, iss) and extracts the roles claim, and in dev (AUTH_MODE=mock) injects a mock identity from MOCK_USER_SUB/MOCK_USER_ROLES. Gate every endpoint per the role matrix; /health, /version and /webhooks/* are exempt. Boot must hard-fail when NODE_ENV=production and AUTH_MODE=mock.

Acceptance: with AUTH_MODE=mock and MOCK_USER_ROLES=Compliance a Compliance-only route returns 200; with MOCK_USER_ROLES=Submitter the same route returns 403 insufficient_permissions; a boot test asserts the process exits non-zero when NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock.

title: Story: Scanner container image + orchestrator (signed verdict)
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/security-scanning.md; specs/security.md#security-scanning

Goal: Build the dedicated scanner Docker image (Gitleaks + Trivy + Foxguard + Opengrep; Veracode optional) with pinned tool versions, the scan-orchestrator that runs the tools in parallel, aggregates SARIF, normalizes severity, and computes a verdict (pass/block/review_required) emitted as a signed verdict. Provide the runScanner runner that invokes the container, records scan_results keyed by (submissionId, contentHash), and returns the canonical ScanReport from @asr/core.

Acceptance: running the scanner container against a fixture skill containing a planted secret yields verdict=block (Gitleaks hard-block) and a clean fixture yields verdict=pass; the runner returns a ScanReport whose signed verdict verifies.

title: Story: MD-only auto-approve publish workflow
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/workflow.md#pipeline (MD-only branch); SPEC.md invariant; specs/git-integration.md#branch-strategy

Goal: For an md-only classified submission, run the auto-approve path: create a branch, commit skill files per-file, open a PR, merge via the system (merge-capable) identity, publish the artifact to the generic package registry, and mark the submission published. No direct-to-main writes.

Acceptance: an end-to-end test (mocked or live Forgejo) drives an md-only submission to status=published having gone through a real PR+merge; the merged commit exists on main and the package artifact is retrievable.

title: Story: Submission API — POST /submissions, GET /submissions/:id
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/api.md#post-submissions, #endpoints (Upload & Lifecycle), #error-responses

Goal: Hono routes to accept a zip upload (multipart), run classifier+extractor, parse SKILL.md, persist a submissions row, kick the workflow, return the submission id; plus GET /submissions/:id returning current status. Dev auth = mock principal.

Acceptance: curl POST /submissions with an MD-only zip returns 201 + {id,status}; GET /submissions/:id returns the documented JSON; an oversized/invalid zip returns the documented error envelope; covered by an integration test.

title: Story: SQLite migration framework + submissions schema
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/audit.md#schema (submissions table); specs/submission-package.md (db module); better-sqlite3 WAL

Goal: Add a forward-only SQLite migration runner (better-sqlite3, WAL) and the initial migration creating the submissions table (status enum, optimistic-lock column for the per-skill mutex) plus indexes. audit_events is owned by the Audit epic; this story stops at submissions.

Acceptance: vitest: running migrations on a temp DB creates submissions with the documented columns; re-running is a no-op; an UPDATE guarded by the lock column rejects a stale write; `pnpm test` green.

title: Story: Zip classifier + safe yauzl extractor
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/security.md#zip-upload-hardening (#must-have-controls, #implementation-sketch), #classification-whitelist-approach

Goal: Implement safe zip extraction with yauzl (path-traversal, zip-bomb size/ratio caps, symlink rejection, polyglot guard) and a whitelist classifier labelling an upload md-only vs code-containing. Drives Phase 1 auto-approve routing. Never use unzipper.

Acceptance: vitest: a path-traversal zip and a zip-bomb fixture are rejected; an MD-only zip classifies as md-only and a zip containing a .py classifies as code-containing; `pnpm test` green.

title: Story: ForgejoClient (branch, per-file commit, PR, merge, package upload)
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient, #branch-strategy, #known-forgejo--github-differences, #token-security

Goal: Implement ForgejoClient in @asr/core using Octokit with baseUrl override on /api/v1: create branch, create/update file per-file (sequential, idempotent on 409), open PR, merge PR (merge-capable token), upload generic package artifact. Dual-token model: the upload token cannot merge.

Acceptance: vitest with a mocked Forgejo (nock/msw) exercises branch -> per-file commit -> PR -> merge -> package upload and asserts the sequential commit order + 409 idempotency; `rg -i github packages/core/src` shows no GitHub host references in the client.

title: Story: Scaffold packages/submission/ service
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/submission-package.md#module-layout, #packagejson, #composition, #production-mode-enforcement

Goal: Create the packages/submission/ workspace (Hono on Node 22, ESM) with the documented module layout (routes, db, workflow, lib), package.json wired into the pnpm workspace, tsconfig extending root, and an entrypoint wiring the Hono app. Include the production-mode guard (NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock must fail to boot).

Acceptance: `pnpm --filter @asr/submission build` succeeds and the server boots responding 200 on GET /healthz in dev (AUTH_MODE=mock); booting with NODE_ENV=production AUTH_MODE=mock exits non-zero.

title: Replace legacy types.ts with canonical ASR types in @asr/core
description:
Parent story: (self — flat task, no story parent; collapsed from former tier:story asr-4ea because acceptance was a single PR)
Parent epic:  asr-dft

Spec ref:     specs/types.md (whole, lines 1-241); enforced by .claude/CLAUDE.md "Canonical types live in @asr/core".

Context:
Seven downstream stories (asr-7jd, asr-she, asr-crh, asr-4bc, asr-a03, asr-0mf.4, asr-0mf.5) all import shared types from @asr/core. Today packages/core/src/types.ts (51 lines) only defines legacy skify shapes (SkillMeta, Skill, InstalledSkill, LockFile). This task replaces them with the full ASR canonical set so no consumer package re-declares anything.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (rewrite; currently 51 lines)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (already re-exports * from ./types.js; verify Forgejo comment stays)

Change:
Replace packages/core/src/types.ts contents with the canonical types from specs/types.md:
  SkillManifest, SkillManifestPermissions, SkillManifestCompatibility,
  Submission, SubmissionStatus (string union: pending|scanning|review|approved|rejected|published|yanked),
  ScanReport, ScanFinding, ScanVerdict,
  QuestionnaireSchema, QuestionnaireAnswer,
  AuditEvent, AuditAction (closed string union),
  VersionDiff,
  Skill (published artifact — rename legacy Skill to LegacySkillSearchResult or drop if no consumer),
  RegistryIndex, MarketplaceManifest.
packages/core/src/index.ts already does `export * from ./types.js` so re-exports flow automatically.

Acceptance:
1. `pnpm --filter @asr/core build` (tsc) exits 0.
2. `tsc --noEmit` on a stub importing { SubmissionStatus, SkillManifest, ScanReport, AuditEvent, RegistryIndex, MarketplaceManifest } from @asr/core type-checks.
3. `rg "interface SkillManifest" packages` returns exactly one match (packages/core/src/types.ts).

Risks / out of scope:
- Do NOT add zod schemas here — those live in @asr/submission per existing pattern.
- Do NOT remove parser.ts; if it references legacy SkillMeta/Skill, keep aliases temporarily so asr-cli still builds — refactor in a follow-up.
- Forgejo client types stay in @asr/core/forgejo (asr-she.1), not in types.ts.
- AUDIT_ACTIONS enum const lives in audit.ts per asr-pf0, not in types.ts.
close_reason: Implemented: replaced legacy Skill export with canonical published skill type, added canonical questionnaire, audit, version diff, registry, and marketplace types, and covered them with core type tests

title: Epic: Phase 2 — Approval Pipeline (code-skill scan + approve)
description:
Phase 2 of the ASR plan (SPEC.md#phase-2-approval-pipeline-week-34).

Goal: A code-containing skill can be questionnaire'd, scanned, confirmed, approved, and published.

In scope:
- Flowcraft integration with HITL nodes (specs/workflow.md)
- Scanner container image: Gitleaks + Trivy + Foxguard + Opengrep, signed verdict (specs/security-scanning.md)
- Questionnaire + confirmation + approve/reject endpoints (specs/api.md)
- Compliance approval UI flow (specs/web-ui.md)
- Entra ID authentication: mock in dev, real OIDC in prod (specs/security.md)
- Per-skill workflow mutex (optimistic lock on submissions.status) + crash-resume

Out of scope (other epics): MD-only path (Phase 1), CLI/marketplace/package registry (Phase 3), enabling remaining/optional scanners + Azure deploy (Phase 4), audit chain (Audit epic), MCP server (MCP epic).

Success criteria: a code-containing zip routes to the questionnaire, the scanner container produces a signed verdict, a compliance reviewer (distinct Entra sub from submitter) approves in the UI, and the skill publishes via Forgejo PR+merge.

Depends on: Phase 1 (shared Forgejo client, types, SQLite schema, web shell).

title: Epic: Phase 1 — Foundation (MD-only skill end-to-end)
description:
Phase 1 of the ASR plan (SPEC.md#phase-1-foundation-week-12).

Goal: A submitted MD-only skill makes it through Forgejo to a published state, end-to-end, in dev (AUTH_MODE=mock).

In scope:
- Scaffold packages/submission/ (specs/submission-package.md)
- Canonical types in @asr/core (specs/types.md)
- Forgejo client: branch, per-file commit, PR, merge, generic package upload (specs/git-integration.md)
- Zip classifier (whitelist) + safe yauzl extractor (specs/security.md)
- SQLite migration framework + submissions schema (specs/audit.md#schema)
- Submission API: POST /submissions, GET /submissions/:id (specs/api.md)
- Registry API: GET /skills, GET /skills/:owner/:name, download endpoint (specs/registry-api.md)
- MD-only auto-approve workflow: creates PR, merges via system identity, publishes artifact
- docker-compose dev stack: Forgejo + API + Web (specs/deployment.md)

Out of scope (other epics): code-scan/questionnaire pipeline (Phase 2), CLI/marketplace (Phase 3), Azure/Bicep (Phase 4), audit HMAC chain + anchoring (Audit epic), version diffing/yank (Versioning epic), MCP server (MCP epic).

Success criteria: e2e dev run uploads an MD-only zip and the skill appears via GET /skills with a downloadable artifact, having gone through a real Forgejo PR+merge. No direct-to-main writes.

Depends on: nothing.

title: Visual: Unknown routes do not show graceful 404
description: Flow: Empty + error states. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: opening /skills/does-not-exist should show a friendly 404/resource-not-found state with a retry or navigation action. Actual: the route renders the normal browse page with two skills and no indication that the requested skill/path does not exist. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-route-skills-does-not-exist.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx has no router/error route; all paths other than exact /review are treated as BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Duplicate of open bead asr-7v6 (unknown skill route does not show an error state)

title: Visual: API failures render as empty successful browse state
description: Flow: Empty + error states. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: a 4xx/5xx API response renders an inline error message/card with retry, never a blank or misleading successful state. Actual: with GET /api/skills intercepted as 500, the browse page still shows 'Connected to Registry' and 'No skills found. Try a different search term.' with zero skills/stars, no retry button, and no error language. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-api-500-skills.png. Root cause hypothesis: fetchSkills does not check res.ok and the catch path collapses API failure into the same empty array state used for legitimate empty searches.

title: Visual: Approval decision buttons have no visible flow
description: Flow: Approval dashboard. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: approve/reject actions open a review/decision flow, require confirmation/comment where appropriate, enforce separation of duties, and visibly update status or show an error. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review produces no visible change, no modal, no disabled/loading state, and no status update. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-before.png and .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-after-approve-click.png. Root cause hypothesis: buttons in ReviewDashboard are static type='button' elements without onClick handlers or connection to /review/:id decision UI.
close_reason: Duplicate of open bead asr-14w (approval decision buttons have no visible result)

title: Visual: Approval dashboard hides API 404 behind mock data
description: Flow: Approval dashboard. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: review queue calls the documented submissions endpoint and shows loading/error states; API failures should render an inline error card with retry. Actual: /review calls http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, which returns 404 in this dev stack, logs console errors, then silently displays mock submissions as if live. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-before.png; console shows 404 for /api/submissions?status=pending. Root cause hypothesis: web uses an unversioned/incorrect submissions endpoint and catch/fallback logic masks backend failures instead of surfacing the error state.

title: Visual: Submission wizard route is missing
description: Flow: Submission. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: /submit exposes the new submission wizard with upload, manifest, questionnaire, review/submit steps; validation should catch empty title, oversize zip, and bad SKILL.md inline. Actual: navigating to /publish during visual review rendered the browse page; App.tsx has no /submit or /publish route and no upload/publish controls were visible from the browse UI. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-route-missing.png. Root cause hypothesis: React Router/submission wizard has not been wired; App.tsx routes everything except /review to BrowseRegistry.

title: Visual: Skill detail route falls back to browse page
description: Flow: Skill detail. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: /skills/:owner/:name renders a dedicated skill detail screen with owner/name header, install snippet using 'asr install owner/name', preview tabs, versions, permissions, and audit per specs/web-ui.md. Actual: opening /skills/does-not-exist or any /skills/... path renders the browse grid with no 404/detail route; skill details are only an in-page modal from card clicks, and the install snippet says 'asr add ...' instead of the specified CLI command. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-security-review.png and .playwright-mcp/error-route-skills-does-not-exist.png. Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only branches on window.location.pathname === '/review'; all other paths render BrowseRegistry.

title: Visual: Browse screen omits persistent mock auth banner
description: Flow: Auth mock mode on Landing / browse. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: specs/web-ui.md requires a persistent yellow 'Mock auth: <role>' banner in dev/mock mode so screenshots cannot be confused with production. Actual: /review shows 'Mock auth: Compliance', but / shows no mock identity banner and no other auth affordance. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry header does not share the ReviewDashboard topbar/session component and has no MockAuthProvider/session UI.

title: Visual: approval actions provide no feedback
description:
Flow: approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Clicked Approve for secure-code-review.
Expected: action opens a confirmation modal or decision panel flow that repeats version/risk and submits or reports validation/permission state; buttons should not appear successful without feedback.
Actual: screen remains visually unchanged after clicking Approve. No modal, toast, disabled state, inline error, or status change appears.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/review-dashboard.png before, .playwright-mcp/review-approve-after.png after
Root cause hypothesis: row-level Approve/Reject buttons render without action handlers or the detail/decision flow is not wired.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-14w (approval decision buttons have no visible result)

title: Visual: review detail route renders browse instead of approval detail
description:
Flow: approval dashboard / review detail. Navigated directly to /review/sub-1042 at 1280x800.
Expected: approval detail screen with diff, dependencies, permissions, scan, audit tabs and sticky decision panel per specs/web-ui.md.
Actual: route renders the browse page, so compliance cannot inspect a submission detail or make a contextual decision.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/review-detail-route.png
Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only special-cases window.location.pathname === /review and otherwise renders BrowseRegistry; no router/detail screen is mounted.

title: Visual: unknown skill route does not show an error state
description: Flow: Empty + error states. Expected: opening /skills/does-not-exist should render a graceful not-found or inline error with retry/navigation, not unrelated content. Actual: direct navigation to /skills/does-not-exist renders the normal browse page with skill cards and no indication the requested skill route failed. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-route-skill-not-found-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: App treats every path except /review as BrowseRegistry and has no route-level 404 or skill detail route handling.

title: Visual: approval decision buttons have no visible result
description: Flow: Approval dashboard decision actions at /review, 1280x800. Expected: Approve and Reject actions should render an actionable flow such as confirmation, loading/disabled state, success/error feedback, or status update. Actual: clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the UI unchanged with no toast, inline message, modal, status change, or disabled state. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-approve-click-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: buttons are rendered without onClick handlers or mutation wiring.

title: Visual: approval dashboard silently falls back to mock queue on API 404
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions list should reflect the API contract or show an inline error with retry if GET /api/submissions?status=pending fails. Actual: browser console shows 404 from http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending, but the UI still displays three mock submissions with no error state, so reviewers can mistake fixtures for live queue data. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/approval-dashboard-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard returns early on non-ok responses and keeps mockReviewQueue seeded in state instead of surfacing the API failure.

title: Visual: submission upload flow is not reachable
description: Flow: Submission. Expected: publish/upload flow exposed with required-field validation, oversize zip handling, and bad SKILL.md validation. Actual: no publish/upload entry point is visible on browse or review; direct navigation to /submit renders the browse page instead of a submission form, so validation states cannot be exercised. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/submission-route-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: App route switch only handles /review; all other paths render BrowseRegistry, and no Submission component is wired.

title: Visual: review queue calls wrong submissions endpoint and silently shows mock data
description: Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800. Expected: pending submissions list comes from the Submission API contract GET /api/v1/submissions?status=pending or shows an inline API error/empty state if unavailable. Actual: browser console records 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending and the UI silently displays hard-coded mock rows, so reviewers can see wrong data without warning. Artefacts: .playwright-mcp/04-review-dashboard.png and .playwright-mcp/console-all.log. Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard uses /api/submissions instead of /api/v1/submissions and swallows non-ok responses.
close_reason: Duplicate visual review beads; asr-0yr duplicates existing open asr-04w, and asr-4zl duplicates existing open asr-37o.

title: Visual: unknown skill route has no graceful 404 state

close_reason: Duplicate visual review beads; asr-0yr duplicates existing open asr-04w, and asr-4zl duplicates existing open asr-37o.

title: Visual: approval decision buttons do nothing

title: Visual: submit route renders browse page instead of upload form

title: Visual: invalid skill route lacks not-found state

title: Visual: approval decision buttons do nothing

title: Visual: approval dashboard shows mock data after submissions API 404
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at /review, 1280x800.

Actual: Browser console reports 404 for http://localhost:3001/api/submissions?status=pending while the dashboard still renders the built-in mock queue as if it were live data. specs/api.md defines GET /api/v1/submissions, so the UI endpoint appears mismatched.

title: Visual: approval decision buttons provide no feedback

Flow: Approval dashboard decision actions.

title: Visual: publish upload flow is not reachable

title: Visual: API failure is shown as connected empty registry
description:
Flow: Empty/error state at 1280x800. Stopped asr-api, loaded /, then waited for the skills request to fail.
Expected: a failed registry request renders an inline error message with retry and does not claim the registry is connected.
Actual: console shows ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED for /api/skills, but the UI still displays Connected to Registry, zero skills/stars, and No skills found. Try a different search term. This misrepresents an outage as an empty result.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/11-api-down-error-after.png; console: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/11-api-down-console.log
Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry catches fetch errors by setting skills to [] without tracking error state, while connection status is hardcoded as connected.
close_reason: Duplicate visual-review beads from current run; canonical existing issues are asr-9i0, asr-1x1, asr-abd, and asr-4si respectively.

title: Visual: approval dashboard uses mock fallback after API 404
description:
Flow: Approval dashboard at 1280x800. Navigated to /review with the dev API running.

Actual: API returns 404 for /api/submissions?status=pending, console logs two failed requests, but the UI silently renders three hardcoded submissions. Clicking Approve on secure-code-review leaves the row/status unchanged and shows no feedback.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/05-approval-dashboard-before.png and .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/06-approval-approve-after.png; console: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/05-approval-dashboard-console.log
Root cause hypothesis: ReviewDashboard falls back to mockReviewQueue on fetch failure and decision buttons are render-only with no handlers wired to /api/v1/submissions/:id/approve or /reject.

title: Fix CLI declaration build after core API removal
description: pnpm build fails in @asr/cli during tsup DTS generation because packages/cli/src/index.ts imports searchSkillRepos, listSkillsInRepo, and downloadSkillFiles from @asr/core, but @asr/core no longer exports them. Reproduce with: pnpm build. The web and core builds pass first; the failure is isolated to @asr/cli declaration output.

title: Align Docker e2e smoke test with current compose services
description:
Required gate `pnpm test:e2e` currently starts the Forgejo-only compose stack, then waits for `http://localhost:3001/health` and fails with `no such service: api` in recent api logs.

Repro:
`npx pnpm@9.15.0 test:e2e`

Observed:
Forgejo starts, but the script fails: `E2E smoke check FAILED: API health check timed out (http://localhost:3001/health)` and `no such service: api`.

Expected:
Either compose includes the API service expected by the smoke test, or the smoke test matches the currently supported stack until the API lands.

title: Sync web package lockfile after remark-gfm addition
description:
Required gate `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` currently fails before code changes are tested because `packages/web/package.json` includes `remark-gfm`, but `pnpm-lock.yaml` does not include the matching specifier.

Repro:
`npx pnpm@9.15.0 install --frozen-lockfile`

Observed:
`ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE` for `<ROOT>/packages/web/package.json`; lockfile specifiers omit `remark-gfm`.

Expected:
Frozen install succeeds with the committed lockfile.

title: Add Playwright E2E for SoD-disabled buttons and queue removal
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md:147-156 (Acceptance Tests E2E #4 + queue removal); ralph-scripts/PROMPT_visual_review.md (Playwright drives the dev stack)

Context:
The story's acceptance signal is a deterministic Playwright assertion: the decision buttons are disabled when submitter sub == reviewer sub, and approving a pending submission removes it from the /review queue. This task adds that E2E so the behaviour built in asr-0mf.4/.5/.6 is verified end-to-end against the mock dev stack.

Affected files:
- packages/web/e2e/approval.spec.ts (new — Playwright spec)
- packages/web/playwright.config.ts (new or extend — point baseURL at the dev web server / docker stack on :5173)

Change:
Add a Playwright test that (1) seeds/uses a mock session whose `sub` equals a seeded pending submission's `submittedBy`, navigates to `/review/:id`, and asserts the Approve and Reject buttons are `disabled`; and (2) with a reviewer whose `sub` differs, approves a pending submission and asserts it disappears from the `/review` queue list.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/web test:e2e` (or the repo's Playwright runner) passes the `approval.spec.ts` cases: buttons disabled on submitter==reviewer, and the approved submission row is absent from /review after approval.

Risks / out of scope:
Runs against mock auth only — no real Entra. Do not assert on the 5-tab richness beyond what asr-0mf.5 implements. Keep selectors role/text based (getByRole) so they survive styling changes.

Depends on asr-0mf.4 (queue) and asr-0mf.6 (decision panel).

title: Add sticky decision panel with separation-of-duties enforcement
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md:101-104 (sticky decision panel; Approve/Reject disabled if submitter sub == reviewer sub); specs/web-ui.md:154 (acceptance #4); specs/api.md:24-25,101,119,157-158 (POST /submissions/:id/approve, POST /submissions/:id/reject {reason})

Context:
This is the headline of the story: the sticky decision panel enforcing separation of duties. Approve/Reject must be disabled when the submission's `submittedBy` equals the reviewer's `sub` (the UI mirror of the server-side SoD check in workflow.md:226). Reject requires a reason; Approve's comment is optional.

Affected files:
- packages/web/src/routes/DecisionPanel.tsx (new — comment textarea, Approve + Reject buttons, confirmation modal; `useMutation` for approve/reject)
- packages/web/src/routes/ReviewDetail.tsx (mount `<DecisionPanel submission={...} />` in the sticky slot)

Change:
Implement `DecisionPanel({ submission })`: read `useSession()`. Compute `isSelf = submission.submittedBy === session.sub`. Render Approve and Reject buttons both `disabled={isSelf}`; additionally Reject is disabled until the reason textarea is non-empty. Approve → `useMutation` POST `/submissions/${id}/approve`; Reject → POST `/submissions/${id}/reject` with body `{ reason }`. On success invalidate the `['submissions','pending']` query. Show a confirmation modal repeating version + risk before firing.

Acceptance:
vitest: when `submission.submittedBy === session.sub` BOTH Approve and Reject buttons have the `disabled` attribute; when they differ, Approve is enabled and Reject becomes enabled only after typing a reason; clicking Approve issues a POST to `/submissions/<id>/approve`.

Risks / out of scope:
Client disabling is defence-in-depth, not the security boundary — do not remove/duplicate the server SoD check. Do not build the queue (asr-0mf.4) or other tabs. Reason field must be sent only to /reject.

Depends on asr-0mf.5 (mounts into the detail shell) and asr-0mf.2 (reads session.sub).

title: Add mock-aware SessionProvider + useSession hook
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md#auth-ux (mock banner "Mock auth: <role>"); specs/security.md (Entra roles: Submitter/Compliance/Admin)

Context:
Client-side route gating and the separation-of-duties check both need the current principal's Entra `sub` and roles. There is no client session context yet. In dev (AUTH_MODE=mock) the SPA must read a mock identity and show the persistent yellow "Mock auth: <role>" banner so screenshots can't be mistaken for prod; in prod the same hook will later be backed by MSAL (asr-k4m owns the server side).

Affected files:
- packages/web/src/auth/SessionProvider.tsx (new — React context holding `{ sub, name, roles }`; in mock mode reads `import.meta.env.VITE_MOCK_SUB` / `VITE_MOCK_ROLES` with sensible dev defaults; renders the yellow "Mock auth: <role>" banner)
- packages/web/src/auth/useSession.ts (new — `useSession(): Session` hook that throws if used outside the provider)
- packages/web/src/main.tsx:6-10 (wrap `RouterProvider` in `<SessionProvider>`)

Change:
Define `interface Session { sub: string; name: string; roles: Array<'Submitter'|'Compliance'|'Admin'> }`. Implement `SessionProvider` reading mock env vars (default `sub='dev-compliance'`, `roles=['Compliance']`) and rendering a fixed-position yellow banner with text `Mock auth: <roles.join(',')>` whenever `import.meta.env.MODE !== 'production'`. Export `useSession`.

Acceptance:
vitest: rendering a child calling `useSession()` inside `<SessionProvider>` with `VITE_MOCK_SUB=u1`,`VITE_MOCK_ROLES=Compliance` returns `{ sub:'u1', roles:['Compliance'] }`, the banner text "Mock auth: Compliance" is in the document, and calling `useSession()` outside the provider throws.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT add `@azure/msal-react` or any real OIDC flow here — prod token acquisition is asr-k4m. Tokens must never be written to the DOM (no data-token); store session only in React context.

Depends on asr-0mf.1 (provider wraps RouterProvider added there).

title: Wire React Router + React Query into the web SPA entry
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md#stack (React Router data routers + @tanstack/react-query); specs/web-ui.md#routes

Context:
The web SPA entry (packages/web/src/main.tsx:6) still renders the legacy skify `<App />` directly with no router and no query client, and packages/web/package.json has neither react-router-dom nor @tanstack/react-query. Every approval-UI screen in this story (/review, /review/:id) needs a data router and a shared QueryClient, so this task lays that foundation once.

Affected files:
- packages/web/package.json:18-23 (add `react-router-dom` ^7 and `@tanstack/react-query` ^5 to dependencies)
- packages/web/src/router.tsx (new — `createBrowserRouter` route table; `/` → existing App, `/review` and `/review/:id` lazy placeholders, `/error` element)
- packages/web/src/main.tsx:6-10 (replace `<App />` render with `<QueryClientProvider><RouterProvider router={router} /></QueryClientProvider>`)

Change:
Add the two deps. Create `router.tsx` exporting `router = createBrowserRouter([...])` with the index route rendering the current `App`, plus `/review`, `/review/:id`, and `/error` routes (the /review elements are temporary placeholders replaced by asr-0mf.4/.5). In main.tsx instantiate one `QueryClient` and wrap `RouterProvider` in `QueryClientProvider` inside the existing `<StrictMode>`.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/web build` succeeds AND a vitest+Testing-Library test rendering `<RouterProvider router={createMemoryRouter(routes,{initialEntries:['/review']})} />` shows the /review placeholder while initialEntries `['/']` still shows the existing browse heading.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT rewrite App.tsx content or change its `/api/skills` calls here (that is the separate browse-migration work). Keep the index route pointing at the existing App so the landing page keeps working. No auth wiring yet (asr-0mf.2).

title: Add slaTimeout cron job: extend-then-auto-reject sweep
description:
Parent story: asr-h4i
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry (lines 214-222); specs/workflow.md#state-persistence (lines 176-178); specs/audit.md (action enum, lines 43-76)

Context:
Stalled submissions must not sit in a HITL state forever. This periodic job enforces the SLA: it finds submissions whose current HITL stage is past its deadline and applies the policy from slaPolicy.ts — extend-once-then-auto-reject for questionnaire, immediate auto-reject for confirmation. Auto-reject is the same terminal transition a human reject produces, recorded in the audit chain with detail.reason='timeout'.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/slaTimeout.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/index.ts (register slaTimeout in the startJobs() registrar; create the file if absent per specs/submission-package.md composition)

Change:
Add `export async function runSlaSweep(now = new Date(), deps): Promise<{ extended: string[]; rejected: string[] }>`. For each non-terminal submission in a HITL stage, read the stage-entry timestamp from the Flowcraft `@flowcraft/history-sqlite` history (workflow.md line 178) and whether it was already extended; compute the deadline via `computeDeadline` (slaPolicy.ts). If `now > deadline`: when `nextAction` is `extend`, persist a one-time extension and call the notifier (asr-qdh) `sla_extended` to the submitter; when `auto_reject`, deliver the reject HITL signal (the same `{ phase:'rejected', reason }` path as POST /reject, asr-kkd.5) and emit `submission.expired` via `audit.emit` (asr-5qw.2) with `detail.reason='timeout'`. Export `registerSlaTimeoutJob(...)` scheduling `runSlaSweep` on an interval, called from `startJobs()` in jobs/index.ts. Inject the clock (`now`) and deps (history reader, repos, notifier, audit.emit) so tests are deterministic.

Acceptance:
Integration test `slaTimeout > extends a stale questionnaire once then auto-rejects` with a controlled clock: a submission whose questionnaire entered 8 days ago → first `runSlaSweep` returns it in `extended` and the mock notifier captures one `sla_extended` mail; advancing the clock past the extended deadline → second `runSlaSweep` returns it in `rejected`, the submission status is `rejected`, and an audit event with `action==='submission.expired'` and `detail.reason==='timeout'` was emitted. A confirmation stage past 14d is auto-rejected on the first pass.

Risks / out of scope:
Use only existing audit actions (specs/audit.md lines 43-76) — there is no `*.escalated` or `*.timeout` action; questionnaire/confirmation timeout uses `submission.expired` with detail.reason='timeout'. The compliance-review 30d escalate-to-admin path is the NEXT task (asr-h4i.3). Do NOT write status directly; reuse the asr-kkd.5 reject transition so separation-of-duties and chain emission stay consistent.

title: Add SLA stage policy table and deadline computation
description:
Parent story: asr-h4i
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry (lines 214-222)

Context:
The approval pipeline's HITL nodes carry per-stage timeouts (questionnaire 7d, confirmation 14d, review 30d) declared inline on the Flowcraft HitlNodes in packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.ts (asr-82o.2). The SLA scheduler needs one authoritative, testable place mapping each stage to its timeout, its on-expiry action, and whether it gets a one-time extension — so the cron job (next task) stays a thin driver over a pure policy.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/slaPolicy.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/slaPolicy.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export type SlaStage = 'questionnaire' | 'confirmation' | 'review'` and `export type SlaAction = 'extend' | 'auto_reject' | 'escalate'`. Export `export const SLA_POLICY: Record<SlaStage, { timeoutDays: number; firstExpiry: SlaAction; extensionDays: number }>` encoding the table at workflow.md lines 216-220: questionnaire {7,'extend',7}, confirmation {14,'auto_reject',0}, review {30,'escalate',7}. Add `export function computeDeadline(stage: SlaStage, enteredAtIso: string, extended = false): Date` (enteredAt + timeoutDays + (extended ? extensionDays : 0)). Add `export function nextAction(stage: SlaStage, alreadyExtended: boolean): SlaAction` returning firstExpiry on the first pass and 'auto_reject' once alreadyExtended is true (confirmation always 'auto_reject').

Acceptance:
vitest case `slaPolicy > drives each stage through its expiry policy` asserts: nextAction('questionnaire',false)==='extend' and nextAction('questionnaire',true)==='auto_reject'; nextAction('confirmation',false)==='auto_reject'; nextAction('review',false)==='escalate' and nextAction('review',true)==='auto_reject'; computeDeadline('questionnaire','2026-01-01T00:00:00Z') is 7 days later and with extended=true is 14 days later.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure module only — no DB, no Flowcraft, no notifications, no audit emit (those are the next two tasks). Do NOT redefine the stage timeouts anywhere else; the HitlNode `timeout` strings in approvalPipeline.ts and this table must agree (keep this table the single numeric source). Do NOT invent new audit actions.

title: Add forgejo (internal) and web (external) Container Apps
description:
Parent story: asr-rkr
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#container-apps (lines 175-191, 227-240)

Context:
Forgejo is the internal-only Git backend pinned to a single replica (single-instance
Git server); web is the stateless SPA host that can scale to zero. Both complete the
three-app prod topology alongside the api.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (edit; from asr-rkr task 1)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:175-240

Change:
Add two containerApps to containerapps.bicep:
- forgejo: ingress internal (external=false), targetPort 3000, minReplicas 1 /
  maxReplicas 1, image param (default '$ACR.azurecr.io/forgejo:15'), env
  FORGEJO__server__ROOT_URL and FORGEJO__database__DB_TYPE=sqlite3, forgejo-storage
  volume mounted at /data.
- web: ingress external, targetPort 80, minReplicas 0 / maxReplicas 3, image param
  (default '$ACR.azurecr.io/asr-web:latest').

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` exits 0; the ARM JSON has a
forgejo app with ingress.external == false and minReplicas == maxReplicas == 1, and
a web app with ingress.external == true, minReplicas 0 and maxReplicas 3.

Risks / out of scope:
Forgejo maxReplicas MUST stay 1. Do not expose forgejo externally. The final ACR
image reference for forgejo is set in asr-1h5 — use the image param here.

title: Configure api ingress CORS for web origin and localhost
description:
Parent story: asr-rkr
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#cors (lines 242-250)

Context:
The React SPA calls the api cross-origin. CORS must allow only the prod web origin
plus the dev origin http://localhost:5173, with Authorization + Content-Type headers
so bearer tokens work, while rejecting arbitrary origins.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (edit; from asr-rkr task 1)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:242-250

Change:
Add ingress.corsPolicy to the api containerApp: allowedOrigins =
['https://web.${fqdn}' (param), 'http://localhost:5173'], allowedMethods =
['GET','POST','PUT','DELETE','OPTIONS'], allowedHeaders =
['Authorization','Content-Type'].

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` exits 0 and the api app's
ingress.corsPolicy.allowedOrigins includes both the web origin param and
http://localhost:5173, and allowedHeaders includes Authorization and Content-Type.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not use a wildcard '*' origin. CORS applies to the api app only — web and forgejo
ingress are unaffected.

title: Wire api Container App secrets to Key Vault references
description:
Parent story: asr-rkr
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#secrets-management (lines 252-261)

Context:
The api needs the Forgejo upload/merge tokens and the audit HMAC key at runtime.
These are secrets and must come from Azure Key Vault (not plaintext env), surfaced
as Container App secrets and referenced by env vars.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (edit; from asr-rkr task 1)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:252-261

Change:
Add to the api containerApp configuration.secrets[] three Key Vault-backed secrets:
forgejo-upload-token, forgejo-merge-token, audit-hmac-key — each with a keyVaultUrl
(param keyVaultUri base) and identity (managed-identity resourceId param).
Reference them from container env vars FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN, FORGEJO_MERGE_TOKEN,
AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES via secretRef.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` exits 0 and the api app's
configuration.secrets[] contains exactly the three names forgejo-upload-token,
forgejo-merge-token, audit-hmac-key, each carrying a keyVaultUrl field.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not inline secret values. entra-client-secret belongs to the forgejo app (OIDC),
not the api — out of scope here.

title: Add api Container App Bicep with nobrl SQLite volume mount
description:
Parent story: asr-rkr
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#container-apps (lines 195-225)

Context:
The API is the external-facing Hono service. In prod it must run
NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=entra (never mock) and persist workflow.db on Azure
Files. SQLite over Azure Files corrupts under byte-range locking, so the volume MUST
mount with mountOptions=nobrl (project hard rule).

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (new)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:195-225

Change:
Create deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep declaring the api containerApp
('Microsoft.App/containerApps@2024-03-01'): ingress external on targetPort 3001,
scale minReplicas 1 / maxReplicas 3, env NODE_ENV=production, AUTH_MODE=entra,
AZURE_TENANT_ID, AZURE_CLIENT_ID, FORGEJO_URL, DATABASE_PATH=/app/data/workflow.db,
image param (default '$ACR.azurecr.io/asr-api:latest'). Add template.volumes[]
{ name:'api-data', storageName:'api-storage', storageType:'AzureFile',
mountOptions:'nobrl' } and a matching container volumeMounts entry at /app/data.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` exits 0 and the ARM JSON
for the api app contains a volume whose mountOptions == "nobrl" and env vars
NODE_ENV=production and AUTH_MODE=entra.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT add secrets, CORS, or the forgejo/web apps here (separate tasks). Never set
AUTH_MODE=mock. minReplicas must be 1 (SQLite single-writer), maxReplicas 3.

title: Add forgejo-storage and api-storage environment storage bindings
description:
Parent story: asr-5v0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#infrastructure-bicep (lines 146-170)

Context:
Container Apps mount Azure Files via named managedEnvironments/storages bindings,
not directly. The api app later mounts api-storage with nobrl and forgejo mounts
forgejo-storage. This task registers both bindings on the managed environment.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/main.bicep (edit; from asr-5v0 tasks 1-2)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:146-170

Change:
Add two resources of type 'Microsoft.App/managedEnvironments/storages@2024-03-01'
with parent = env:
- forgejoStorage named 'forgejo-storage', properties.azureFile
  {accountName=storageAccount.name, accountKey=storageAccount.listKeys().keys[0].value,
  shareName='forgejo-data', accessMode='ReadWrite'}.
- apiStorage named 'api-storage' with the same shape but shareName='api-data'.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/main.bicep` exits 0 and the ARM JSON contains
exactly two Microsoft.App/managedEnvironments/storages resources named
forgejo-storage and api-storage whose azureFile.shareName values are forgejo-data
and api-data respectively.

Risks / out of scope:
No app definitions here. accessMode must be ReadWrite for both. Source accountKey
from listKeys() — never hardcode storage keys.

title: Add StorageV2 account and two Azure Files shares to main.bicep
description:
Parent story: asr-5v0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#infrastructure-bicep (lines 115-130)

Context:
Forgejo's Git data and the API's SQLite workflow DB both need durable storage that
survives Container App restarts and scale events. Two Azure Files shares back the
two managedEnvironments/storages bindings added by the next task.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/main.bicep (edit; created in asr-5v0 task 1)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:115-130

Change:
Add to main.bicep:
- resource storageAccount 'Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts@2023-01-01' named
  'asrstorage${uniqueString(resourceGroup().id)}', sku name 'Standard_LRS',
  kind 'StorageV2'.
- resource forgejoShare 'Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/fileServices/shares@2023-01-01'
  named '${storageAccount.name}/default/forgejo-data', properties.shareQuota 50.
- resource apiShare '.../fileServices/shares@2023-01-01' named
  '${storageAccount.name}/default/api-data', properties.shareQuota 10.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/main.bicep` exits 0 and the ARM JSON contains
one Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts (kind "StorageV2") and exactly two
.../fileServices/shares whose shareQuota values are 50 and 10.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT add the managedEnvironments/storages bindings here (next task). Share names
must be exactly forgejo-data and api-data — the storages bindings and the app
volume mounts reference them by name.

title: Add Bicep managed environment and Log Analytics workspace
description:
Parent story: asr-5v0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#infrastructure-bicep (lines 106-144)

Context:
Phase 4 production runs on Azure Container Apps. The managed environment is the
substrate every prod app shares, and it must ship container stdout/stderr to a
Log Analytics workspace so operators can see logs (specs/deployment.md#monitoring).
This task lays the Bicep foundation that the storage and app stories build on.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/main.bicep (new)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:106-144 (params, logAnalytics, env)

Change:
Create deploy/azure/main.bicep with params `location` (default 'westeurope') and
`envName` (default 'asr-env'). Declare:
- resource logAnalytics 'Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces@2022-10-01'
  named '${envName}-logs', properties.retentionInDays 30, sku name 'PerGB2018'.
- resource env 'Microsoft.App/managedEnvironments@2024-03-01' named envName whose
  properties.appLogsConfiguration.destination = 'log-analytics' and
  logAnalyticsConfiguration.{customerId, sharedKey} reference the workspace's
  customerId and listKeys().primarySharedKey.
No storage or app resources in this task.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/main.bicep` exits 0 and the emitted ARM JSON
contains one Microsoft.OperationalInsights/workspaces resource and one
Microsoft.App/managedEnvironments resource whose appLogsConfiguration.destination
== "log-analytics".

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT add the storage account, file shares, storages bindings, or any
containerApps here (separate tasks/stories). Keep the API versions exactly as in
the spec to avoid drift.

title: Upload canonical skill.zip to generic package registry on publish
description:
Parent story: asr-p29
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     SPEC.md#phase-3-cli--registry-integration-week-56; specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (publishArtifact, lines 168-180); specs/cli-integration.md (post-install hash verification, line 105)

Context:
Merging the submission PR lands the skill files in Git, but the downloadable artifact is the canonical skill.zip in Forgejo's generic package registry. This task is the glue: after publish, upload that exact zip so `asr install` can fetch and hash-verify it. The download endpoint (asr-4it.3) already redirects to this URL.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/nodes/publish.ts (or the publishMdOnly orchestrator from asr-6hl.3 — add the upload step)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/nodes/publish.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In the publish path, after the version row is written (asr-ai5.3), obtain the canonical zip buffer from the zip packer (asr-6hl.2 — the same bytes whose canonical SHA-256 equals version.contentHash) and call forgejo.publishArtifact({ owner, name, version, zipBuffer }) (asr-she.5) on the registry-bound ForgejoClient. Make it idempotent: a 409 from publishArtifact (already uploaded) is a no-op success (publishArtifact already swallows 409).

Acceptance:
vitest (publish.test.ts) with a mocked ForgejoClient: completing publish for acme/x@1.0.0 calls publishArtifact exactly once with { owner:'acme', name:'x', version:'1.0.0', zipBuffer } where zipBuffer's canonical SHA-256 equals the recorded contentHash. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Upload the byte-identical canonical zip (do NOT re-pack from committed files — re-packing can change bytes and break hash equality). Uses the UPLOAD token. The redirect endpoint already exists (asr-4it.3) — do not modify it here.

title: Trigger marketplaceSync on version.published and version.yanked
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#sync-job (lines 263-266: triggers are version.published and version.yanked); specs/submission-package.md#composition

Context:
The sync job is reactive: it must run right after a version is published or yanked so the marketplace tracks the registry. This task wires runMarketplaceSync into the two code paths that emit those audit events — the publish orchestrator and the yank endpoint — passing the affected skill name.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/nodes/publish.ts (or the publish orchestrator from asr-6hl.3 — invoke runMarketplaceSync after version.published)
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.ts (the yank handler from asr-sm0.3 — invoke runMarketplaceSync after version.yanked)

Change:
After the publish path emits version.published (asr-6hl.3), call runMarketplaceSync(skillName, deps) with the marketplace-bound client; do the same after the yank path emits version.yanked (asr-sm0.3). The call is fire-and-forwarded into the job runner (await but swallow non-fatal so a sync failure does not roll back the publish/yank — the failure path already audits + pages). Pass the skill name from the merged manifest / yanked version row.

Acceptance:
vitest: a publish test (mocked runMarketplaceSync) asserts runMarketplaceSync is invoked exactly once with the published skill's name after version.published; a yank test asserts the same after version.yanked. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
A marketplace sync failure must NOT fail/rollback the publish or yank (idempotency: the cron rescan can recover). Do not implement the sync internals here (asr-3d8 earlier tasks own them). Do not emit version.published/version.yanked here — those already exist (asr-6hl.3 / asr-sm0.3).

title: Add syncMarketplaceRepo PR-open and auto-merge
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#sync-job (lines 261-273); specs/git-integration.md#branch-strategy (PR + auto-merge)

Context:
This is the I/O half of the sync job: read the current registry index, generate the marketplace file set, and land it on the marketplace repo through a PR that auto-merges when CI is green — using the same merge-bot identity, never a direct push to main.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.ts (extend with syncMarketplaceRepo)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add syncMarketplaceRepo(deps: { client: ForgejoClient; readPublishedSkills: () => Promise<SkillRow[]> }): Promise<{ prNumber: number; merged: boolean }>. Steps: load published skills via readPublishedSkills (the asr-4it.1 read repo), call buildMarketplaceFiles, then client.openSubmissionPR-style flow to push files to a branch `marketplace-sync/<ulid>` and open a PR with the merge-bot identity, poll mergeable, and client.mergePR when mergeable; return { prNumber, merged }. Reuse openSubmissionPR (asr-she.3) and mergePR (asr-she.4) on the marketplace-bound client from marketplaceForgejoFromEnv.

Acceptance:
vitest with a mocked ForgejoClient (openSubmissionPR/mergePR stubbed) and a stub readPublishedSkills returning one skill: syncMarketplaceRepo opens a PR whose file paths include plugins/<name>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json and a marketplace.json, then calls mergePR and returns { merged: true }. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
No direct write to marketplace main — always PR + merge bot (Hard Rule). Failure handling and rate-limited paging are the next task; here, let errors propagate. Do not regenerate registry.json here (separate concern).

title: Add buildMarketplaceFiles manifest and per-plugin generator
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#repo-structure (lines 237-253); specs/types.md#marketplace-manifest (lines 225-241)

Context:
On every publish/yank the marketplace repo must be regenerated from the registry index. This task is the pure transform: given the registry's published skills, produce the marketplace.json (MarketplaceManifest) plus the per-plugin file set, with no I/O so it is trivially unit-testable. The sync job (asr-3d8 next task) wires it to Forgejo.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.ts (new — buildMarketplaceFiles only)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add buildMarketplaceFiles(skills: Array<{ name: string; version: string; description: string; kind: SkillKind; skillMd: string }>): { manifest: MarketplaceManifest; files: Array<{ path: string; content: string }> }. For each skill emit plugins/{name}/.claude-plugin/plugin.json, plugins/{name}/.codex-plugin/plugin.json (each {name,version,description}), and plugins/{name}/skills/{name}/SKILL.md (the skillMd). Build MarketplaceManifest { name: 'skill-marketplace', version: '1', plugins: MarketplacePlugin[] } where each plugin = { name, version, description, path: `plugins/${name}`, kind }. Import MarketplaceManifest/MarketplacePlugin/SkillKind from @asr/core (asr-9qp).

Acceptance:
vitest (marketplaceSync.test.ts): buildMarketplaceFiles with two skills returns a manifest.plugins of length 2 with correct path `plugins/<name>` and kind, and a files[] containing both .claude-plugin/plugin.json and .codex-plugin/plugin.json plus skills/<name>/SKILL.md for each skill. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure function — no Forgejo calls, no fs writes, no audit emit. Do not read registry.json here (the caller passes the skill list). Reuse the canonical MarketplaceManifest from @asr/core; never redefine it locally.

title: Add marketplace-repo env vars and marketplaceForgejoFromEnv factory
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#marketplace-distribution (lines 233-273); specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (jobs/marketplaceSync.ts, line 60); specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient

Context:
The marketplaceSync job writes to a SEPARATE Forgejo repo (the skill-marketplace) from the skills-registry, so it needs its own owner/repo coordinates and a ForgejoClient bound to them. The existing forgejoFromEnv (asr-6hl.1) only constructs a client for the registry repo. This task adds the marketplace coordinates and a dedicated factory the sync job will use.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/env.ts (extend the zod schema from asr-6ax.2)
- packages/submission/src/forgejo/index.ts (add marketplaceForgejoFromEnv alongside forgejoFromEnv from asr-6hl.1)

Change:
Add two optional-with-defaults env vars to the zod schema: FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_OWNER (required) and FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_REPO (default 'skill-marketplace'). Add marketplaceForgejoFromEnv(): ForgejoClient that constructs a ForgejoClient (from @asr/core/forgejo) reusing FORGEJO_URL, FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN and FORGEJO_MERGE_TOKEN but with owner=env.FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_OWNER and repo=env.FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_REPO. Export it from the forgejo barrel.

Acceptance:
vitest: with FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_OWNER set and FORGEJO_MARKETPLACE_REPO unset, env parse succeeds and repo defaults to 'skill-marketplace'; marketplaceForgejoFromEnv() returns a ForgejoClient whose cfg.owner/cfg.repo equal the marketplace coordinates (not the registry repo). `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not change forgejoFromEnv (registry client) — add a parallel factory. No PR/commit logic here; this is config + construction only. The marketplace repo must NOT be the same repo as FORGEJO_REPO (skills-registry).

title: Add submission_status MCP tool with cross-principal not-found scoping
description:
Parent story: asr-3po
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (submission_status, line 37); specs/mcp.md#error-envelope (resource_not_found -32003, line 63)

Context:
A caller polls the detailed workflow state of one of their submissions by id. The security-critical behavior: a submission owned by a different principal must be indistinguishable from a non-existent one — both return resource_not_found (-32003), never insufficient_permissions, so a caller cannot probe for other principals' submission ids.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/submissionStatus.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (register inside createMcpServer)

Change:
export `registerSubmissionStatus(server, db)` -> `server.tool('submission_status', { description:'Get the detailed status of one of your own submissions.', inputSchema:{ submissionId: z.string() } }, handler)`. Handler: `requireToolRole(principal,'Submitter')` (asr-x16.2); `getSubmissionById(db, submissionId)` (asr-igs.2); if the row is undefined OR `row.submitted_by !== principal.sub` -> `throw new McpToolError(MCP_ERROR.resource_not_found, 'resource_not_found')` (asr-x16.1, -32003) — same error for missing and not-owned; otherwise return `rowToSubmission(row)` (full Submission with the SubmissionStatus union) as the tool result. Register inside createMcpServer (server.ts).

Acceptance:
vitest integration: with a submission owned by principalA, calling submission_status as principalA returns the submission with its status.phase; calling submission_status for that same id as principalB throws code -32003 (resource_not_found); calling it for a wholly unknown id as principalA also throws -32003; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Not-owned and not-found MUST collapse to the same -32003 (no -32001 for not-owned — that would leak existence). Reuse getSubmissionById + rowToSubmission (asr-igs.2); do not re-map rows. List is the sibling submissions_mine (asr-3po.2). Rate limiting/telemetry is asr-3f8.

title: Add submissions_mine MCP tool (own-scoped list)
description:
Parent story: asr-3po
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (submissions_mine, line 36; read-only note line 27); specs/mcp.md#authentication (principal binding, lines 18-19)

Context:
Agents need to see the status of submissions they have made without the web UI. submissions_mine is a Submitter-role read tool that returns the caller's OWN submissions + statuses, scoped to the authenticated principal so one caller can never enumerate another's submissions.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/submissionsMine.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (register inside createMcpServer)

Change:
export `registerSubmissionsMine(server, db)` calling `server.tool('submissions_mine', { description:'List your own submissions and their workflow status.', inputSchema:{ limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(50) } }, handler)`. Handler: `requireToolRole(principal,'Submitter')` (asr-x16.2; lacking role -> -32001); then `listSubmissionsBySubmitter(db, principal.sub, limit)` (asr-3po.1) and return `rows.map(rowToSubmission)` (asr-igs.2) — id, status (full SubmissionStatus union), manifest.name, classification, createdAt — as the tool result content. The principal comes from the session-bound auth gate (asr-xcs.2). Register inside createMcpServer (server.ts).

Acceptance:
vitest integration (temp sqlite seeded with two submissions for principalA and one for principalB): calling submissions_mine as principalA returns a result containing exactly the two principalA submissions and never principalB's; calling it as a principal lacking the Submitter role throws code -32001; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Always scope by principal.sub from the session — never accept a submitter id as input (that would let a caller read others' submissions). Reuse listSubmissionsBySubmitter (asr-3po.1) + rowToSubmission (asr-igs.2); do not query SQLite directly. Detail-by-id is the sibling submission_status (asr-3po.3). Rate limiting/telemetry is asr-3f8.

title: Add list-by-submitter query to the submissions read repository
description:
Parent story: asr-3po
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (submissions_mine, line 36); specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (db/repositories/submissions.ts, line 52); reuses SubmissionRow/rowToSubmission from asr-igs.2

Context:
The submissions_mine MCP tool (asr-3po.2) must list only the calling principal's own submissions, but no list-by-owner read exists — asr-igs.2 added getSubmissionById/rowToSubmission and asr-igs.4 added GET /:id only ("No list endpoint here"). This adds the own-scoped repository query so the tool never hand-writes SQL and reuses the existing row->aggregate mapping.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.ts (extend; keep getSubmissionById/rowToSubmission/insertSubmission)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `export function listSubmissionsBySubmitter(db: Database, submittedBy: string, limit = 50): SubmissionRow[]` running `SELECT * FROM submissions WHERE submitted_by = ? ORDER BY submitted_at DESC LIMIT ?` and returning the rows. Callers map each row through the existing `rowToSubmission` (asr-igs.2). Do not add a new mapping path — reuse SubmissionRow + rowToSubmission.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/submissions.test.ts: after runMigrations + insertSubmission of two submissions with submitted_by='userA' and one with 'userB', listSubmissionsBySubmitter(db,'userA') returns exactly the two userA rows newest-first and never the userB row; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not change the 0001 schema (asr-7jd.3 owns submissions/submitted_by). No HTTP and no MCP wiring here (that is asr-3po.2). No cross-principal access — this query is always filtered by submitted_by.

title: Refuse installing a yanked version
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands ("install ... refuses yanked"); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload (X-ASR-Yanked)

Context:
A yanked version is typically pulled for a CVE or policy violation, so a fresh install of it must be blocked (existing installs may still re-verify, but a new install must abort). resolveDownload (K8) already surfaces the yanked flag; this enforces refusal in the orchestrator.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/install.ts (enforce refusal)
- packages/cli/src/install.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In installSkill, after resolving the target version: if the chosen version is yanked (detail.versions entry .yanked OR resolveDownload().yanked), throw/exit before downloading with a clear message that includes the version and yankReason. No --force escape hatch in this task.

Acceptance:
vitest (install.test.ts): installSkill('owner/skill@1.1.0') where 1.1.0 is yanked rejects (non-zero) with a message naming the yanked version, and no files are written and no lock entry is created.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add a --force override (a future task if needed). Do not block re-verification/download of an already-installed pinned version used by update.
acceptance_criteria: installing a yanked version aborts non-zero with no files or lock entry

title: Add install orchestrator and asr install command
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets; specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr install <owner/skill>[@version], --global, --agent)

Context:
This is the end-to-end install: pick the version, resolve the package URL, verify the hash, unpack into each detected agent dir, and record the lock entry. It composes the K5/K6/K7/K8/K9 building blocks behind one command.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/install.ts (new — orchestration)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (register the `install <slug>` command, segregated from legacy `add`)
- packages/cli/src/install.test.ts (new)

Change:
install.ts: `installSkill(slug, opts: { version?; global?; agent?: 'claude'|'codex'|'both' })`: parse owner/name(@version); getSkillDetail -> choose requested version or detail.latestVersion; resolveDownload(owner,name,version) -> { url, yanked }; downloadAndVerify(url, versionContentHash); for each detectAgents(opts) target extractZip into agentSkillDir(agent,name,{global}); recordInstall(...{ contentHash, sourceUrl: url }). index.ts: add commander `install <slug>` with `--global`, `--agent <claude|codex|both>`, and `@version` parsed from the slug, calling installSkill.

Acceptance:
vitest (install.test.ts) with registry-client, downloadAndVerify, and extractZip stubbed and a temp cwd: installSkill('owner/skill') writes the skill files under the detected scope and records an entry in .agent/asr.lock.json with the resolved version and contentHash.

Risks / out of scope:
Yanked-refusal is K11 — only read the flag here. Update/diff is K14. Do not delete the legacy `add` command in this task (a later cleanup removes it).
acceptance_criteria: installSkill writes files under detected scope and records asr.lock.json entry

title: Record contentHash and source URL in install lockfile
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets (.agent/asr.lock.json: version, content-hash, install timestamp, source registry URL)

Context:
asr update/list and re-verification need each install's content hash and originating registry URL persisted. Today InstalledSkill (packages/core/src/types.ts:18-25) and recordInstall (packages/cli/src/lockfile.ts:26-50) store only source/version/commit — no contentHash and no explicit registry URL.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts:18-25 (add `contentHash?: string` and `sourceUrl?: string` to InstalledSkill)
- packages/cli/src/lockfile.ts:26-50 (recordInstall: accept and persist contentHash + sourceUrl)
- packages/cli/src/lockfile.test.ts (new)

Change:
Append `contentHash?: string;` and `sourceUrl?: string;` to the InstalledSkill interface (do not reorder existing fields). Widen recordInstall to take a trailing options object `{ contentHash?; sourceUrl? }` and write those into lock.skills[name]. Keep legacy positional callers compiling (new fields optional, additive).

Acceptance:
