vitest (lockfile.test.ts) writing to a temp dir: recordInstall(..., { contentHash: 'sha256:abc', sourceUrl: 'https://reg/...' }) produces a .agent/asr.lock.json whose entry has contentHash 'sha256:abc' and that sourceUrl.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not bump LockFile.version (still 1) — fields are additive/optional. Do not break existing recordInstall call sites in index.ts.
acceptance_criteria: recordInstall persists contentHash and sourceUrl into asr.lock.json entry

title: Add resolveDownload to registry client (302 + yank header)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload (302 Location + X-ASR-Yanked)

Context:
Install needs the concrete Forgejo generic-package URL for a version and must know whether that version is yanked. The registry returns this as a 302 whose Location points at the package, plus an X-ASR-Yanked header. This extends the K1 read client with that resolution step.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/registry-client.ts (extend: add resolveDownload)
- packages/cli/src/registry-client.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Export `resolveDownload(owner, name, version, opts?: { token? }): Promise<{ url: string; yanked: boolean }>` issuing GET /api/v1/skills/:owner/:name/v/:version/download with `redirect: 'manual'`; read the `Location` response header as url and `X-ASR-Yanked` === 'true' as yanked; throw if a 3xx has no Location or the status is non-3xx.

Acceptance:
vitest (registry-client.test.ts) with fetch mocked to a 302 carrying Location and `X-ASR-Yanked: true`: resolveDownload('o','n','1.0.0') resolves to { url: '<location>', yanked: true }.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not fetch the artifact body here (K6 owns download+verify). Do not auto-follow the redirect — read Location manually so the package URL is exposed to the caller.
acceptance_criteria: mocked 302 with Location and X-ASR-Yanked true returns url and yanked true

title: Add yauzl safe zip extraction (extract.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets; project rule "Zip extraction uses yauzl only — never unzipper"

Context:
After hash verification the artifact zip must be unpacked into the agent skill dir. Per the project hard rule the CLI must extract with yauzl and guard against zip-slip path traversal so a crafted entry cannot escape the target directory.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/extract.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/extract.test.ts (new)
- packages/cli/package.json (add deps: yauzl ^3, @types/yauzl ^2)

Change:
Export `extractZip(zip: Buffer, destDir: string): Promise<string[]>`: open with yauzl.fromBuffer (lazyEntries); for each entry resolve join(destDir, entry.fileName), throw on any entry whose resolved path escapes destDir or is absolute or contains `..`; create directories and write files; return the list of written relative paths.

Acceptance:
vitest (extract.test.ts): extractZip of an in-test-built zip (two files) writes both under destDir and returns their paths; a zip containing an entry `../evil.txt` causes the promise to reject with no file written outside destDir.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add unzipper or adm-zip. No symlink-entry support. Do not buffer entire large entries unnecessarily — stream entry read streams to disk.
acceptance_criteria: extracts entries under destDir and rejects a ../ traversal entry

title: Add download-and-verify with canonical SHA-256 (download.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets (post-install hash check); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload; specs/versioning.md (canonical content hash)

Context:
A published skill artifact must be verified against the registry's contentHash before extraction, or a tampered/MITM'd zip could install malicious code. This module fetches the artifact and enforces the hash gate, emitting the documented version.hash.mismatch on failure.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/download.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/download.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export `class HashMismatchError extends Error { code = 'version.hash.mismatch' }`. Export `downloadAndVerify(url: string, expectedHash: string, opts?: { token? }): Promise<Buffer>`: fetch(url) following redirects, read arrayBuffer -> Buffer, compute `sha256:` + crypto.createHash('sha256').update(buf).digest('hex'), compare case-insensitively to expectedHash; on mismatch throw HashMismatchError with both hashes in the message; else return the buffer.

Acceptance:
vitest (download.test.ts) with fetch mocked to a known body: downloadAndVerify(url, '<wrong-hash>') rejects with an error whose `.code === 'version.hash.mismatch'`; with the correct `sha256:` hash it resolves to the buffer.

Risks / out of scope:
No unzip here (K7 owns extraction). No yank handling (K8/K10/K11). Match the canonical hash format used server-side (`sha256:<hex>`); do not invent a different prefix.
acceptance_criteria: wrong hash rejects with code version.hash.mismatch; correct hash resolves buffer

title: Add agent detection and skill target paths (agents.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets (detection order + path table)

Context:
Install must place skills in the right per-agent directory. The legacy config.ts (packages/cli/src/config.ts:32-53) only knows cursor/claude/project and never targets Codex. The spec defines a Claude/Codex/both model with a precise detection order, which this module owns so install/list/remove share one source of truth.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/agents.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/agents.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export `type AgentTarget = 'claude' | 'codex'`. `detectAgents(opts: { explicit?: 'claude'|'codex'|'both'; cwd?: string }): AgentTarget[]` implementing detection order: explicit flag wins (both -> ['claude','codex']); else presence of .claude/ or .codex/ in cwd selects those; else default ['claude','codex']. `agentSkillDir(agent: AgentTarget, name: string, opts: { global?: boolean }): string` -> project: `<cwd>/.claude/skills/<name>` or `.codex/skills/<name>`; global: `<home>/.claude/skills/<name>` or `<home>/.codex/skills/<name>`.

Acceptance:
vitest (agents.test.ts) using a temp cwd: detectAgents({}) with both .claude and .codex present returns ['claude','codex']; detectAgents({explicit:'codex'}) returns ['codex']; agentSkillDir('claude','x',{global:false}) ends with `.claude/skills/x`.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not delete config.ts getTargetDir yet (legacy add/remove still use it until their migration tasks). No filesystem writes here — path computation + detection only.
acceptance_criteria: detectAgents and agentSkillDir return per-spec detection order and paths

title: Add registry read client to @asr/cli (search, detail, versions)
description:
Parent story: asr-8ce
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#endpoints (GET /skills, /skills/:owner/:name, /skills/:owner/:name/versions); specs/cli-integration.md#commands

Context:
The browse commands (asr search/info/versions) and the later install command all read the Forgejo-backed Registry API at base /api/v1. Today index.ts has only an inline fetchRegistry() (packages/cli/src/index.ts:28-40) that targets the legacy /api/skills path, not the spec's /api/v1. This introduces a typed, reusable read client so commands stop hand-rolling fetch.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/registry-client.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/registry-client.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export `registryFetch(path, { token? })` resolving the base URL from process.env.ASR_URL || getConfig().registry (config.ts:19), attaching `Authorization: Bearer` only when a token is present and requesting `Accept: application/json`. Build on it: `searchSkills(q, opts?: { tag?; kind?; limit?; cursor? }): Promise<{ items: SkillSummary[]; nextCursor?: string }>` -> GET /api/v1/skills?q=...; `getSkillDetail(owner, name): Promise<SkillDetail>` -> GET /api/v1/skills/:owner/:name; `listVersions(owner, name): Promise<SkillVersion[]>` -> GET /api/v1/skills/:owner/:name/versions. Import SkillSummary/SkillDetail/SkillVersion from '@asr/core' (added by asr-9qp). Throw on non-2xx with the status and response body.

Acceptance:
vitest (registry-client.test.ts) with global.fetch mocked: searchSkills('foo') issues a GET to `${base}/api/v1/skills?q=foo` and returns the parsed `items` array; a 404 from getSkillDetail rejects with an error carrying the status code.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not modify or delete the legacy fetchRegistry()/browse command yet (K2 migrates `search`). No download/redirect logic here (K8 adds it). No write/publish endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: searchSkills(foo) GETs /api/v1/skills?q=foo and returns parsed items array; 404 detail rejects with status

title: Add vitest test runner to @asr/cli package
description:
Parent story: asr-8ce
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     packages/core/package.json (vitest ^4.0.18 precedent); root package.json#scripts.test

Context:
Every read- and install-side CLI task in the asr-8ce/asr-5wr slice ships a *.test.ts file, but @asr/cli currently has no test runner: packages/cli/package.json has no "test" script and no vitest devDependency (only @asr/core does). This lays the test foundation so all later CLI acceptance checks are actually runnable under `pnpm test`.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/package.json (add "test": "vitest run" to scripts; add "vitest": "^4.0.18" to devDependencies, matching @asr/core)
- packages/cli/vitest.config.ts (new; minimal defineConfig with test.environment 'node')
- package.json (root: change scripts.test from `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` to also run `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test`)

Change:
Add vitest to @asr/cli with a node-environment config and chain the CLI test run into the root `pnpm test` so CI runs both packages. Add packages/cli/src/sanity.test.ts (expect(true).toBe(true)) only if vitest errors on zero test files; otherwise omit it.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` exits 0 (vitest runs, reporting either the sanity test or "no test files"), and `pnpm test` from the repo root invokes both @asr/core and @asr/cli vitest runs.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add coverage thresholds or modify @asr/core's config. Do not introduce jest. Keep ESM (package is "type":"module").
acceptance_criteria: pnpm --filter @asr/cli test exits 0 and root pnpm test runs CLI tests too

title: Wire asr publish command with --watch
description:
Parent story: asr-rtz
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (lines 36-37); specs/api.md#post-submissions (lines 62-80)

Context:
asr publish ties validation, classification, packing, and upload together: it fails fast on an invalid manifest before any network call, reports the predicted approval path, uploads, and (with --watch) streams status until terminal.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/commands/publish.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (register publish; the legacy hidden publish at lines 514-572 is replaced)

Change:
Add commands/publish.ts registering asr publish [dir] [--watch]: read manifest.yaml from the dir and validate required fields (name, semver version) locally, exiting non-zero before any HTTP if invalid; run classifySkill and print the predicted path; packSkillDir then postSubmission; print { id, status }. With --watch, poll GET /submissions/:id every few seconds until a terminal phase and print transitions. Register in index.ts.

Acceptance:
vitest against a stub submission API: asr publish ./fixtures/md-skill returns 201 and prints an object containing id and status; an invalid manifest (missing version) exits non-zero with no HTTP request made; --watch on a code skill prints status transitions ending at a terminal phase.

Risks / out of scope:
Local manifest validation is a minimal required-field/semver check; full @asr/core SkillManifest schema validation (asr-4bc) is a later enhancement noted here. Depends on api.ts, pack.ts, classify.ts.

title: Add content classifier (src/publish/classify.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-rtz
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#classification-whitelist-approach (lines 84-104); DESIGN.md#content-classification (lines 196-201)

Context:
Before upload the CLI classifies the skill so the user knows whether it will auto-approve (content-only) or require approval (code-containing). The classifier mirrors the server-side whitelist so the client prediction matches.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/publish/classify.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/classify.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add classify.ts with CONTENT_ONLY_EXTENSIONS = { .md .txt .rst .png .jpg .jpeg .gif .svg .webp .yaml .yml .json } and classifySkill(files: string[]): "md-only" | "code-containing" returning md-only only when every file has a whitelisted extension (files with no/unknown extension force code-containing). Lowercase the extension before lookup.

Acceptance:
vitest: classifySkill(["SKILL.md","img/logo.png"]) === "md-only"; classifySkill(["SKILL.md","scripts/run.py"]) === "code-containing"; a file with no extension classifies as code-containing.

Risks / out of scope:
Whitelist must stay byte-identical to specs/security.md. Do not perform SVG sanitization here (server concern). No HTTP calls.

title: Add skill-directory zip packer (src/publish/pack.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-rtz
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr publish line 36); specs/api.md#post-submissions (multipart file: skill.zip)

Context:
asr publish uploads the skill directory as a zip. The packer turns a local directory into an in-memory zip the API client can upload, deterministically and without native deps.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/publish/pack.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/pack.test.ts (new)
- packages/cli/package.json (add a pure-JS zip dependency, e.g. fflate)

Change:
Add pack.ts exporting packSkillDir(dir): Promise<{ buffer: Buffer; files: string[] }>. Recursively read the directory (skipping node_modules and .git), build a zip with fflate (pure JS, bundles cleanly), and return the zip bytes plus the relative file list. Add fflate to dependencies.

Acceptance:
vitest: packing a fixture directory containing SKILL.md and scripts/run.py returns a non-empty Buffer and a files array listing both relative paths; unzipping the buffer reproduces the file contents.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure-JS only (fflate), no archiver/native zip. Do not classify content here (separate task). Server-side extraction still uses yauzl (out of scope).

title: Add authed submission API client (src/api.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-rtz
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#post-submissions (lines 62-80); specs/cli-integration.md#token-export-for-mcp; specs/api.md#authentication-authorization (lines 139-160)

Context:
The publish, status, and submissions commands all talk to the Submission API and must attach a bearer token (or skip auth in dev mode). Centralizing the HTTP client keeps auth handling and error decoding in one place.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/api.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/api.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add api.ts exporting apiFetch(path, init) that resolves the base URL via env.ts, and unless isAuthDisabled attaches Authorization: Bearer <getValidAccessToken()>; it parses JSON, and on non-2xx throws an error carrying status and the parsed { error } body (e.g. version_already_exists). Add a multipart helper postSubmission(zipBuffer, filename) that POSTs multipart/form-data field file to /submissions and returns the 201 body { id, status, manifest, contentHash, createdAt }.

Acceptance:
vitest against a stub server: postSubmission returns the parsed 201 body and sends an Authorization header when auth is enabled; with ASR_URL=http://localhost:* no Authorization header is sent; a 409 response surfaces an error exposing body.error.

Risks / out of scope:
Depends on env.ts and the session accessor. Do not build the zip here (separate task); accept a Buffer.

title: Wire asr whoami and asr logout commands
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (lines 19-21), #authentication

Context:
After login users need to confirm who they are and to sign out. whoami reads the cached session and shows identity plus roles; logout clears the cached tokens.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/commands/auth.ts (add registerWhoami, registerLogout)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (register whoami and logout)

Change:
Add asr whoami: read getStoredTokens; if none (or dev-mode), print "Not signed in"; else decode and print identity + roles. Add asr logout: call clearTokens and print "Signed out". Register both in index.ts.

Acceptance:
vitest: after a stubbed login that stored tokens, whoami prints the cached identity and roles; running logout then whoami prints "Not signed in" (tokens cleared).

Risks / out of scope:
Depends on the token store; do not re-implement persistence. No network calls needed for whoami/logout.

title: Wire asr login command
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#authentication (lines 50-57); DESIGN.md#cli-authentication (lines 214-221)

Context:
asr login is the user-facing entry point that ties the device-code flow, token storage, and dev-mode skip together and reports the signed-in identity and roles. This replaces the legacy GitHub token command flow.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/commands/auth.ts (new: registerLogin)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (register the login command; remove the legacy token GitHub path lines 607-710 in a later task)

Change:
Add commands/auth.ts exporting a function that registers asr login on the program: resolve base URL via env.ts; if isAuthDisabled, print that auth is skipped in dev mode and exit 0; otherwise call requestDeviceCode, print the verification URL and user code, call pollForToken, persist via storeTokens, decode identity+roles from the access token claims, and print "Logged in as <user> (roles: ...)". Wire registerLogin into index.ts.

Acceptance:
vitest: against a mock device-code + token endpoint, invoking the login action completes and writes tokens via the store and the printed output contains "Logged in as" with the stub identity and roles; with ASR_URL=http://localhost:9999 the action prints the dev-mode skip message and stores nothing.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not print the raw access token. Removal of the legacy token/config GitHub commands is tracked separately; only add login here.

title: Implement device-code flow client (RFC 8628)
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#device-code-flow-rfc-8628 (lines 46-59)

Context:
asr login authenticates via the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant against Entra ID. This task implements the protocol mechanics (request a device code, poll the token endpoint) independent of the command UX, so it can be unit-tested against a stub token server.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/auth/device-code.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/device-code.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add device-code.ts exporting requestDeviceCode(baseUrl) -> { verificationUri, userCode, deviceCode, interval } and pollForToken(baseUrl, deviceCode, opts) -> StoredTokens. Poll every interval seconds (default 5, override ASR_DEVICE_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS), give up after ASR_DEVICE_POLL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default 900); on an authorization_pending response keep polling, on slow_down double the interval up to 30s, on access_denied/expired_token throw a descriptive error, on success return tokens with computed expiresAt.

Acceptance:
vitest against a stub endpoint that returns authorization_pending then slow_down then a token: pollForToken resolves with the tokens, the effective interval doubled after slow_down (asserted up to 30s cap), and a timeout case rejects with a timeout error.

Risks / out of scope:
No keyring writes here (caller persists via token-store). Use injectable/fake timers in tests so the suite does not actually wait seconds.

title: Implement token store with keyring and 0600 file fallback
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#token-storage (lines 61-69)

Context:
After a successful device-code login the access and refresh tokens must persist across CLI invocations, in the OS keyring where available and otherwise a 0600 file, so whoami/publish/install can reuse the session without re-authenticating.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/auth/token-store.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/token-store.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add token-store.ts exporting an interface StoredTokens { accessToken, refreshToken?, expiresAt: number, account: string } and async functions storeTokens(t), getStoredTokens(): Promise<StoredTokens|null>, clearTokens(). Try to dynamically import keytar (optional) and use it; on import failure or Linux without libsecret, fall back to ~/.config/asr/token.json written with fs mode 0600 (chmod 0o600). Service name "asr". Keytar stays external (not bundled).

Acceptance:
vitest forcing the file-fallback path (keytar import stubbed to throw): storeTokens then getStoredTokens round-trips the object, the token.json file is created with mode 0600, and clearTokens removes it so getStoredTokens returns null.

Risks / out of scope:
keytar must be dynamically imported in a try/catch so the pure-JS bundle still loads on machines without it. Do not implement refresh here (separate task); only persistence.

title: Add dev-mode and API base-URL resolver (src/env.ts)
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#dev-mode (lines 89-91); DESIGN.md#cli-authentication (line 225)

Context:
Auth must be skipped entirely when ASR_URL is a non-HTTPS (localhost) endpoint, and every network command needs one place to resolve the API base URL from ASR_URL or config. Centralizing this avoids each command re-implementing dev-mode detection.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/env.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/env.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add env.ts exporting getApiBaseUrl(): string (reads process.env.ASR_URL, falling back to getConfig().registry, throwing a clear error if neither set) and isAuthDisabled(url: string): boolean returning true when the URL scheme is not https (http://localhost:*, http://127.0.0.1:*, any non-https). Add env.test.ts covering both helpers.

Acceptance:
vitest: isAuthDisabled("http://localhost:3001") === true, isAuthDisabled("https://asr.example.com") === false; getApiBaseUrl() returns the ASR_URL value when set.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure helpers only; no token logic (that is the token-store task). Do not read keyring here.

title: Add vitest test harness to packages/cli
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#authentication (acceptance: integration tests with a stubbed device-code server); packages/cli/package.json (scripts line 11-13)

Context:
Every asr-thq/asr-rtz acceptance criterion is an integration test, but packages/cli has no test runner yet (only @asr/core has vitest). This task adds the shared CLI test harness so subsequent auth and publish tasks can assert behavior against stub servers.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/package.json (add devDependency vitest, add scripts.test)
- packages/cli/vitest.config.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add vitest to devDependencies, add "test": "vitest run" and "test:watch": "vitest" to scripts. Add vitest.config.ts with test.environment node and globals true. Add a smoke.test.ts asserting a trivial truth so the runner is wired. Ensure the root pnpm -r test or pnpm --filter @asr/cli test discovers it.

Acceptance:
pnpm --filter @asr/cli test runs vitest and reports the smoke test passing with exit code 0.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not change the @asr/core test setup. No application code here; harness only. Keep vitest in devDependencies so it never reaches the bundled asr.mjs.

title: Add Forgejo release.yml release workflow
description:
Parent story: asr-bl6
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     DESIGN.md#release-workflow-forgejoworkflowsreleaseyml (lines 110-162)

Context:
Tagging a v* release must automatically build the bundle and publish it plus its checksum and both install scripts as Forgejo Release assets, so the install scripts can fetch them. This is the only release path; no GitHub release action is permitted.

Affected files:
- .forgejo/workflows/release.yml (new)

Change:
Add a Forgejo Actions workflow per DESIGN.md lines 119-160: trigger on push tags v*, runs-on ubuntu-latest, actions/checkout@v4, actions/setup-node@v4 node 22, corepack enable + pnpm install --frozen-lockfile, pnpm build, compute sha256sum asr.mjs > asr.mjs.sha256 in packages/cli/dist, then a step using FORGEJO_TOKEN/FORGEJO_URL/REPO secrets that POSTs to /api/v1/repos/$REPO/releases to create the release and loops curl -F attachment=@file to /releases/$RID/assets for asr.mjs, asr.mjs.sha256, scripts/install.sh, scripts/install.ps1.

Acceptance:
The workflow YAML parses (yq eval or actionlint) and contains the Forgejo /api/v1 create-release POST plus the per-asset upload loop covering all four assets; grep confirms no github.com release action and no /api/v3 path.

Risks / out of scope:
Use the documented curl-against-API approach (no third-party release CLI). Forgejo API is /api/v1 not /api/v3. Do not hardcode secrets; read FORGEJO_TOKEN/FORGEJO_URL/REPO from workflow secrets.

title: Configure tsup single-ESM bundle to dist/asr.mjs
description:
Parent story: asr-bl6
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     DESIGN.md#build-strategy (lines 28-33); packages/cli/package.json (build script lines 11-13, bin.asr line 8)

Context:
The asr CLI ships as a single self-contained ESM file downloaded from Forgejo Releases, with no npm install on end-user machines. The current build script (tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts) does not bundle workspace deps, so @asr/core would be unresolved at runtime, and it emits dist/index.js rather than the dist/asr.mjs that the install scripts and release workflow expect. This task makes the bundle self-contained and renames the artifact.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/tsup.config.ts (new)
- packages/cli/package.json (scripts.build/dev lines 11-13, bin.asr line 8, files array line 6-7)

Change:
Add tsup.config.ts exporting defineConfig with entry { asr: "src/index.ts" }, format ["esm"], target "node20", outDir "dist", outExtension to .mjs, noExternal [/.*/] to inline @asr/core and node_modules, external ["keytar"] so the optional native module is never bundled, banner.js "#!/usr/bin/env node", clean true, dts false. Update package.json build to "tsup", dev to "tsup --watch", set bin.asr to ./dist/asr.mjs, add dist/asr.mjs coverage to files.

Acceptance:
pnpm --filter @asr/cli build emits packages/cli/dist/asr.mjs; node packages/cli/dist/asr.mjs --help exits 0 and prints the command list with no MODULE_NOT_FOUND for @asr/core.

Risks / out of scope:
Keep the bundle pure-JS and cross-OS: keytar (added in the auth story) stays external and dynamically imported. Do not change any command behavior here; packaging only.

title: Commit .publish-record.json and write skill_versions row in publishMdOnly
description:
Parent story: asr-ai5
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#invariant... (lines 5-21); specs/versioning.md#git-history-as-source-of-truth (line 268); specs/workflow.md#pipeline (publish, lines 14-62)

Context:
This closes the publish-record loop: when an md-only submission publishes (asr-6hl.3 orchestrator), the .publish-record.json must be part of the PR (so it lands in the merge commit) and a skill_versions row must be written after merge with the real merge_commit sha. This makes a published version both Git-verifiable and SQLite-queryable.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/publishMdOnly.ts (extend the asr-6hl.3 orchestrator)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/publishMdOnly.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In publishMdOnly, before openSubmissionPR, build the record via buildPublishRecord({ contentHash: submission.contentHash, scanReportId: null, approver: 'system', runId: submission.id }) and append { path: 'skills/'+manifest.author+'/'+manifest.name+'/.publish-record.json', content: serializePublishRecord(record) } to the files array passed to openSubmissionPR (so it is committed on the submit branch). After mergePR returns { sha }, call insertSkillVersion(db, { skill_name: manifest.name, version: manifest.version, content_hash: submission.contentHash, submission_id: submission.id, published_at: new Date().toISOString(), published_by: submission.submittedBy, approved_by: null, pr_number: prNumber, merge_commit: sha, scan_report_id: null, yanked_at: null, yanked_by: null, yank_reason: null }) inside the same step as the status flip.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/publishMdOnly.test.ts: with a mocked ForgejoClient (openSubmissionPR captures its files arg -> {branch,prNumber:1}, mergePR -> {sha:'abc'}, publishArtifact/deleteBranch ok) and temp sqlite holding a seeded md-only submission, after publishMdOnly the captured files include a path ending '/.publish-record.json' whose JSON.parse.contentHash === submission.contentHash, AND getSkillVersion(db, manifest.name, manifest.version) returns a row with merge_commit==='abc'; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Append to the existing files list — do not call the private putFile directly (it is private in ForgejoClient, asr-she.2). Reuse insertSkillVersion (asr-ai5.1) and buildPublishRecord/serializePublishRecord (asr-ai5 builder); do not redefine them. Keep the call idempotent so crash-resume (asr-fc6) can re-run — getSkillVersion-then-insert or INSERT OR IGNORE on the (skill_name,version) PK. The code-path publish (asr-82o) wires the same two effects separately; not in scope here.
acceptance_criteria: vitest integration: with a mocked ForgejoClient and temp sqlite seeded with an md-only submission, publishMdOnly causes openSubmissionPR to receive a .publish-record.json file AND afterwards getSkillVersion(db, name, version) returns a row with merge_commit===mocked sha and content_hash===submission hash; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Add .publish-record.json builder to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-ai5
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#git-history-as-source-of-truth (line 268); specs/versioning.md#invariant... (lines 5-21)

Context:
Every published version writes a .publish-record.json into its merge commit so the version is independently verifiable from Git even if SQLite is wiped (contains canonical hash, scan report id, approver, Flowcraft run id). This is the pure builder that assembles that JSON; the wiring task commits it. Splitting the builder out keeps it unit-testable without Forgejo or a DB.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/publishRecord.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/publishRecord.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export interface PublishRecord { schema: 1; contentHash: string; scanReportId: string | null; approver: string; runId: string; publishedAt: string }` and `export function buildPublishRecord(input: { contentHash: string; scanReportId: string | null; approver: string; runId: string; publishedAt?: string }): PublishRecord` returning { schema: 1, contentHash: input.contentHash, scanReportId: input.scanReportId, approver: input.approver, runId: input.runId, publishedAt: input.publishedAt ?? new Date().toISOString() }. Add `export function serializePublishRecord(r: PublishRecord): Buffer` returning Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(r, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf8') for committing as file content.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/publishRecord.test.ts: buildPublishRecord({ contentHash:'sha256:ab', scanReportId:null, approver:'system', runId:'run_1', publishedAt:'2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' }) deep-equals { schema:1, contentHash:'sha256:ab', scanReportId:null, approver:'system', runId:'run_1', publishedAt:'2026-01-01T00:00:00Z' }; serializePublishRecord(record) parses back via JSON.parse to the same object; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure builder only — no Forgejo commit, no DB write, no skill_versions row (those are the wiring task and asr-ai5.1). The merge-commit sha is NOT in the record (it would be circular — the record lives inside that commit). approver is 'system' for md-only auto-approve; the compliance sub for the code path.
acceptance_criteria: vitest publishRecord.test.ts: buildPublishRecord(input) returns { schema:1, contentHash, scanReportId, approver, runId, publishedAt } with exactly those keys and the passed values; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Add skill_versions insert/read repository to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-ai5
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#storage (skill_versions table, lines 140-160); specs/versioning.md#invariant... (lines 5-21)

Context:
On a successful publish the system must persist a queryable skill_versions row so a version is discoverable without replaying Git. The 0005 migration (asr-efq.1) creates the table but no write/read helpers; this adds the thin repository the publish path (asr-ai5 wiring) and later yank/latest tasks reuse. Keeping SQL here means the workflow never touches raw queries.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skillVersions.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skillVersions.test.ts (new)

Change:
Import the Database type from ../index.js. Add `export interface SkillVersionRow { skill_name: string; version: string; content_hash: string; submission_id: string; published_at: string; published_by: string; approved_by: string | null; pr_number: number; merge_commit: string; scan_report_id: string | null; yanked_at: string | null; yanked_by: string | null; yank_reason: string | null }`. Add `export function insertSkillVersion(db: Database, row: SkillVersionRow): void` (INSERT INTO skill_versions with all columns). Add `export function getSkillVersion(db: Database, skillName: string, version: string): SkillVersionRow | undefined` (SELECT * FROM skill_versions WHERE skill_name=? AND version=?). Do not start a transaction (callers compose).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skillVersions.test.ts: after runMigrations(db) + inserting a submissions row, insertSkillVersion(db, sampleRow) then getSkillVersion(db, 'acme/x'.split... -> skill_name,'1.0.0') deep-equals sampleRow (merge_commit, content_hash, yanked_at:null round-trip); getSkillVersion for an unknown (name,version) is undefined; a duplicate (skill_name,version) insert throws (PK); pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Read/write of one row only — resolveLatestVersion/listVersions (asr-nh4) and markVersionYanked (asr-sm0) extend this same file in their own tasks; do not add them here. Depends on the 0005 migration (asr-efq.1) for the table and 0001 submissions for the FK. No HTTP, no Git, no .publish-record.json here.
acceptance_criteria: vitest skillVersions.test.ts: after runMigrations + a submissions row, insertSkillVersion(db,row) then getSkillVersion(db,'acme/x'... ) round-trips the row including merge_commit and content_hash; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Wire content-hash gate into POST /submissions handler
description:
Parent story: asr-gh1
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash (lines 68-70); specs/api.md (409 + content_blocked, lines 78-79, 200, 209)

Context:
On submission, rejected or yanked content must not re-enter under a new version, and identical content already submitted must not duplicate. This adds the content-hash gate to the POST /submissions handler between extraction and persistence, using the canonical hash and the lookup repository.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.ts (extend the POST handler from asr-igs.3)
- packages/submission/test/integration/submissions.contentHash.test.ts (new)

Change:
In the POST /submissions handler, after the zip is extracted/classified and before inserting the submission, compute `const hash = canonicalHash(files)` (canonicalHash from @asr/core, asr-2fb.2). Call getBlockedHash(db, hash); if present, return apiError(c, 409, 'content_blocked', { details: { source: row.source, reason: row.reason } }). Else call findSubmissionIdByContentHash(db, hash); if present, return apiError(c, 409, 'content_blocked', { details: { reason: 'duplicate_content', existingSubmissionId } }). Otherwise persist the submission with content_hash = hash and continue the existing flow.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/test/integration/submissions.contentHash.test.ts (temp SQLite): POSTing content whose canonical hash is already a blocked_hashes row returns 409 with body.error 'content_blocked' and body.details.source set; POSTing content whose hash matches an existing submission returns 409 'content_blocked' with body.details.reason 'duplicate_content'; POSTing novel content returns the normal created response and writes a submissions row whose content_hash equals canonicalHash(files); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Extends the asr-igs.3 handler at the post-extraction/pre-insert seam — do not duplicate the handler or the apiError helper (asr-igs.1). The blocked-hash check runs before the dedup check so an explicitly blocked hash reports its source. Concurrent-submission soft lock (pending_versions) and the semver downgrade rule are separate stories (asr-nh4 / asr-iew.2 wiring), not in scope here.

title: Add content-hash dedup and blocked-hash lookup repository to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-gh1
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash (lines 68-72); specs/versioning.md#re-publish-after-yank (lines 207-209)

Context:
The submission gate must answer two questions cheaply: has this exact content been submitted before (dedup, including rejected submissions), and is this content permanently blocked. These read helpers wrap the submissions.content_hash index (0001) and the blocked_hashes table (0005) so the handler stays thin.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/versions.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/versions.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function findSubmissionIdByContentHash(db: Database, contentHash: string): string | undefined` (SELECT id FROM submissions WHERE content_hash = ? LIMIT 1; return the id or undefined). Add `export interface BlockedHashRow { content_hash: string; skill_name: string; version: string; blocked_at: string; blocked_by: string; reason: string; source: 'rejected'|'yanked'|'incident' }` and `export function getBlockedHash(db: Database, contentHash: string): BlockedHashRow | undefined` (SELECT * FROM blocked_hashes WHERE content_hash = ?). Import the Database type from ../index.js.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/versions.test.ts: after runMigrations and inserting a submissions row with content_hash 'abc', findSubmissionIdByContentHash(db,'abc') returns that id and findSubmissionIdByContentHash(db,'zzz') is undefined; after inserting a blocked_hashes row (source 'rejected'), getBlockedHash returns the row with source 'rejected' and getBlockedHash for an unknown hash is undefined; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Read-only helpers — insertion into blocked_hashes happens in the reject/yank stories, not here. Depends on the 0005 migration (asr-efq.1) for blocked_hashes and on 0001 submissions for the dedup query. No HTTP wiring in this task.

title: Add 0005 versioning migration (skill_versions, version_diffs, blocked_hashes)
description:
Parent story: asr-efq
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#storage (lines 137-182); migration framework asr-7jd.2 (runner), asr-7jd.3 (0001 submissions), asr-st5.4 (0002 scan_results)

Context:
The version history, per-submission diffs, and the permanent block list need durable tables that mirror Git history for cheap queries. These back publish (asr-ai5), dedup/block (asr-gh1), update routing (asr-ak7), yank (asr-sm0), and latest resolution (asr-nh4). Numbering continues the existing sequence (0001 submissions, 0002 scan_results, 0003 workflow_locks, 0004 audit_events), so this is 0005.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0005_versioning.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (import + push the 0005 entry, ascending id order)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0005_versioning.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export Migration `{ id: 5, name: 'versioning', up }` whose up runs three CREATE TABLEs verbatim per specs/versioning.md#storage: skill_versions (PK (skill_name,version); content_hash, submission_id REFERENCES submissions(id), published_at, published_by, approved_by, pr_number, merge_commit, scan_report_id REFERENCES scan_results(id), yanked_at, yanked_by, yank_reason); version_diffs (PK submission_id REFERENCES submissions(id); from_version, to_version, diff_json, risk CHECK(risk IN ('low','medium','high')), computed_at); blocked_hashes (PK content_hash; skill_name, version, blocked_at, blocked_by, reason, source CHECK(source IN ('rejected','yanked','incident'))). Then idx_versions_hash ON skill_versions(content_hash), idx_versions_pub ON skill_versions(published_at), and partial idx_versions_yanked ON skill_versions(skill_name) WHERE yanked_at IS NULL. Push the entry after 0004 in migrations/index.ts.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0005_versioning.test.ts: after runMigrations(db), pragma table_info shows the documented columns for all three tables and the three indexes appear in sqlite_master; a duplicate (skill_name,version) insert into skill_versions throws; an insert into version_diffs with risk='bogus' throws; an insert into blocked_hashes with source='bogus' throws; a second runMigrations(db) is a no-op; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Numbering is fixed at 0005 — do not renumber existing migrations. FK targets submissions (0001) and scan_results (0002) must already be registered. No repository/query helpers in this task (those are asr-gh1.1 and the publish/yank stories). Up-only; no down migration.

title: Add no-downgrade version-upgrade validator to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-iew
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#version-rules (lines 40-50 — "No downgrades", pre-release ordered properly)

Context:
The submission API must reject a new submission whose version is malformed or not strictly greater than the currently published version (no downgrades, no re-using the same number). This validator centralises that rule so the POST /submissions handler and the update-flow workflow share one decision.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/version.ts (extend: add validateVersionUpgrade)
- packages/core/src/version.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `export type VersionUpgradeError = 'invalid_format' | 'not_greater'` and `export function validateVersionUpgrade(next: string, current: string | null): { ok: true } | { ok: false; error: VersionUpgradeError }`. Rules: if !isValidVersion(next) → {ok:false,error:'invalid_format'}; if current is null (first publish) → {ok:true}; if !gtVersion(next, current) → {ok:false,error:'not_greater'} (covers equal and lower, including pre-release ordering via gtVersion); else {ok:true}.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/version.test.ts: validateVersionUpgrade('1.1.0','1.0.0').ok === true; validateVersionUpgrade('1.0.0','1.0.0') deep-equals {ok:false,error:'not_greater'}; validateVersionUpgrade('0.9.0','1.0.0') → {ok:false,error:'not_greater'}; validateVersionUpgrade('1.0.0', null).ok === true; validateVersionUpgrade('1.2','1.0.0') → {ok:false,error:'invalid_format'}; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure function — no DB lookup of the current version (the caller resolves currentPublished from skill_versions and passes it). Duplicate-name@version and blocked-hash enforcement live in their own stories against storage, not here.

title: Add pinned semver dependency and version wrappers to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-iew
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#version-format (lines 23-38)

Context:
Strict semver 2.0 comparison must be done by exactly one pinned library across the workspace so version ordering is consistent everywhere (submission API, CLI, registry). This task adds that dependency to @asr/core and exposes thin, typed wrappers the rest of the system imports instead of reaching for semver directly.

Affected files:
- packages/core/package.json (add "semver":"^7.6.0" to dependencies; "@types/semver":"^7.5.0" to devDependencies)
- packages/core/src/version.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/version.test.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (add `export * from './version.js';`)

Change:
Add `export function isValidVersion(v: string): boolean` (semver.valid(v) !== null, strict — no loose/coerce); `export function compareVersions(a: string, b: string): -1|0|1` (semver.compare); `export function gtVersion(a: string, b: string): boolean` (semver.gt — true iff a strictly greater than b); `export function rsortVersions(vs: string[]): string[]` (semver.rsort over a copy, descending). Run pnpm install so the lockfile resolves a single semver.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/version.test.ts: isValidVersion('1.2.0') true and isValidVersion('1.2') false; gtVersion('1.0.0','1.1.0') false and gtVersion('1.1.0','1.0.0') true; compareVersions('1.0.0-beta.1','1.0.0') === -1; rsortVersions(['1.0.0','1.2.0','1.1.0']) deep-equals ['1.2.0','1.1.0','1.0.0']; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes; `pnpm why semver` resolves exactly one version.

Risks / out of scope:
Use the same range "^7.6.0" the submission package uses (specs/submission-package.md line 91) to avoid a second resolved copy. Strict mode only — do not enable semver loose/coerce. No upgrade-rule logic here (that is asr-iew.2).

title: Implement canonicalHash over sorted skill files in @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-2fb
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash (lines 53-72)

Context:
A skill version's identity is a deterministic SHA-256 over its files, independent of insertion order, real mtimes, and OS cruft. This primitive is consumed by submission dedup (asr-gh1), the blocked_hashes table, and skill_versions.content_hash, so its byte layout must match the spec exactly and never drift.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/hash.ts (extend: add CanonicalFile + canonicalHash)
- packages/core/src/hash.test.ts (extend)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (add `export * from './hash.js';`)

Change:
Add `export interface CanonicalFile { path: string; content: Uint8Array; executable?: boolean }`. Add `export function canonicalHash(files: CanonicalFile[]): string` that: drops entries where isCanonicalExcluded(path); sorts the rest bytewise by UTF-8 path; for each entry concatenates path (UTF-8 bytes) + mode (0o755 if executable else 0o644, 4-byte BE uint) + fixed mtime (epoch seconds 946684800 for 2000-01-01T00:00:00Z, 8-byte BE uint) + uncompressed size (content.length, 8-byte BE uint) + SHA-256(content) (32 raw bytes); returns lowercase-hex SHA-256 of the full concatenation. Use node:crypto createHash('sha256').

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/hash.test.ts: two CanonicalFile[] with identical contents but reversed array order produce the same hash; adding a {path:'.DS_Store'} or {path:'__MACOSX/x'} entry leaves the hash unchanged; flipping one byte of one file's content changes the hash; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Encoding is frozen — do not reorder fields or change the mtime/mode constants (downstream hashes would silently change). mode derives only from the executable flag, never from real fs permissions. No zip parsing here; callers pass an in-memory file list (the yauzl extractor adapter lives in @asr/submission src/zip/canonicalHash.ts).

title: Add canonical-path exclusion predicate to @asr/core hash module
description:
Parent story: asr-2fb
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash (lines 66-67 — excludes .DS_Store, __MACOSX, .git/, build artifacts, classifier-stripped files)

Context:
The canonical content hash must be stable regardless of OS cruft and VCS metadata, so the hash input is filtered before encoding. This predicate is the single place that decides which paths never contribute to a skill version's identity, consumed by canonicalHash (asr-2fb.2) and reusable by the zip classifier.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/hash.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/hash.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function isCanonicalExcluded(path: string): boolean` returning true when the path's basename is `.DS_Store`, or any forward-slash path segment equals `__MACOSX` or `.git`. Operate on POSIX forward-slash paths and compare case-sensitively. No other exports in this task.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/hash.test.ts: isCanonicalExcluded returns true for '.DS_Store', 'sub/.DS_Store', '__MACOSX/foo', '.git/config', 'a/.git/HEAD'; returns false for 'SKILL.md', 'src/index.ts', 'assets/logo.png'; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure string predicate only — no filesystem access, no hashing. Do not also strip build artifacts or classifier output here; those exclusions are applied by the caller (classifier) that passes an already-filtered file list, with this predicate as a backstop.

title: Add GET /audit/submission/:id with owner check
description:
Parent story: asr-xdi
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#read-side-authorization (lines 182-187); specs/audit.md#per-skill--per-user-views (line 169)

Context:
The reviewer UI shows a single submission's timeline, and unlike the other audit views the submitter may read their OWN submission's events. This needs a row-level check (does the caller own this submission?) on top of the role gate.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/audit.ts (add the /submission/:id route to auditRoutes)
- packages/submission/src/http/audit.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add GET /submission/:id to auditRoutes: load the submission (submissions repo asr-igs.2) to read submittedBy; allow if identity.roles includes 'Compliance' or 'Admin', OR identity.sub === submission.submittedBy; else apiError(c,403,'insufficient_permissions') (asr-igs.1). On allow return getBySubmission(db,id) (asr-xdi repo) as chronological JSON; apiError(c,404,'not_found') if the submission does not exist.

Acceptance:
vitest http/audit.test.ts: GET /api/v1/audit/submission/:id as the owning submitter (identity.sub === submittedBy, roles ['Submitter']) returns 200 with that submission's events; the same call as an unrelated Submitter returns 403 { error:'insufficient_permissions' }; as Compliance (non-owner) returns 200; an unknown id returns 404; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The owner check is row-level and cannot be expressed by requireRole alone -- do it in the handler. Do NOT change the other audit routes. Reuse the submissions read helper rather than a raw query.

title: Add role-gated GET /audit skill,user,verify routes
description:
Parent story: asr-xdi
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#per-skill--per-user-views (lines 165-171); specs/audit.md#read-side-authorization (lines 182-189)

Context:
Compliance reviewers and admins need first-class history endpoints. These three families are gated purely by role: skill history (Compliance/Admin), per-user history (Admin only, privacy-sensitive), and chain verify (Admin only, rate-limited full scan).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/audit.ts (new -- auditRoutes Hono router)
- packages/submission/src/http/audit.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (mount app.route('/api/v1/audit', auditRoutes))

Change:
Build auditRoutes = new Hono(). GET /skill/:owner/:name and GET /skill/:owner/:name/v:version -> requireRole('Compliance','Admin') (asr-k4m.3) then getBySkill (asr-xdi repo). GET /user/:sub -> requireRole('Admin') then getByUser. GET /verify -> requireRole('Admin') then verifyChain(db, keys) (asr-7gx) returning the VerifyResult as JSON. Return events chronologically via c.json. Mount under /api/v1/audit in index.ts (specs/submission-package.md line 137).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/http/audit.test.ts (Hono test app, identity injected via c.set): GET /api/v1/audit/skill/o/foo as Compliance returns 200 with only foo's events; GET /api/v1/audit/user/s1 as Compliance returns 403 and as Admin returns 200; GET /api/v1/audit/verify as Admin returns 200 with { valid:true, ... }; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement /audit/submission/:id here (its row-level owner check is the asr-xdi submission task). Rate-limiting of /verify (spec line 189) is owned by the security/rate-limit story -- leave a TODO referencing it, do not inline a limiter. Use the existing requireRole; do not re-implement role logic.

title: Add audit_events query repository
description:
Parent story: asr-xdi
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#per-skill--per-user-views (lines 161-171)

Context:
The compliance dashboards query the audit log by submission, by skill (all versions or one), and by user. Centralising these reads in one repository (over the idx_audit_* indexes) keeps the HTTP handlers thin and gives verifyChain/anchor a shared row->AuditEvent mapper.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/auditEvents.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/auditEvents.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export a row->AuditEvent mapper and: getBySubmission(db,id), getBySkill(db,skillName,version?), getByUser(db,actor), getAllChronological(db) -- each returning AuditEvent[] ordered by timestamp ASC (rowid tiebreak), using the matching idx_audit_* index. getBySkill omits the version predicate when version is undefined (all versions). Import AuditEvent from '@asr/core'.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/auditEvents.test.ts: after emitting events for two skills/submissions, getBySubmission returns only that submission's events in chronological order; getBySkill(db,'foo') returns events across all versions of foo and none of bar; getBySkill(db,'foo','1.0.0') filters to that version; getByUser(db,'user-sub') returns only that actor's events; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Read-only mappers -- no auth here (asr-xdi route tasks enforce roles). Do NOT add HTTP routes. Mapping snake_case->camelCase must match the AuditEvent shape so callers (verifyChain, handlers) agree.

title: Add degraded-mode guard on chain break
description:
Parent story: asr-7gx
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#chain-verification (lines 137-141)

Context:
When the chain is broken the system must stop accepting writes and announce the breach rather than silently continuing. A guard middleware runs verifyChain and, on valid:false, blocks write endpoints with 503 audit_chain_broken and emits an audit.verify.failed event (itself audited) so on-call alerting fires.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/degraded.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/degraded.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export a Hono auditChainGuard(db, keys): MiddlewareHandler that, for non-GET requests on /api/v1/* (skip GET reads and /health), runs verifyChain(db, keys); if valid:false, emits audit.verify.failed once (emitAudit action 'audit.verify.failed', actor 'system', actorType 'system', detail { brokenAt, reason }) guarded so it is not re-emitted on every blocked request (e.g. only emit when the latest audit row is not already audit.verify.failed), then returns apiError(c, 503, 'audit_chain_broken', { brokenAt }) (errors helper asr-igs.1); else await next(). Cache the verify result for a short TTL to avoid a full table scan per write.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/degraded.test.ts: a Hono test app with the guard over a clean chain lets a POST through (handler 200); after tampering one audit row, the same POST returns res.status===503 with body { error:'audit_chain_broken', brokenAt:<id> } and exactly one audit.verify.failed row exists afterwards; a GET is never blocked; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT run verifyChain on every read (perf) -- only gate writes. Guard against an infinite emit loop. On-call paging / alert rules (spec line 141) are the Azure monitoring epic. The canonical mount order in src/index.ts is finalised with the other middleware -- reference the guard, do not reorder existing middleware.

title: Add verifyChain integrity walker
description:
Parent story: asr-7gx
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#chain-verification (lines 116-135)

Context:
Tamper-evidence is only real if something checks the chain. verifyChain walks the audit_events table in insertion order, asserting each row's prev_hash links to the prior row's hash and that the recomputed HMAC matches, resolving keys by hmac_key_id through the KeyRing. Backs the epic success criterion 'tampering with one row fails verification'.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/verify.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/verify.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export type VerifyResult = { valid: true; eventCount: number; lastHash: string } | { valid: false; brokenAt: string; reason: 'prev_hash mismatch'|'unknown key'|'hash mismatch' } and function verifyChain(db: Database.Database, keys: KeyRing): VerifyResult per specs/audit.md lines 118-134: SELECT * FROM audit_events ORDER BY rowid; expectedPrev = '0'.repeat(64); for each row map snake_case->AuditEvent, check prev_hash===expectedPrev (else 'prev_hash mismatch'), key=keys.get(hmac_key_id) (undefined -> 'unknown key'), computeHash(row,key)!==hash -> 'hash mismatch'; advance expectedPrev=hash; return valid:true with eventCount + lastHash. Reuse computeHash (asr-5qw hash task) and KeyRing (asr-tqo).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/verify.test.ts: verifyChain over 3 cleanly-emitted events returns { valid:true, eventCount:3 } with lastHash === the 3rd row hash; UPDATE-ing the 2nd row's detail makes verifyChain return valid:false, brokenAt = that row id, reason 'hash mismatch'; a row whose hmac_key_id is absent from the ring returns reason 'unknown key'; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Read-only -- verifyChain must never write (the audit.verify.failed emission + 503 gating is the asr-7gx degraded-mode task). Field mapping snake_case->camelCase must match the computeHash payload exactly. Do NOT add the HTTP route here (asr-xdi owns GET /audit/verify).

title: Add audit.emit transactional chokepoint
description:
Parent story: asr-5qw
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#event-types-closed-enum (lines 80-89); specs/audit.md#hash-chain (lines 91-114)

Context:
All audit rows must flow through ONE helper so the closed action enum is enforced and every row is hash-chained and written in the same transaction as the state change it documents (no orphan audit rows). Workflow nodes and API handlers call this instead of inserting audit rows directly.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/emit.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/emit.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/package.json (add "ulid":"^2.3.0" to dependencies if absent)

Change:
Export function emitAudit(db: Database.Database, input: { action: AuditAction; submissionId?: string|null; skillName?: string|null; version?: string|null; actor: string; actorType: 'user'|'system'|'compliance'; detail: Record<string,unknown> }): AuditEvent. Steps: (1) if !AUDIT_ACTIONS.includes(input.action) throw new Error(`unknown audit action: ${input.action}`) BEFORE any DB access; (2) id = ulid(); timestamp = new Date().toISOString(); (3) prevHash = `SELECT hash FROM audit_events ORDER BY rowid DESC LIMIT 1` value or '0'.repeat(64) if none; (4) hmacKeyId = process.env.AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_ID, key = Buffer.from(process.env.AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES, 'base64'); (5) hash = computeHash({...event, hmacKeyId}, key) (asr-5qw hash task); (6) INSERT the row. Provide it so the CALLER runs it inside their own db.transaction(...) -- do not open a new transaction inside emitAudit -- document this in a doc-comment. Import AUDIT_ACTIONS, AuditAction, AuditEvent from '@asr/core'; ulid from 'ulid'.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/emit.test.ts (AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_ID/BYTES set in test): after runMigrations(db) + a submissions row, two sequential emitAudit calls return events where event2.prevHash === event1.hash and each event.hash === computeHash(event, key); emitAudit with action 'bogus.action' throws and SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_events stays 0; calling emitAudit inside a db.transaction that then throws leaves 0 audit rows; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT open a transaction inside emitAudit -- atomicity comes from the CALLER's wrapping transaction (spec line 89). Single env key only; KeyRing rotation is asr-tqo. Do NOT add the verify endpoint or degraded mode (asr-7gx). ulid is also assumed by asr-igs.3; add the dep once.

title: Add computeHash audit payload hasher
description:
Parent story: asr-5qw
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#hash-chain (lines 91-114)

Context:
Each audit row's hash links it to the previous row, making the log tamper-evident. The hash is an HMAC-SHA256 over a fixed pipe-joined payload of the event fields. Isolating this as a pure function lets both audit.emit (asr-5qw emit task) and verifyChain (asr-7gx) compute identical hashes, which is the whole basis of chain verification.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/hash.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/hash.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export function computeHash(event: Omit<AuditEvent, 'hash'>, hmacKey: Buffer): string importing AuditEvent from '@asr/core'. Build payload = [event.id, event.submissionId ?? '', event.skillName ?? '', event.version ?? '', event.timestamp, event.actor, event.action, JSON.stringify(event.detail), event.prevHash].join('|') exactly per specs/audit.md lines 100-110, then return createHmac('sha256', hmacKey).update(payload).digest('hex') (node:crypto). Pure, no DB, no I/O.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/hash.test.ts: computeHash(sampleEventWithoutHash, Buffer.from('k'.repeat(32))) is a 64-char lowercase hex string and is deterministic across two calls; changing event.detail changes the digest; a null submissionId/skillName/version serialises to empty-string segments (asserted against a hand-computed payload digest); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The payload field order and the `?? ''` nullable handling are load-bearing -- any drift silently breaks verifyChain. Do NOT read AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES here (callers pass the Buffer). Do NOT insert rows or generate ids here.

title: Add 0004 audit_events migration
description:
Parent story: asr-a03
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#schema (lines 13-35)

Context:
The audit trail is an append-only HMAC-chained log; every emitter and the chain verifier read/write this one table. The schema must exist before audit.emit (asr-5qw), verifyChain (asr-7gx), or the view endpoints (asr-xdi) can be built. The @asr/core AuditEvent type + AUDIT_ACTIONS enum are already provided by asr-pf0; this task is the SQLite side only.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0004_audit_events.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (import + push the 0004 entry, ascending id order)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0004_audit_events.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export a Migration { id: 4, name: 'audit_events', up } whose up runs CREATE TABLE audit_events(id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, submission_id TEXT, skill_name TEXT, version TEXT, timestamp TEXT NOT NULL, actor TEXT NOT NULL, actor_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(actor_type IN ('user','system','compliance')), action TEXT NOT NULL, detail TEXT NOT NULL, prev_hash TEXT NOT NULL, hash TEXT NOT NULL, hmac_key_id TEXT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY (submission_id) REFERENCES submissions(id)) verbatim from specs/audit.md lines 14-28, plus the five indexes idx_audit_submission(submission_id), idx_audit_skill(skill_name, version), idx_audit_actor(actor), idx_audit_action(action), idx_audit_timestamp(timestamp) (lines 30-34). In migrations/index.ts import the 0004 entry and push it after 0003 (keep ascending id order).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0004_audit_events.test.ts: after runMigrations(db), db.pragma('index_list(audit_events)') contains all five idx_audit_* names; db.pragma('table_info(audit_events)') includes columns id, submission_id, actor_type, action, detail, prev_hash, hash, hmac_key_id; inserting a row with actor_type='robot' throws (CHECK constraint); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Migration numbering is a single global sequence: 0001=submissions (asr-7jd.3), 0002=scan_results (asr-st5.4), 0003=workflow_locks (asr-fc6.1) -- this is 0004, do not renumber or collide. Do NOT define AUDIT_ACTIONS/AuditEvent here (owned by asr-pf0). Do NOT add audit.emit or hashing here. The FK requires the submissions table (0001).

title: Add review_decision MCP tool with SoD-guarded approve/reject
description:
Parent story: asr-3hs
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-definitions (review_decision lines 99-106); specs/workflow.md#separation-of-duties (lines 224-226)

Context:
review_decision is the only mutating MCP tool: a Compliance reviewer approves or rejects one submission, delivering the HITL signal into the workflow. It must enforce both the Compliance role and submitter!=approver separation of duties, mirroring the REST approve handler (asr-kkd.4).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/reviewDecision.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (register)

Change:
export `registerReviewDecision(server, db)` -> `server.tool('review_decision', { inputSchema:{ submissionId:z.string(), decision:z.enum(['approve','reject']), reason:z.string().optional() } }, handler)` (schema per specs/mcp.md lines 101-105). Handler: `requireToolRole(principal,'Compliance')` (Submitter -> -32001); load the submission via the read repo (asr-igs.2), missing -> `throw new McpToolError(MCP_ERROR.resource_not_found,'resource_not_found')` (-32003); call `assertSeparation(submission.submittedBy, principal.sub)` (asr-3hs.1) and on SeparationOfDutiesError return an MCP tool result with `isError:true` and message 'separation_of_duties_violation'; otherwise deliver the review approve/reject HITL signal into the workflow (same path as asr-kkd.4 / asr-kkd.5) and return the resulting status (e.g. phase 'published' on approve).

Acceptance:
vitest integration: review_decision { submissionId, decision:'approve' } as a Submitter principal throws code -32001; as a Compliance principal whose `sub` equals submission.submittedBy returns a tool result with `isError===true` and message containing 'separation_of_duties_violation'; as a Compliance principal whose `sub` differs transitions the submission to phase 'published'; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes (HITL signal stubbed).

Risks / out of scope:
SoD surfaces as an MCP tool-result error (isError:true), NOT a new JSON-RPC code — the spec error table has no SoD code; do not invent one. Do not implement the Flowcraft engine or the merge/publish step (asr-82o) — stub the HITL signal in the test. Rate limiting (60/min/sub, specs/mcp.md line 111) is asr-3f8.

title: Add review_queue MCP tool (Compliance role)
description:
Parent story: asr-3hs
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (line 38)

Context:
Compliance reviewers list the submissions awaiting their decision through the review_queue tool before acting. It is a Compliance-role read tool that proxies the submissions read repository (asr-igs.2) for rows in the compliance-review HITL phase.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/reviewQueue.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (register)

Change:
export `registerReviewQueue(server, db)` -> `server.tool('review_queue', { inputSchema:{ limit:z.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(20) } }, handler)`. Handler: `requireToolRole(principal,'Compliance')` (asr-x16.2) — a Submitter therefore gets -32001; then query the submissions repository (asr-igs.2) for submissions whose phase is awaiting compliance review and return them (up to limit) as the tool result content. Register inside createMcpServer.

Acceptance:
vitest integration (a submission seeded in the compliance-review phase): review_queue as a Compliance principal returns a result containing that submission; review_queue as a Submitter-only principal throws code -32001; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Read-only listing — no mutation (that is asr-3hs.3). Reuse the submissions read repo (asr-igs.2); do not query SQLite directly. The exact "review-pending" predicate follows the workflow status model (asr-82o.2) — filter on that existing phase, do not invent a new column.

title: Add submitter-not-approver separation-of-duties guard helper
description:
Parent story: asr-3hs
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#separation-of-duties (lines 224-226); specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (auth/separation.ts line 17)

Context:
Both the REST approve/reject handler (asr-kkd.4) and the MCP review_decision tool (asr-3hs.3) must refuse a Compliance user acting on their own submission. Per the module layout this rule lives in one shared auth/separation.ts so it is single-sourced and enforced as defence-in-depth alongside the HITL node's forbiddenActors (workflow.md line 131).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/auth/separation.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/auth/separation.test.ts (new)

Change:
separation.ts: export `class SeparationOfDutiesError extends Error {}` and `export function assertSeparation(submitterSub: string, approverSub: string): void` that throws `new SeparationOfDutiesError('separation_of_duties_violation')` when submitterSub===approverSub, else returns. Keep it transport-agnostic (no Hono Context, no MCP code) so the REST handler maps it to apiError(403,'separation_of_duties_violation') (asr-kkd.4) and the MCP tool maps it to an isError tool result (asr-3hs.3).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/auth/separation.test.ts: assertSeparation('u1','u1') throws a SeparationOfDutiesError; assertSeparation('u1','u2') returns undefined; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure rule helper — no role check (that is asr-k4m.3 / asr-x16.2), no HITL signal delivery, no DB access. The mid-flow role-loss check (workflow.md line 226) is the approve/reject handler's concern, not this helper. Do not couple to Hono or MCP types.

title: Add registry_download_url MCP tool with yank rejection
description:
Parent story: asr-76e
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-definitions (registry_download_url lines 86-97); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload (lines 81-91)

Context:
registry_download_url resolves a skill version to the Forgejo generic-package URL the client downloads, plus the expected content hash so the client can verify before extracting. A yanked version is refused so agents never install a withdrawn skill (spec line 89).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/registryDownloadUrl.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (register)

Change:
export `registerRegistryDownloadUrl(server, db)` -> `server.tool('registry_download_url', { inputSchema:{ owner:z.string(), name:z.string(), version:z.string().optional() } }, handler)` (schema per specs/mcp.md lines 90-94). Handler: `requireToolRole(principal,'Submitter')`; resolve the version via getPublishedSkill (asr-4it.1) — default to the latest non-yanked when version is omitted; if the resolved version is yanked -> `throw new McpToolError(MCP_ERROR.version_yanked,'version_yanked')` (-32004); if no such published version -> resource_not_found (-32003). Build `url = ${forgejoBase}/api/packages/${owner}/generic/${name}/${version}/skill.zip` (same shape as asr-4it.3 / ForgejoClient.publishArtifact; forgejoBase = env.FORGEJO_URL with any trailing /api/v1 stripped). Return `{ url, contentHash: version.contentHash /* 'sha256:...' */, sizeBytes, manifest, expiresAt }` (specs/mcp.md line 96).

Acceptance:
vitest integration (acme/x v1.0.0 published, v0.9.0 yanked, seeded via the skills repo): registry_download_url { owner:'acme', name:'x', version:'1.0.0' } as a Submitter returns a result whose `url` matches `/api/packages/acme/generic/x/1.0.0/skill.zip`, `contentHash` starts with 'sha256:', and `manifest` is the SkillManifest; registry_download_url for the yanked v0.9.0 throws code -32004; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Returns metadata only — the client (asr-5wr CLI) downloads + verifies; this tool never streams bytes. In Phase 1 `expiresAt` is nominal (Forgejo package URLs are stable, not signed/expiring). `sizeBytes` may be 0/stubbed until the publish record (asr-ai5) persists it.

title: Add registry_info and registry_versions MCP tools
description:
Parent story: asr-76e
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (lines 32, 34); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownername (lines 59-79)

Context:
registry_info returns the manifest plus full version list for one skill; registry_versions returns just the non-yanked versions (semver-ordered). Both are Submitter-role read tools proxying getPublishedSkill (asr-4it.1), giving agents skill detail before they decide to download.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/registryInfo.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/registryVersions.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (register both)

Change:
registryInfo.ts: export `registerRegistryInfo(server, db)` -> `server.tool('registry_info', { inputSchema:{ owner:z.string(), name:z.string() } }, handler)`; handler `requireToolRole(principal,'Submitter')` then `getPublishedSkill(db, owner, name)` (asr-4it.1); undefined -> `throw new McpToolError(MCP_ERROR.resource_not_found,'resource_not_found')`; else return the SkillDetail (manifest + versions, yanked entries marked per registry-api.md line 61). registryVersions.ts: export `registerRegistryVersions(server, db)` -> `registry_versions` with the same input; return only `detail.versions.filter(v => v.yanked !== true)` semver-rsorted. Register both inside createMcpServer.

Acceptance:
vitest integration (skills repo seeded with acme/x versions 1.0.0 and 1.1.0): registry_info { owner:'acme', name:'x' } as a Submitter returns a SkillDetail whose `versions.length===2`; registry_info for a missing skill throws code -32003; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
registry_versions excludes yanked entries (spec line 34: "non-yanked"); registry_info includes them marked (registry-api.md line 61). Reuse getPublishedSkill — no direct SQL. download_url is asr-76e.3.

title: Add registry_search and registry_list MCP tools
description:
Parent story: asr-76e
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (lines 31-33), #tool-definitions (registry_search lines 75-84)

Context:
Agents discover published skills through the registry_search and registry_list Submitter-role tools. Both are read-only metadata queries that proxy the published-skills read repository (listPublishedSkills, asr-4it.1) — they return SkillSummary lists, never touch the user's disk (spec line 27).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/registrySearch.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/registryList.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (register both tools inside createMcpServer)

Change:
registrySearch.ts: export `registerRegistrySearch(server, db)` calling `server.tool('registry_search', { description, inputSchema:{ query:z.string().optional(), tag:z.array(z.string()).optional(), author:z.string().optional(), kind:z.enum(['skill','persona']).optional(), limit:z.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(20) } }, handler)` (schema verbatim from specs/mcp.md lines 75-84). Handler: `requireToolRole(principal,'Submitter')` (asr-x16.2), then `listPublishedSkills(db,{ q:query, tag, kind, limit })` (asr-4it.1) and return the items as the tool result content. registryList.ts: export `registerRegistryList(server, db)` for `registry_list` — same pattern, filter-only listing (no free-text query). Register both inside createMcpServer (server.ts).

Acceptance:
vitest integration (temp sqlite seeded via the skills repo with a published acme/x): calling the registry_search tool with { query:'x' } as a Submitter principal returns a result containing the seeded skill's SkillSummary; calling it as a principal lacking Submitter throws code -32001; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Read-only — never write to disk (spec line 27). Reuse listPublishedSkills (asr-4it.1); do not re-query SQLite directly. Pagination cursors mirror the REST registry route, but the tool may return the first page only in Phase 1. registry_info / registry_versions / registry_download_url are siblings asr-76e.2 / asr-76e.3.

title: Bind authenticated principal to MCP session and enforce at request time
description:
Parent story: asr-xcs
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#authentication (lines 16-20)

Context:
After initialize authenticates the caller, the issued Mcp-Session-Id must be bound to that principal so subsequent tool calls run as the same identity without re-sending the token, and an unauthenticated or unknown session is refused. This wires resolveMcpPrincipal (asr-xcs.1) into the transport session store (asr-1c3.1) and exposes the bound principal to tool handlers.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (extend mcpHandler: authenticate at initialize, store principal with the session, resolve it on later requests)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Widen the session store value to `{ transport: StreamableHTTPServerTransport; principal: Identity }`. In mcpHandler's POST initialize branch, call `await resolveMcpPrincipal(req)` BEFORE creating the transport; on throw, serialise the McpToolError via mcpError (asr-x16.1) into a JSON-RPC error response and return WITHOUT opening a session (no Mcp-Session-Id issued). On success, store the resolved principal alongside the transport. For subsequent POST/GET requests, look up the session and expose the bound principal to tool handlers (e.g. via the per-request handler `extra` passed by the SDK) so asr-76e/asr-3hs tools read `principal`. A request with no resolvable session/principal -> authentication_required (-32002).

Acceptance:
