vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/server.test.ts: an initialize POST with no bearer (AUTH_MODE=entra) returns a JSON-RPC error whose `code===-32002` and issues NO Mcp-Session-Id header; an initialize with a valid Submitter token issues a session and a follow-up tools/list request on that session succeeds; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Don't re-verify the token on every request — bind once at initialize and trust the session (spec lines 16-20). The mock-mode path must still bind a (mock) principal so tools have an identity. SSE GET reuses the same session lookup (asr-1c3.2). Per-tool role checks remain inside each tool (asr-x16.2).

title: Add MCP auth gate resolving principal from bearer or mock
description:
Parent story: asr-xcs
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#authentication (lines 12-23)

Context:
The /mcp surface must be gated by the SAME Entra bearer validation as the REST API — the MCP server issues no tokens of its own (spec line 21). initialize requires Authorization: Bearer <jwt> with the access_as_user scope and at least the Submitter role; in AUTH_MODE=mock the mock principal is injected with no token (spec line 23). This task provides the resolver; asr-xcs.2 binds the result to the session.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/auth.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/auth.test.ts (new)

Change:
auth.ts: export `async function resolveMcpPrincipal(req): Promise<Identity>`. When env.AUTH_MODE==='mock', build the mock Identity from MOCK_USER_SUB / MOCK_USER_ROLES exactly as mockAuth (asr-k4m.1) does. Otherwise extract the Bearer token from the Authorization header (missing/malformed -> throw `new McpToolError(MCP_ERROR.authentication_required,'authentication_required')`); verify it with the shared jose verifier exported by ../auth/entra.ts (extend asr-k4m.2 to export `verifyBearer(token): Promise<Identity>` reused by both the REST middleware and here), producing { sub, roles }; on verify failure -> throw authentication_required. Finally enforce the Submitter floor: if roles excludes 'Submitter', throw `new McpToolError(MCP_ERROR.insufficient_permissions,'insufficient_permissions',{ required:'Submitter', actual: roles })`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/auth.test.ts: with AUTH_MODE=entra, resolveMcpPrincipal on a request with no Authorization header rejects with an McpToolError whose `code===-32002`; a request bearing a valid jose-signed Submitter token resolves to {sub, roles:['Submitter',...]}; with AUTH_MODE=mock and MOCK_USER_* set, resolveMcpPrincipal with no token resolves to the mock principal; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes (JWKS injected, no network).

Risks / out of scope:
Reuse the entra verifier — do not re-implement JWT validation; extend asr-k4m.2 to export verifyBearer rather than duplicating jose logic. Session binding is sibling asr-xcs.2. Per-tool Compliance checks remain in asr-x16.2. The token is obtained client-side via `asr login` (asr-thq) — not this server's concern.

title: Add per-tool role guard for MCP tool handlers
description:
Parent story: asr-x16
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory (lines 29-41); specs/mcp.md#error-envelope (lines 53-55)

Context:
Per-tool authorization is enforced inside each tool handler (not transport middleware), so an authenticated Submitter who calls a Compliance-only tool (review_decision) receives insufficient_permissions in the MCP envelope (spec line 41). This guard is the single helper every tool (asr-76e, asr-3hs) calls first with its required role.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/roles.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/roles.test.ts (new)

Change:
roles.ts: import `McpToolError` and `MCP_ERROR` from './errors.js' (asr-x16.1) and the `Identity` type from '../auth/types.js' (asr-k4m.1). Export `requireToolRole(principal: Identity, required: string): void` — if `principal.roles` does not include `required`, throw `new McpToolError(MCP_ERROR.insufficient_permissions, 'insufficient_permissions', { required, actual: principal.roles })`; otherwise return. Keep the data payload shape exactly { required, actual } per spec line 54.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/roles.test.ts: requireToolRole({sub:'u1',roles:['Submitter']},'Compliance') throws an McpToolError whose `code===-32001` and `data` deep-equals {required:'Compliance',actual:['Submitter']}; requireToolRole({sub:'u1',roles:['Compliance']},'Compliance') returns undefined (no throw); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Single-role check only — the MCP tool table assigns exactly one required role per tool, so no multi-role-any is needed. Do not perform separation-of-duties here (that compares subs at the handler, asr-3hs.1). Authentication (token -> principal) is asr-xcs, not this guard.

title: Add MCP JSON-RPC error envelope and ApiError-to-code mapping
description:
Parent story: asr-x16
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#error-envelope (lines 43-67); aligned with specs/api.md#error-responses via packages/submission/src/http/errors.ts (asr-igs.1)

Context:
Every MCP tool must surface failures in the documented JSON-RPC error shape so agents branch on a stable code set, never on raw exceptions. Centralising the code map (and the ApiError->code bridge) stops each tool hand-rolling error objects and keeps the mapping single-sourced alongside the REST ApiError union.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/errors.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/errors.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export const MCP_ERROR = { insufficient_permissions:-32001, authentication_required:-32002, resource_not_found:-32003, version_yanked:-32004, rate_limited:-32005, audit_chain_broken:-32006, internal_error:-32099 } as const` (verbatim from specs/mcp.md lines 59-67). Add `export class McpToolError extends Error { constructor(public code:number, message:string, public data?:Record<string,unknown>){ super(message); } }`. Add `export function mcpError(code:number, message:string, data?:Record<string,unknown>): { code:number; message:string; data?:Record<string,unknown> }` returning the JSON-RPC error object; when code===MCP_ERROR.internal_error force data to `{ traceId }` only, dropping any other keys (no internals leaked — spec line 67). Add `export function fromApiError(e: ApiError): number` mapping the asr-igs.1 ApiError union to MCP_ERROR codes (authentication_required, insufficient_permissions, *_not_found -> -32003, version_yanked -> -32004, too_many_requests -> rate_limited, audit_chain_broken; everything else -> internal_error). Import the ApiError type from '../http/errors.js'.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/errors.test.ts: mcpError(MCP_ERROR.insufficient_permissions,'insufficient_permissions',{required:'Compliance',actual:['Submitter']}) deep-equals {code:-32001,message:'insufficient_permissions',data:{required:'Compliance',actual:['Submitter']}}; mcpError(MCP_ERROR.internal_error,'boom',{secret:'x',traceId:'t1'}).data deep-equals {traceId:'t1'}; fromApiError('version_yanked')===-32004; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Keep codes byte-identical to specs/mcp.md (clients depend on the closed set). No role logic here (sibling asr-x16.2). Do not redefine the ApiError union — import it from asr-igs.1's http/errors.ts.

title: Wire GET /mcp SSE result stream to the session transport
description:
Parent story: asr-1c3
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#transport (lines 7-9)

Context:
Streamable HTTP delivers tool output and server notifications over a server-sent-events stream opened with GET /mcp, correlated to a session by the Mcp-Session-Id header. Without this, clients can initialize but never receive streamed results or teardown sessions. Builds on the session store created in asr-1c3.1.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (extend mcpHandler with GET SSE branch + DELETE teardown)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In mcpHandler, when the HTTP method is GET: read the Mcp-Session-Id header; if no transport is stored for it, return a not-found error (JSON-RPC resource_not_found / -32003 once asr-x16 lands; until then a 404 status). Otherwise call `transport.handleRequest(req, res)` so the SDK opens the SSE stream bound to that session. Also handle DELETE /mcp: look up the transport, call its `close()` and delete it from the sessions map (session teardown). Reuse the same Hono<->Node req/res bridge introduced in asr-1c3.1.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/server.test.ts: after an initialize handshake yields an Mcp-Session-Id, a GET /mcp carrying that header responds with Content-Type `text/event-stream` (SSE stream opened); a GET /mcp with an unknown session id returns a not-found error; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Don't re-implement SSE framing — delegate to transport.handleRequest. The -32003 envelope is owned by asr-x16.1; reference it once available, fall back to a 404 status if x16 is not yet merged. No tool logic here. Principal/session-auth lookup is asr-xcs.2.

title: Bootstrap MCP server with Streamable HTTP transport and session store
description:
Parent story: asr-1c3
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#transport (lines 5-10); specs/submission-package.md#composition (mcpHandler import line 120, app.all('/mcp', mcpHandler) line 139), #module-layout (mcp/server.ts line 26)

Context:
Agents (Claude Code, Codex) talk to the registry over MCP Streamable HTTP at a single /mcp endpoint. Nothing exists under packages/submission/src/mcp yet. This task stands up the McpServer + Streamable HTTP transport and a per-session transport store so the tool tasks (asr-76e, asr-3hs) and auth (asr-xcs) have a server to register on. POST /mcp carries JSON-RPC; the initialize handshake issues an opaque Mcp-Session-Id and advertises protocol version 2025-06-18.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (new — McpServer factory + StreamableHTTPServerTransport session store + exported mcpHandler)
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (mount app.all('/mcp', mcpHandler) per submission-package.md line 139)

Change:
Add "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk":"^1.0.0" to packages/submission deps (submission-package.md line 83). server.ts: export `createMcpServer(): McpServer` from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js' with serverInfo { name:'asr-registry', version } and no tools yet. Maintain `const sessions = new Map<string, StreamableHTTPServerTransport>()`. Export `mcpHandler` (Hono handler): on a POST with no Mcp-Session-Id header and an `initialize` body, create a `StreamableHTTPServerTransport({ sessionIdGenerator: () => randomUUID() })`, connect a fresh createMcpServer() to it, store the transport keyed by the generated session id, and bridge Hono's raw Node req/res (via @hono/node-server's incoming/outgoing) into `transport.handleRequest(req, res, body)`. Subsequent POSTs resolve the transport by the Mcp-Session-Id header. index.ts: `import { mcpHandler } from './mcp/server.js'` and add `app.all('/mcp', mcpHandler)` (its own auth gate lives inside, per submission-package.md line 139).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/server.test.ts: a POST /mcp `initialize` request returns a response carrying an `Mcp-Session-Id` header and a JSON-RPC result whose `protocolVersion === '2025-06-18'`; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
No auth gate here (asr-xcs adds it) — but mount with app.all so xcs can wrap inside mcpHandler. No tools registered here (asr-76e/asr-3hs). Do not implement the GET SSE branch here (sibling asr-1c3.2). Bridging Hono<->Node req/res is the tricky part: StreamableHTTPServerTransport expects Node http req/res objects, so use @hono/node-server's raw objects rather than Fetch Request/Response.

title: Implement resumeWorkflows() crash-resume driver for non-terminal submissions
description:
Parent story: asr-fc6
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#crash-recovery-resume (lines 180-188); specs/workflow.md#state-persistence (lines 176-178)

Context:
Workflow state persists to the single WAL `workflow.db` (via @flowcraft/history-sqlite), so an in-flight workflow survives a process crash. On service startup the service must re-hydrate and resume every non-terminal submission. Per the spec, `resumeWorkflows()` scans `submissions` for any row whose status is non-terminal and re-enters the Flowcraft engine; idempotency keys (submissionId, nodeName, attempt) ensure compute nodes are not harmfully double-executed on resume.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/resume.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/resume.test.ts (new)

Change:
Import the `Database` type from `../db/index.js`. Add `export const TERMINAL_PHASES = ['published','rejected','error'] as const` (the SubmissionStatus terminal phases). Add `export function findResumableSubmissions(db: Database): { id: string; statusPhase: string }[]` running `SELECT id, status_phase FROM submissions WHERE status_phase NOT IN ('published','rejected','error')`. Add `export async function resumeWorkflows(db: Database, reenter: (submissionId: string) => Promise<void>): Promise<{ resumed: number }>` that loads findResumableSubmissions and, for each, awaits `reenter(id)` (the engine re-entry callback supplied by the app bootstrap / asr-82o blueprint), counting successes and returning `{ resumed }`; a reenter failure is logged and does not abort the remaining resumes.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/resume.test.ts: after runMigrations and inserting submissions rows with status_phase values 'scan' (resumable), 'questionnaire' (resumable) and 'published' (terminal), findResumableSubmissions(db) returns exactly the two non-terminal ids; resumeWorkflows(db, fakeReenter) calls fakeReenter once per resumable id and resolves to { resumed: 2 }; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Resume only re-enters the engine — the actual node execution and idempotency-key plumbing (Forgejo 409-as-done, scan_results dedupe by (submissionId, contentHash)) is owned by the blueprint/compute nodes (asr-82o) and the Forgejo/scan tasks; this task supplies the boot-time scan + re-entry driver and the terminal-phase set. Keep TERMINAL_PHASES byte-identical to the SubmissionStatus terminal tags (specs/types.md). Do not wire this into app.ts boot here — that integration is the bootstrap task's concern.

LABELS: submission, workflow, tier:task

title: Implement per-skill advisory publish lock with backoff
description:
Parent story: asr-fc6
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#per-skill-mutex (lines 205-212)

Context:
Two submissions for *different* versions of the same skill may both run, but their merges to main must serialise. Per the spec, the workflow takes a per-skill advisory lock just before the `publish` node via `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO publish_locks(skill_name)`; if 0 rows changed it waits + retries with backoff up to 60s, then transitions to error. After a successful publish the lock row is deleted. This primitive is what the publish node (asr-82o blueprint) calls to gate concurrent merges.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/publishLock.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/publishLock.test.ts (new)

Change:
Import the `Database` type from `../db/index.js`. Add `export function tryAcquirePublishLock(db: Database, skillName: string): boolean` running `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO publish_locks (skill_name) VALUES (?)` and returning `info.changes === 1`. Add `export function releasePublishLock(db: Database, skillName: string): void` running `DELETE FROM publish_locks WHERE skill_name=?`. Add `export async function withPublishLock<T>(db: Database, skillName: string, fn: () => Promise<T>, opts?: { timeoutMs?: number; baseDelayMs?: number }): Promise<T>` that retries tryAcquirePublishLock with exponential backoff (default timeoutMs 60000) and throws an Error `publish_lock_timeout` if not acquired within the budget; on acquire it runs fn() and releases the lock in a finally block.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/publishLock.test.ts: after runMigrations, tryAcquirePublishLock(db,'demo') returns true and an immediate second call returns false; withPublishLock(db,'demo', fn, { timeoutMs: 50, baseDelayMs: 10 }) rejects with /publish_lock_timeout/ while the lock is held, and after releasePublishLock(db,'demo') a fresh tryAcquirePublishLock returns true; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The lock must always be released in a finally so a publish failure does not strand the lock. Cap retry at timeoutMs (default 60s per spec) — do NOT loop forever. The "transition to error" on timeout is the publish node's responsibility (asr-82o); here just throw publish_lock_timeout. This is distinct from pending_versions (same-version 409) which is the previous task.

LABELS: submission, workflow, tier:task

title: Implement per-skill+version pending-version mutex (acquire/release)
description:
Parent story: asr-fc6
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#per-skill-mutex (lines 191-203); specs/security.md#race-condition-prevention (lines 197-199)

Context:
The submission API must guarantee that only one workflow runs per (skill_name, version) at a time. Per the spec, the API inserts into `pending_versions` inside the SAME transaction as the `submissions` row; a second submission for the same pair gets a unique-constraint violation that the handler maps to 409 version_in_progress. This task provides the reusable acquire/release primitive (the API handler in asr-igs.3 / asr-kkd wires it into the insert transaction). It mirrors the optimistic-lock prior art in asr-7jd.4.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/pendingVersionLock.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/pendingVersionLock.test.ts (new)

Change:
Import the `Database` type from `../db/index.js`. Add `export function acquirePendingVersion(db: Database, skillName: string, version: string, submissionId: string): boolean` running `INSERT INTO pending_versions (skill_name, version, submission_id, acquired_at) VALUES (?,?,?,?)` with `new Date().toISOString()`, returning true on success and false when better-sqlite3 throws `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY` / `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT` (caught and mapped to false; rethrow any other error). Add `export function releasePendingVersion(db: Database, skillName: string, version: string): void` running `DELETE FROM pending_versions WHERE skill_name=? AND version=?`. Do not start its own transaction — callers compose it into the submissions insert transaction.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/workflow/pendingVersionLock.test.ts: after runMigrations + inserting a submissions row, acquirePendingVersion(db,'demo','1.0.0',id) returns true; a second acquirePendingVersion(db,'demo','1.0.0',id2) returns false (contention); after releasePendingVersion(db,'demo','1.0.0') a third acquire returns true again; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
This is the lock primitive only — it returns false on contention rather than throwing; the 409 version_in_progress mapping lives in the API handler (asr-igs.3 / asr-kkd, not this task). Do NOT open a transaction inside acquire (it must run inside the caller's submissions-insert tx to be atomic). Catch only constraint violations; rethrow everything else. The publish (different-version) lock is the next task.

LABELS: submission, workflow, tier:task

title: Add 0003 workflow-locks migration (pending_versions + publish_locks)
description:
Parent story: asr-fc6
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#per-skill-mutex (lines 189-213); specs/security.md#race-condition-prevention (lines 197-199)

Context:
The per-skill mutex needs two backing tables. `pending_versions` enforces that a given (skill_name, version) pair can only have one in-flight submission — a duplicate insert violates the PK and the API maps it to 409 version_in_progress. `publish_locks` is the per-skill advisory lock taken just before merge so concurrent submissions of *different* versions of the same skill serialise their writes to main. Both are created by a single forward-only migration registered against the runner (asr-7jd.2).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0003_workflow_locks.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (import and push the 0003 entry into `migrations`, ascending id order)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0003_workflow_locks.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export a Migration `{ id: 3, name: 'workflow_locks', up }` whose up runs, verbatim from specs/workflow.md lines 193-200 and 207-208: `CREATE TABLE pending_versions (skill_name TEXT NOT NULL, version TEXT NOT NULL, submission_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES submissions(id), acquired_at TEXT NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (skill_name, version))` and `CREATE TABLE publish_locks (skill_name TEXT PRIMARY KEY)`. In migrations/index.ts import the 0003 entry and push it after the existing entries (keep ascending id order). No query/repository logic here.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0003_workflow_locks.test.ts: after runMigrations(db), `db.pragma('table_info(pending_versions)')` includes columns skill_name, version, submission_id, acquired_at and the PK spans (skill_name, version) (assert via `db.pragma('index_list(pending_versions)')` / a duplicate INSERT throwing), and `db.pragma('table_info(publish_locks)')` includes skill_name as PRIMARY KEY; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Use the table/column names verbatim from the spec — `pending_versions` and `publish_locks`, NOT `workflow_locks` (the file name is just the migration slug). The submissions table FK target is owned by asr-7jd.3; this migration depends on it existing. No acquire/release logic here — that is the mutex module (next task). Do not add a down/rollback path (runner is up-only).

LABELS: submission, workflow, tier:task

title: Add fixture-based scanner integration test (planted secret -> block, clean -> pass)
description:
Parent story: asr-st5
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#test-fixtures (lines 602-625); ### Integration Tests (lines 611-616); story-level acceptance (asr-st5)

Context:
This is the story's headline acceptance: prove the real scanner image produces the right verdicts end-to-end. A planted-secret fixture must hard-block (Gitleaks rule, security-scanning.md line 119) and a clean fixture must pass — exercising the whole image + orchestrator + signed-verdict path, not mocks.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/test/fixtures/scanning/clean-skill/SKILL.md (new)
- packages/submission/test/fixtures/scanning/skill-with-secret/SKILL.md + scripts/leak.ts (new) — planted hardcoded API key
- packages/submission/test/integration/scanner.e2e.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add the two fixtures from the spec's fixtures tree (lines 620-624): `clean-skill/` (md-only, expect pass) and `skill-with-secret/` (scripts/ with a hardcoded `AKIA…`/token literal, expect block). Add a vitest integration test (guarded to run only when the `asr-scanner` image is built / a `RUN_SCANNER_E2E=1` env gate, so CI without Docker skips) that calls `runScanner` (task 03) against each fixture dir: assert `skill-with-secret` → `verdict==='block'` with a gitleaks finding, `clean-skill` → `verdict==='pass'`, and that the returned ScanReport's `signature` verifies (runScanner already throws on bad signature, so a resolved promise proves it).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/test/integration/scanner.e2e.test.ts (with RUN_SCANNER_E2E=1 and the image built): `runScanner` on skill-with-secret resolves to verdict==='block'; on clean-skill resolves to verdict==='pass'; when RUN_SCANNER_E2E is unset the suite is skipped (test.skipIf), so `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` is green with or without Docker.

Risks / out of scope:
Reuses runScanner (task 03) and the image (task 01) — do NOT re-implement container invocation here. Gate on Docker availability so the default unit-test run stays hermetic. Only the secret + clean fixtures are in scope; skill-with-cve / skill-with-injection / skill-network-violation fixtures (spec lines 624-626) are follow-ups, not this task.

title: Validate reject reason (10-500 chars) and deliver reject HITL signal in POST /submissions/:id/reject
description:
Parent story: asr-kkd
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#post-submissionsidreject (lines 119-128); specs/api.md#role-matrix (lines 149-164); specs/workflow.md#separation-of-duties (lines 224-226)

Context:
A Compliance reviewer rejects a submission at the `review` HITL node with a mandatory human-readable reason. The reason is persisted on the terminal `rejected` status and surfaced to the submitter, so it must be validated (10–500 chars per spec line 123). Like approve, separation of duties is re-checked at the handler.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/reject.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/reject.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function registerRejectRoute(app: Hono): void` mounting `POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/reject`. Body: `{ reason: string }`, required, length 10–500 (spec line 123). Load the submission via the read repository (asr-igs.2); 404 `apiError(c,404,'submission_not_found')`. If `reason` is missing or outside 10–500 chars, return a 400 using the closed-enum envelope: `apiError(c, 400, 'invalid_manifest', { message: 'reason must be 10-500 characters', details: { reason: '...' } })` — reuse the existing 400-class ApiError value rather than inventing one, and put the human-readable rule in `message`/`details`. Re-check submitter≠approver → `apiError(c,403,'separation_of_duties_violation')` (workflow.md line 226). Otherwise deliver the `review`-reject HITL signal (story asr-82o) → `{ phase: 'rejected'; rejectedAt; reason }`, emit `workflow.review.rejected` via audit.emit (asr-pf0), and return 200 `{ status: { phase: 'rejected', rejectedAt, reason } }` per spec lines 125-126. Import `apiError` from `../errors.js`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/http/routes/reject.test.ts: POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/reject with a 5-char reason returns status 400; with a valid 10–500 char reason from a distinct Compliance caller returns 200 and `(await res.json()).status.phase === 'rejected'` carrying the reason; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Reason validation is the single load-bearing acceptance — keep the 10–500 bound exact (spec line 123). The `Compliance` role gate (role matrix line 158) is owned by story asr-k4m; this handler enforces only SoD + reason validation. Do NOT implement the Flowcraft engine (asr-82o) or auto-reject-on-timeout (workflow.md lines 214-223, owned by the workflow timeout story). Use only existing ApiError enum values from specs/api.md lines 188-203 — do not add a new one.

title: Implement POST /submissions/:id/confirm to deliver user-confirmation HITL signal
description:
Parent story: asr-kkd
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#workflow-interactions (lines 18-26); specs/api.md#role-matrix (lines 149-164); specs/workflow.md (confirmation HITL node, lines 116-120)

Context:
Once the scan report is ready the pipeline pauses at the `confirmation` HITL node (workflow.md lines 116-120, allowedActors = submitter only) so the submitter acknowledges the findings before Compliance review. Delivering this signal resumes the pipeline into the `review` node (compliance-review phase).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/confirm.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/confirm.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function registerConfirmRoute(app: Hono): void` mounting `POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/confirm`. Body is empty/optional. Load the submission via the read repository (asr-igs.2); 404 `apiError(c,404,'submission_not_found')` if absent. Deliver the `confirmation` HITL signal to the Flowcraft engine (story asr-82o) which transitions the row to `{ phase: 'compliance-review' }`. Return 200 `{ status: { phase: 'compliance-review' } }`. Import `apiError` from `../errors.js`; SubmissionStatus from `@asr/core`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/http/routes/confirm.test.ts: POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/confirm on a submission at phase `user-confirmation-pending` returns status 200 and `(await res.json()).status.phase === 'compliance-review'`; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement the Flowcraft signal dispatch (story asr-82o) — invoke its signal API and stub in test. Auth (`Submitter` + sub-match, role matrix line 155) is owned by story asr-k4m; assume identity present. No scan/report logic here.

title: Implement GET /submissions/:id/scan returning ScanReport or 202 while scanning
description:
Parent story: asr-kkd
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#workflow-interactions (lines 18-26); specs/api.md#get-submissionsidscan (lines 97-100); specs/api.md#role-matrix (lines 149-164)

Context:
After the questionnaire signal the pipeline runs the external scan container (workflow.md lines 116-126). The submitter and Compliance reviewers poll this endpoint to fetch the verbatim ScanReport before confirming or approving. While the scan is still running there is no report yet, so the endpoint must distinguish "in progress" from "ready".

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/scan.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/scan.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function registerScanRoute(app: Hono): void` mounting `GET /api/v1/submissions/:id/scan`. Load the submission via the read repository (asr-igs.2); 404 `apiError(c,404,'submission_not_found')` if absent. If the submission has a completed report (phase `scan-complete` or later carries `report`), return 200 with the `ScanReport` body verbatim per spec line 99 (type imported from `@asr/core`). If phase is `scanning` (report not yet produced), return 202 with `{ status: { phase: 'scanning', scanJobId } }`. Import `apiError` from `../errors.js`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/http/routes/scan.test.ts: GET on a submission whose report is ready returns 200 and `await res.json()` deep-equals the stored ScanReport; GET on a submission at phase `scanning` returns status 202; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Read-only endpoint — do NOT trigger or run the scanner (security-scanning epic) and do NOT mutate submission state. Auth scoping (Submitter-own vs Compliance-all, role matrix line 161) is owned by story asr-k4m; assume identity present. ScanReport shape is owned by @asr/core (asr-btm) — do not redefine it.

title: Implement POST /submissions/:id/questionnaire to deliver HITL signal and transition to scanning
description:
Parent story: asr-kkd
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/api.md#workflow-interactions (lines 18-26); specs/api.md#post-submissionsidquestionnaire (lines 82-96); specs/api.md#role-matrix (lines 149-164)

Context:
A code-containing submission pauses at the `questionnaire` HITL node (workflow.md lines 110-114). The submitter answers the generated security questions; delivering that signal resumes the pipeline into the `scan` compute node. This is the first HITL endpoint and the entry point for the human-reviewed path.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/questionnaire.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/http/routes/questionnaire.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function registerQuestionnaireRoute(app: Hono): void` mounting `POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/questionnaire`. Body: `{ responses: Array<{ questionId: string; answer: string | boolean }> }`. Load the submission via the read repository (asr-igs.2); 404 `apiError(c,404,'submission_not_found')` if absent. Deliver the `questionnaire` HITL signal to the Flowcraft engine (engine surface from story asr-82o) which transitions the row to `{ phase: 'scanning'; scanJobId }`. Return 200 `{ status: { phase: 'scanning', scanJobId } }` exactly per spec lines 93-95. Import `apiError` from `../errors.js`; the SubmissionStatus discriminated union from `@asr/core`.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/http/routes/questionnaire.test.ts: POST /api/v1/submissions/:id/questionnaire with a valid responses array on a submission at phase `questionnaire-pending` returns status 200 and `(await res.json()).status.phase === 'scanning'` with a non-empty `scanJobId`; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement the Flowcraft engine/signal dispatch itself (story asr-82o) — call its signal API and stub it in this test. Do NOT implement auth/role middleware (story asr-k4m owns the `Submitter` + sub-match guard, role matrix line 154) — assume an identity is on the context. Do not regenerate questions or run the scanner here.

title: Align API liveness route to /health and pass docker e2e
description:
Parent story: asr-mcx
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/submission-package.md#composition (app.route('/health', healthRoutes), line 133); scripts/e2e-docker.mjs (line 37 polls ${apiBaseUrl}/health); specs/deployment.md#monitoring (/health probe, line 320)

Context:
The docker e2e smoke (scripts/e2e-docker.mjs) and the compose healthcheck both poll /health, but asr-6ax.3 mounted the liveness app at /healthz. This reconciles the route to /health (matching spec + e2e + healthcheck) and makes the existing e2e script green against the full stack — the story's acceptance signal.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (change app.route('/healthz', healthRoutes) -> '/health')
- packages/submission/src/index.test.ts (update the asserted path)

Change:
Rename the mounted liveness path from /healthz to /health in index.ts and its test. No behaviour change to healthRoutes. This aligns the route with the compose healthcheck and scripts/e2e-docker.mjs (which already targets /health).

Acceptance:
`pnpm test:e2e` (scripts/e2e-docker.mjs) brings up deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml, the API answers 200 at /health, and the script prints "E2E smoke check passed" and exits 0; the index.test.ts assertion on app.request('/health') also passes.

Risks / out of scope:
One-line route change plus its test — do not refactor healthRoutes. If a future spec wants both paths, add /healthz as an alias rather than reverting. Requires Docker on the runner (the e2e is environment-gated).

title: Extend docker-compose with api and web services
description:
Parent story: asr-mcx
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#development-docker-compose (lines 11-66), #first-time-setup-dev (lines 68-92)

Context:
The compose file currently brings up only Forgejo (it documents api/web as Phase-1 TODO). This wires the api and web services so `docker compose up` yields the full dev stack the e2e smoke runs against.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml (extend: add api + web services)
- deploy/docker/.env.example (new — FORGEJO_ADMIN_TOKEN=)

Change:
Add service `api` (build context ../.. + dockerfile deploy/docker/Dockerfile.api; ports 3001:3001; env NODE_ENV=development, AUTH_MODE=mock, MOCK_USER_SUB=dev-user-0001, MOCK_USER_ROLES=Submitter,Compliance, FORGEJO_URL=http://forgejo:3000, FORGEJO_UPLOAD_TOKEN/FORGEJO_MERGE_TOKEN=${FORGEJO_ADMIN_TOKEN}, FORGEJO_OWNER=asr-admin, FORGEJO_REPO=skills-registry, DATABASE_PATH=/app/data/workflow.db, PORT=3001; volume ./data/api:/app/data; depends_on forgejo; healthcheck: wget -qO- http://localhost:3001/health). Add service `web` (build context ../.. + dockerfile deploy/docker/Dockerfile.web with VITE_API_URL/VITE_AUTH_MODE args; ports 5173:5173; depends_on api). Add deploy/docker/.env.example documenting FORGEJO_ADMIN_TOKEN.

Acceptance:
`docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml config` validates and lists services forgejo, api, web; `docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d` brings the api container to healthy (healthcheck passing) within the timeout.

Risks / out of scope:
The compose build `context` must be the repo root (../.. from deploy/docker) so pnpm sees the workspace — a common footgun. Real tokens go in .env (gitignored), never .env.example. First-time Forgejo admin/token/repo bootstrap stays the documented manual step (specs/deployment.md lines 68-92), not automated here.

title: Add Dockerfile.web and minimal SPA entry
description:
Parent story: asr-mcx
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#development-docker-compose (web service build + args, lines 50-61); specs/web-ui.md (SPA entry)

Context:
The dev stack serves the React/Vite SPA so the full browser flow can run against the API. packages/web is a Vite app but currently has no HTML entry, so it cannot build. This adds a minimal placeholder entry plus the Dockerfile that builds and statically serves it. The web-ui epic replaces the placeholder App content.

Affected files:
- packages/web/index.html (new — minimal Vite entry)
- packages/web/src/main.tsx (new — mounts a trivial root component)
- deploy/docker/Dockerfile.web (new)

Change:
Add packages/web/index.html (script type=module src /src/main.tsx, a #root div) and packages/web/src/main.tsx rendering a minimal "ASR" root so `vite build` produces dist/index.html. Multi-stage Dockerfile.web: builder FROM node:22-slim with pnpm, install workspace, ARG VITE_API_URL + VITE_AUTH_MODE (exported for the build), `pnpm --filter @asr/web... build`; runner serves packages/web/dist statically (nginx:alpine or node + `serve`) on the dev port 5173.

Acceptance:
`docker build -f deploy/docker/Dockerfile.web --build-arg VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:3001 --build-arg VITE_AUTH_MODE=mock -t asr-web:dev .` exits 0; `docker run --rm -p 5173:5173 asr-web:dev` serves the SPA so `curl -s localhost:5173` returns HTML (status 200) containing the #root div.

Risks / out of scope:
Vite inlines VITE_* at build time — they must be build args, not runtime env. The placeholder main.tsx is intentionally trivial; routes/screens are owned by the web-ui epic (specs/web-ui.md). Keep the runtime server image small.

title: Add Dockerfile.api for the submission service
description:
Parent story: asr-mcx
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#development-docker-compose (api service build, lines 28-48); specs/submission-package.md#composition (serve on PORT, lines 111-142)

Context:
The dev compose stack and the prod Container App both build the submission API from a Dockerfile; none exists yet (compose currently runs Forgejo only). This adds a multi-stage Node 22 image that installs the pnpm workspace, builds @asr/submission, and runs it.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/Dockerfile.api (new)
- .dockerignore (new — node_modules, dist, data, .git)

Change:
Multi-stage Dockerfile. builder FROM node:22-slim: install build-essential + python3 (better-sqlite3 native build), enable corepack/pnpm, COPY pnpm-workspace.yaml + root package.json + packages/core/package.json + packages/submission/package.json, `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`, COPY sources, `pnpm --filter @asr/submission... build`. runner FROM node:22-slim: copy node_modules + packages/*/dist + package manifests, ENV PORT=3001, EXPOSE 3001, CMD ["node","packages/submission/dist/index.js"]. Build context is the repo root.

Acceptance:
`docker build -f deploy/docker/Dockerfile.api -t asr-api:dev .` from the repo root exits 0 and produces an image; `docker run --rm -e NODE_ENV=development -e AUTH_MODE=mock -e DATABASE_PATH=/tmp/w.db -p 3001:3001 asr-api:dev` boots and answers 200 on its liveness route.

Risks / out of scope:
The NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock entrypoint guard (specs/submission-package.md lines 178-187) is owned by asr-nbj — this Dockerfile runs node directly; asr-nbj adds the entrypoint shell guard later. better-sqlite3 is a native module — keep a toolchain in the builder and a matching runtime base. Never bake secrets into the image.

title: Add version download redirect endpoint
description:
Parent story: asr-4it
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload (lines 81-91); specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (publishArtifact URL, lines 168-180)

Context:
The CLI/MCP download path resolves a version to its Forgejo generic-package URL, follows the redirect, and verifies the zip's content hash. This endpoint emits that 302 so artifacts are fetched from Forgejo, not proxied through the API.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/registry.ts (extend registryRoutes)
- packages/submission/src/http/registry.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add GET `/:owner/:name/v/:version/download`: build `location = ${forgejoBase}/api/packages/${owner}/generic/${name}/${version}/skill.zip` where forgejoBase = env.FORGEJO_URL with any trailing /api/v1 stripped (same URL shape as ForgejoClient.publishArtifact); return c.redirect(location, 302). If the resolved version row is marked yanked, also set header X-ASR-Yanked: true (still redirect). When no such published version exists -> apiError(c,404,'submission_not_found').

Acceptance:
vitest integration: GET /api/v1/skills/acme/x/v/1.0.0/download (acme/x v1.0.0 seeded published) returns status 302 and a Location header matching `/api/packages/acme/generic/x/1.0.0/skill.zip`; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The endpoint only redirects — never streams bytes (the artifact lives in Forgejo's package registry). Hash verification happens client-side in the CLI (asr-5wr), not here. The full yank flow is asr-sm0.

title: Add GET /skills list and detail handlers
description:
Parent story: asr-4it
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#get-skills (lines 23-57), #get-skillsownername (lines 59-77), #caching (lines 94-97); specs/submission-package.md#composition (registry routes unauth, line 134)

Context:
Public, unauthenticated read endpoints the web browser and CLI hit to discover skills. They mount before the auth middleware (registry routes are unauth per the composition).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/registry.ts (new — exports `registryRoutes`)
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (mount app.route('/api/v1/skills', registryRoutes) BEFORE any auth middleware)
- packages/submission/src/http/registry.test.ts (new)

Change:
Build `registryRoutes = new Hono()`. GET `/`: parse query q, tag, kind, limit (clamp <=100), cursor (base64-decoded {offset}); call listPublishedSkills; return c.json({ items, nextCursor }) where nextCursor = base64({offset+limit}) when nextOffset!==null else null; set header Cache-Control: public, max-age=60. GET `/:owner/:name`: getPublishedSkill; undefined -> apiError(c,404,'submission_not_found'); else c.json(detail). Mount in index.ts before auth.

Acceptance:
vitest integration (app.request, temp sqlite seeded via the skills repo): GET /api/v1/skills returns 200 with { items:[...], nextCursor } and a Cache-Control header; GET /api/v1/skills/acme/x returns 200 SkillDetail; GET /api/v1/skills/acme/missing returns 404 { error:'submission_not_found' }; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Rate limiting (specs/registry-api.md#rate-limiting) and ETag are deferred to Phase 4 hardening — set Cache-Control only. Personalised ranking via bearer is out of scope. Keep these routes unauthenticated.

title: Add published-skills read repository
description:
Parent story: asr-4it
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#get-skills (lines 23-57), #get-skillsownername (lines 59-77); specs/types.md (SkillSummary/SkillDetail lines 196-212)

Context:
The read side serves published skills to the web UI, CLI and MCP. In Phase 1 the source of truth for "published" is the submissions table (rows whose status_phase='published' after the md-only publish in asr-6hl); the versioning epic later backs this with skill_versions. This repository maps those rows to the canonical SkillSummary/SkillDetail read types.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skills.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skills.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function listPublishedSkills(db, opts: { q?: string; tag?: string; kind?: 'skill'|'persona'; limit?: number; offset?: number }): { items: SkillSummary[]; nextOffset: number | null }` selecting submissions WHERE status_phase='published', parsing manifest_json, filtering by q (name/description/tags LIKE) / tag / kind, ordering by submitted_at desc, applying limit (default 20, max 100) + offset and computing nextOffset. Add `export function getPublishedSkill(db, owner, name): SkillDetail | undefined` aggregating all published rows for that owner(=manifest.author)/name into a SkillDetail with versions: SkillVersion[]. Types from @asr/core (asr-9qp).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skills.test.ts: after inserting two published submission rows (manifest.author='acme', name='x', versions 1.0.0 and 1.1.0), listPublishedSkills({}) returns items length>=1 containing a SkillSummary for acme/x; getPublishedSkill(db,'acme','x') returns a SkillDetail whose versions length===2; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Phase 1 reads from submissions; do NOT build the skill_versions table here (owned by asr-efq, versioning epic). latestVersion uses semver rsort over non-yanked rows — yank handling is out of scope (no yanks in Phase 1). downloadCount may be 0/stubbed.

title: Implement parseSkillManifest in @asr/core parser
description:
Parent story: asr-4bc
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#skill-manifest; code: packages/core/src/parser.ts (lines 4-15)

Context:
The MD-only submission path extracts owner/name/version/permissions from a SKILL.md before pushing to Forgejo. parser.ts today only has the loose parseSkillMd (returns SkillMeta, no validation, defaults missing fields). This adds a strict parse that yields a validated SkillManifest with field-level errors so a malformed submission is rejected with a useful message instead of silently defaulting.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/parser.ts (add new export below line 15; keep parseSkillMd and generateAgentsMd unchanged)
- packages/core/src/parser.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export function parseSkillManifest(content: string): { manifest: SkillManifest; body: string }`. Use gray-matter (already a dependency, imported line 1) to split YAML frontmatter from the markdown body; if `matter` returns an empty `data` object (no frontmatter keys) throw `new Error('SKILL.md is missing YAML frontmatter')`. Run the frontmatter object through skillManifestSchema (from ./manifest-schema.js); on ZodError, throw an Error whose message concatenates each issue's `path.join('.')` + ': ' + message (field-level errors). Return { manifest: parsed, body: body.trim() }.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/parser.test.ts: parseSkillManifest on an inline valid SKILL.md fixture (frontmatter with name/version/author/description/tags/permissions + a markdown body) returns manifest.name and manifest.version equal to the fixture values and body equal to the trimmed markdown; parseSkillManifest on a fixture string with no frontmatter throws /missing YAML frontmatter/; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not remove or alter parseSkillMd (the CLI in packages/cli still imports it via @asr/core). No network or filesystem access — operate on the passed string only. Fixtures are inline template strings, no fixture files.

title: Add zod SkillManifest schema to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4bc
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#skill-manifest (lines 5-48)

Context:
The submission write-path must turn an untrusted YAML manifest into a typed SkillManifest with unknown-field rejection (strict mode) before anything else runs. specs/types.md line 48: "The Hono validation layer parses raw manifest.yaml through a zod schema and emits a typed SkillManifest. Unknown fields are rejected (strict mode)." This schema is the reusable validator the parser (next task) and the API both call.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/manifest-schema.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (add `export * from './manifest-schema.js';` after line 2)
- packages/core/package.json (add "zod": "^3.23.0" to dependencies)

Change:
Add `skillManifestSchema` built with zod, a `.strict()` object mirroring SkillManifest from specs/types.md: name/version/author/description z.string(); tags z.array(z.string()); kind z.enum(['skill','persona']).default('skill'); persona_mode z.enum(['inject','delegate']).optional(); references/entrypoint/dependencies optional; permissions a nested strict object { network: z.boolean(), networkHosts: z.array(z.string()).optional(), filesystem: z.enum(['none','read-own','read-write-own']), subprocess: z.boolean(), environment: z.array(z.string()) }; compatibility an optional nested strict object { 'claude-code'?: string, codex?: string }. Add a superRefine: persona_mode required iff kind==='persona' (default 'inject'); networkHosts present implies network must be true. Export `skillManifestSchema`. The parsed output must be assignable to the canonical SkillManifest from asr-5up.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/manifest-schema.test.ts: skillManifestSchema.parse(validManifestObj) returns an object with kind==='skill' applied by default; skillManifestSchema.parse({...valid, bogusField:1}) throws a ZodError; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT redefine the SkillManifest interface here — import it from ./types.js (added by asr-5up) and let zod's output conform to it. No SKILL.md/file parsing in this task (that is the parser task). Keep `.strict()` so unknown fields are rejected — relaxing it is a security regression.

title: Add type-level test asserting all canonical @asr/core types compile
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md (whole) — story acceptance "a consumer importing { SubmissionStatus, SkillManifest, ScanReport } type-checks"

Context:
The story's acceptance requires that a consumer can import the canonical types and that they type-check together (SubmissionStatus narrowing, ScanReport embedding). A compile-time test in @asr/core locks this in and guards against future duplicate or drifted definitions.

Affected files:
- packages/core/test/types.test.ts (new)
- packages/core/vitest.config.ts (only if typecheck must be enabled; otherwise leave untouched)

Change:
Add packages/core/test/types.test.ts importing { SkillManifest, Submission, SubmissionStatus, ScanReport, ScanFinding, Questionnaire, AuditEvent, AUDIT_ACTIONS, VersionDiff, SkillDetail, RegistryIndex, MarketplaceManifest } from '../src/index.js'. Construct one valid literal of each, and add an exhaustive switch (status.phase) over SubmissionStatus with a const _exhaustive: never = status default to force compile-time exhaustiveness. Runtime body trivial (expect(true).toBe(true)); the value is tsc compiling the file.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes including types.test.ts; deliberately deleting one SubmissionStatus member from src/types.ts makes the never assignment fail to compile (verified once, then reverted).

Risks / out of scope:
No runtime validation libraries; this is a compile-time guard. Keep sample literals minimal. Do not add new production types here.

title: Add VersionDiff type to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#versiondiff-canonical-type; re-export per specs/types.md#version-diff

Context:
VersionDiff is computed before classification so the workflow can branch on permissionsExpanded; it is re-exported from @asr/core/types per specs/types.md#version-diff. It references PermissionsManifest, so the manifest types must exist first.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append; do NOT modify lines 1-52)

Change:
Append verbatim from specs/versioning.md#versiondiff-canonical-type: export interface VersionDiff (skillName, fromVersion, toVersion, fromContentHash: string|null, toContentHash, filesAdded: string[], filesRemoved: string[], filesModified: string[], dependenciesAdded: Record<string,string>, dependenciesRemoved: Record<string,string>, dependenciesChanged: Record<string,{from:string;to:string}>, permissionsBefore: PermissionsManifest|null, permissionsAfter: PermissionsManifest, permissionsExpanded, manifestKindChanged, riskAssessment: 'low'|'medium'|'high', computedAt).

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds and importing { VersionDiff } from '@asr/core' type-checks.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement computeVersionDiff(), the version_diffs table, or risk computation — those are owned by the Versioning epic asr-cg4. Type only.

title: Add published-artifact, registry, and marketplace read types to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#skill-published-artifact-distinct-from-submission; #registry-index; #marketplace-manifest

Context:
The read side (registry-api asr-4it, MCP server, CLI) consumes published artifacts, the serialised registry index, and the marketplace manifest. These are distinct from the write-side Submission. They reference SkillManifest and SkillKind, so the manifest types must exist first.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append; do NOT modify lines 1-52)

Change:
Append verbatim from specs/types.md: export interface SkillVersion (owner, name, version, contentHash, publishedAt, publishedBy, approvedBy: string|null, prNumber, mergeCommit, yanked, yankedAt?, yankReason?, riskAssessment: 'low'|'medium'|'high'); export interface SkillSummary (owner, name, latestVersion, description, tags, kind: SkillKind, publishedAt, downloadCount, riskAssessmentLatest); export interface SkillDetail extends SkillSummary (manifestLatest: SkillManifest, versions: SkillVersion[]); export interface RegistryIndex (generatedAt, specVersion: '1', skills: SkillSummary[]); export interface MarketplacePlugin (name, version, description, path, kind: SkillKind); export interface MarketplaceManifest (name, version, plugins).

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds and importing { SkillDetail, RegistryIndex, MarketplaceManifest } from '@asr/core' type-checks.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT add the registry.json serialiser or marketplace sync logic (registry-api / CLI epics). Do not collide with or remove the legacy Skill interface at types.ts:9 — these are the separate SkillVersion/SkillSummary/SkillDetail family.

title: Add Questionnaire types to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#questionnaire

Context:
The questionnaire HITL node serves a static, versioned security question set to submitters; these types shape the request/response stored against a submission. Needed before the workflow questionnaire endpoint (asr-kkd) and the generator in @asr/submission.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append; do NOT modify lines 1-52)

Change:
Append verbatim from specs/types.md#questionnaire: export interface QuestionnaireQuestion (id, text, type: 'boolean'|'text'|'select', options?, required); export interface QuestionnaireResponse (questionId, answer: string|boolean); export interface Questionnaire (id, submissionId, questions, responses?, completedAt?).

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds and importing { Questionnaire, QuestionnaireQuestion } from '@asr/core' type-checks.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement generateSecurityQuestions() or questionnaireSetVersion handling — those live in @asr/submission and are owned by the workflow story. Types only.

title: Add audit.ts AUDIT_ACTIONS enum and AuditEvent type to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-4ea
Parent epic:  asr-dft
Spec ref:     specs/types.md#audit; specs/audit.md (action enum, lines 43-77)

Context:
AuditEvent is one link in the HMAC-chained audit log; its action field is constrained to the closed AUDIT_ACTIONS set. The const + type must live in @asr/core/audit so every emitter shares one enum. Defining it here unblocks both this types story and the audit-schema story asr-a03.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/audit.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/types.ts (append AuditEvent)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (add export * from './audit.js';)

Change:
Create packages/core/src/audit.ts with export const AUDIT_ACTIONS = [...] as const; (the full 25-action list from specs/audit.md lines 43-76: submission.*, workflow.*, scan.finding, version.*, hash.blocked, token.rotated, key.rotated, audit.anchored, audit.verify.failed) and export type AuditAction = typeof AUDIT_ACTIONS[number];. In types.ts append import type { AuditAction } from './audit.js'; and export interface AuditEvent (id, submissionId|null, skillName|null, version|null, timestamp, actor, actorType: 'user'|'system'|'compliance', action: AuditAction, detail: Record<string,unknown>, prevHash, hash, hmacKeyId). Add the audit re-export line to index.ts.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/core build` succeeds, `rg "audit.verify.failed" packages/core/src/audit.ts` returns one line, and importing { AUDIT_ACTIONS, AuditAction, AuditEvent } from '@asr/core' type-checks.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT create the SQLite audit_events table, the audit.emit() helper, or HMAC chaining — those belong to asr-a03 and the Audit Trail epic asr-62i. This task is the type/const surface only.

title: Story: SLA timeout/expiry scheduler (extend, escalate, auto-reject)
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry

Goal: Add a periodic scheduler in packages/submission that scans HITL stage deadlines and applies the per-stage policy: Questionnaire 7d -> notify + extend 7d -> auto-reject; User confirmation 14d -> auto-reject; Compliance review 30d -> escalate to admin + extend 7d -> auto-reject. Each expiry emits workflow.review.rejected (or the stage auto-reject event) with detail.reason='timeout' and triggers the notifier.

Acceptance: an integration test with clock control shows a questionnaire past 7d gets one extension then auto-rejects on the second pass; a review past 30d escalates to admin; each emits an audit event with detail.reason='timeout'.

title: Story: Container Apps definitions with SQLite nobrl mount, CORS, and Key Vault secrets
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: specs/deployment.md#container-apps; specs/deployment.md#cors; specs/deployment.md#secrets-management

Goal: Define the three Container Apps (forgejo internal min=max=1; api external min1/max3 with NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=entra; web external min0/max3) as Bicep/az config. The api app mounts the api-data Azure Files volume with mountOptions=nobrl (hard rule), pulls forgejo-upload-token, forgejo-merge-token, and audit-hmac-key from Key Vault as Container App secrets, and has CORS limited to the web origin (+ localhost:5173) with Authorization,Content-Type headers.

Acceptance: deploying the app templates produces an api Container App whose volume shows mountOptions=nobrl and whose secrets resolve from Key Vault; 'curl -i https://<api-fqdn>/healthz' returns 200 and an OPTIONS preflight from the web origin returns the configured Access-Control-Allow-* headers.

title: Story: Azure infrastructure Bicep templates (managed env, storage, file shares, logs)
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: specs/deployment.md#infrastructure-bicep

Goal: Author the Bicep templates under deploy/azure/ that provision the production substrate: a Microsoft.App/managedEnvironments env wired to a Log Analytics workspace, a StorageV2 account, two Azure Files shares (forgejo-data 50Gi, api-data 10Gi), and the two managedEnvironments/storages bindings (forgejo-storage, api-storage) referencing those shares. No app definitions here (separate story).

Acceptance: 'az bicep build --file deploy/azure/main.bicep' compiles with no errors and the emitted ARM JSON contains the managedEnvironment, the storage account, both fileShares, and both managedEnvironments/storages resources.

title: Story: Audit view endpoints with read-side role authorization
description:
Epic: asr-62i
Spec: specs/audit.md#per-skill--per-user-views; specs/audit.md#read-side-authorization

Goal: Implement GET /audit/skill/:owner/:name, GET /audit/skill/:owner/:name/v:version, GET /audit/user/:sub, GET /audit/submission/:id, and GET /audit/verify, each querying the indexed audit_events table and returning events in chronological order. Enforce the read-side matrix: /audit/submission/:id readable by the submission's submitter OR Compliance/Admin; /audit/skill/* Compliance/Admin only; /audit/user/* Admin only; /audit/verify Admin only and rate-limited (full table scan).

Acceptance: integration — GET /audit/submission/:id as the owning submitter returns 200 with that submission's events; the same call as an unrelated user returns 403; GET /audit/user/:sub returns 403 for Compliance and 200 for Admin; GET /audit/skill/:owner/:name returns only events for that skill across all its versions.

title: Story: Chain verification endpoint + degraded mode on break
description:
Epic: asr-62i
Spec: specs/audit.md#chain-verification

Goal: Implement verifyChain(db, keys) walking audit_events ORDER BY rowid, asserting prev_hash linkage (expectedPrev starts at 64 zeros) and recomputing each hash via the row's hmac_key_id (missing key => valid:false reason 'unknown key'), returning {valid:false,brokenAt,reason} or {valid:true,eventCount,lastHash}. On any valid:false result the API enters degraded mode: all write endpoints return 503 audit_chain_broken, an audit.verify.failed event is emitted (itself audited), and GET /audit/verify surfaces the break. Backs the epic success criterion 'tampering with one row fails verification'.

Acceptance: integration — verifyChain over a clean emitted sequence returns valid:true with the correct eventCount; mutating one audit_events row's detail or hash makes verifyChain return valid:false with brokenAt = that row id, and a subsequent write endpoint returns 503 audit_chain_broken.

title: Story: audit.emit chokepoint with atomic HMAC hash chaining
description:
Epic: asr-62i
Spec: specs/audit.md#event-types-closed-enum; specs/audit.md#hash-chain

Goal: Implement the single audit.emit helper (in packages/submission/, over @asr/core AuditEvent + AuditAction) that validates action against AUDIT_ACTIONS at runtime (defensive against dynamic dispatch), generates a ULID id + ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, reads prev_hash as the previous row's hash (genesis = 64 zeros), computes hash via createHmac('sha256', key) over the spec's pipe-joined payload (id|submissionId|skillName|version|timestamp|actor|action|JSON(detail)|prevHash), and inserts the audit row inside the SAME better-sqlite3 transaction as the state mutation it documents so the audit row and workflow row land together or not at all. Uses a single env key for now (hmac_key_id from AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_ID); KeyRing rotation is a separate story.

Acceptance: unit — two sequential audit.emit calls produce events where event2.prev_hash === event1.hash and each hash equals computeHash over the documented payload; emitting an action not in AUDIT_ACTIONS throws before any DB write; a wrapping transaction that rolls back leaves zero audit rows (no orphan audit row).

title: Story: audit_events schema + closed AUDIT_ACTIONS enum
description:
Epic: asr-62i
Spec: specs/audit.md#schema; specs/audit.md#event-types-closed-enum; specs/types.md#audit

Goal: Add the migration that creates the audit_events table (ULID pk, nullable submission_id FK -> submissions(id), denormalised skill_name/version, actor + actor_type CHECK in ('user','system','compliance'), action, detail JSON, prev_hash/hash/hmac_key_id) plus the five indexes (idx_audit_submission, idx_audit_skill, idx_audit_actor, idx_audit_action, idx_audit_timestamp), and define the closed AUDIT_ACTIONS const + AuditAction literal-union type in a new packages/core/src/audit.ts re-exported from packages/core/src/index.ts, backing the canonical AuditEvent interface in specs/types.md#audit. Builds on the Phase 1 migration framework (asr-7jd) and canonical-types story (asr-4ea).

Acceptance: migration up creates audit_events with all five idx_audit_* indexes (PRAGMA index_list assertion in a vitest case); importing AUDIT_ACTIONS from @asr/core yields exactly the spec's action set and AuditAction is a string-literal union; inserting a row with an unknown actor_type rejects on the CHECK constraint.

title: Story: MCP compliance review tools (review_queue, review_decision) with SoD guard
description:
Epic: asr-2jw
Spec: specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory; specs/mcp.md#tool-definitions; specs/workflow.md#separation-of-duties
Module: packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/ proxying the workflow approve/reject path and packages/submission/src/auth/separation.ts

Goal: Implement the two Compliance-role tools: review_queue (list pending compliance reviews) and review_decision (approve|reject one submission, mutating). review_decision delivers the HITL signal into the workflow and enforces separation of duties (submitter != approver) in addition to the workflow node. Role enforcement is inside the handler so a Submitter calling review_decision gets insufficient_permissions (-32001).

Acceptance: integration tests show a Submitter calling review_decision returns -32001; a Compliance principal approving a submission they themselves submitted returns a separation-of-duties error; a Compliance principal approving a different submitter's submission transitions it to published.

title: Story: MCP read-only registry tools (search/info/list/versions/download_url)
description:
Epic: asr-2jw
Spec: specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory; specs/mcp.md#tool-definitions
Module: packages/submission/src/mcp/tools/ (one file per tool), proxying packages/submission/src/http/registry.ts

Goal: Implement the five Submitter-role read tools that expose the published registry to agents: registry_search (query/tag/author/kind/limit), registry_info (manifest + versions for one skill), registry_list (filtered listing), registry_versions (all non-yanked versions), and registry_download_url (returns { url, contentHash:'sha256:...', sizeBytes, manifest, expiresAt } for a specific or latest non-yanked version). All tools are read-only from the user's machine; they return URLs/metadata, never write to disk. A yanked version requested via registry_download_url returns version_yanked (-32004).

Acceptance: integration tests show registry_search returns published skills matching a query; registry_download_url for a live version returns a url plus 'sha256:' contentHash and a SkillManifest; registry_download_url for a yanked version returns error code -32004.

title: Story: MCP error envelope + per-tool role enforcement helper
description:
Epic: asr-2jw
Spec: specs/mcp.md#error-envelope; specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory
Module: packages/submission/src/mcp/ (shared error/role helpers), aligned with packages/submission/src/http/errors.ts

Goal: Provide the shared MCP error envelope and the per-tool role guard used by every tool handler. Map ASR error conditions to the documented JSON-RPC codes: insufficient_permissions (-32001), authentication_required (-32002), resource_not_found (-32003), version_yanked (-32004), rate_limited (-32005), audit_chain_broken (-32006), internal_error (-32099, trace id only, no internals leaked). Role enforcement happens inside the tool handler (not transport middleware), so an authenticated Submitter calling a Compliance-only tool gets -32001 with data { required, actual }.

Acceptance: unit tests assert each error condition produces the correct JSON-RPC error code and data payload; a Submitter principal invoking a Compliance-gated tool through the role guard receives code -32001 with data.required='Compliance' and data.actual=['Submitter']; internal_error responses contain only a trace id in data.

title: Story: MCP authentication — reuse Entra middleware + session-principal binding + dev mock
description:
Epic: asr-2jw
Spec: specs/mcp.md#authentication
Module: packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts reusing packages/submission/src/auth/{entra.ts,mock.ts}

Goal: Gate the /mcp surface with the SAME Entra bearer validation as the REST API. The initialize call requires Authorization: Bearer <jwt> with access_as_user scope and at least the Submitter role; the issued Mcp-Session-Id is bound to that principal and reused for subsequent requests. The MCP server issues no tokens of its own. When AUTH_MODE=mock, token validation is skipped and the mock principal is injected.

Acceptance: an integration test shows a tool call without a bearer token returns authentication_required (-32002); a call with a valid Submitter token binds the session to the principal and succeeds; in AUTH_MODE=mock the same call succeeds with the injected mock principal and no token.

title: Story: MCP Streamable HTTP transport + server bootstrap
description:
Epic: asr-2jw
Spec: specs/mcp.md#transport
Module: packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (mcpHandler), mounted via app.all('/mcp', ...) in packages/submission/src/index.ts

Goal: Stand up the MCP server using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk Streamable HTTP transport behind a single /mcp route. POST /mcp handles tool invocations (JSON), GET /mcp returns the SSE result stream for a session. On initialize the server issues an opaque Mcp-Session-Id header and advertises protocol version 2025-06-18; clients echo the session id on subsequent requests. Older clients are rejected with unsupported_protocol_version.

Acceptance: an integration test performs the initialize handshake against POST /mcp and asserts the response carries an Mcp-Session-Id header and protocolVersion '2025-06-18'; a client advertising an older protocol version receives an unsupported_protocol_version error; a follow-up GET /mcp with the issued session id opens the SSE stream.

title: Story: Generic package registry wiring — publish-time skill.zip upload + download-URL resolution
description:
Epic: asr-klk
Spec: SPEC.md#phase-3-cli--registry-integration-week-56; specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload; specs/git-integration.md (generic package upload)

Goal: Wire the publish path to upload the canonical skill artifact to the Forgejo generic package registry at /api/packages/{owner}/generic/{name}/{version}/skill.zip using the ForgejoClient package-upload primitive (asr-she), and ensure the Registry download endpoint (asr-4it) resolves its 302 Location to that URL. This is the glue that makes published artifacts downloadable by 'asr install'.

Acceptance: an integration test publishes a version and then GETs /skills/:owner/:name/v/:version/download, receiving a 302 whose Location points at the generic package URL that actually serves the uploaded skill.zip whose canonical SHA-256 equals the version contentHash. Forgejo only; reuses the asr-she upload primitive (no new client method if avoidable).

title: Story: Marketplace repo sync job on publish/yank (marketplace.json + per-plugin files via PR)
description:
Epic: asr-klk
Spec: specs/cli-integration.md#marketplace-distribution, #sync-job; specs/types.md#marketplace-manifest

Goal: Implement the marketplaceSync job in packages/submission, triggered by version.published and version.yanked audit events. It reads registry.json, regenerates marketplace.json (MarketplaceManifest) and per-plugin files (plugins/{name}/.claude-plugin/plugin.json, .codex-plugin/plugin.json, skills/{name}/SKILL.md), opens a PR against the (separate) marketplace repo with the merge-bot identity, and auto-merges if CI passes. On failure it emits marketplace_sync.failed and pages on-call (rate-limited to 1/hour/skill).

Acceptance: an integration test fires version.published and asserts a PR is opened on the marketplace repo adding plugins/{name}/... and a marketplace.json entry; firing version.yanked removes/updates the entry; a forced sync failure emits marketplace_sync.failed exactly once within the rate-limit window. Forgejo only.

title: Story: CLI distribution — tsup single-ESM bundle, Forgejo Releases release.yml, install.sh/install.ps1
description:
Epic: asr-klk
Spec: DESIGN.md#build-strategy, #install-scripts, #release-workflow-forgejoworkflowsreleaseyml

Goal: Configure tsup to bundle the CLI + @asr/core into a single self-contained packages/cli/dist/asr.mjs (node>=20, no native deps). Add .forgejo/workflows/release.yml triggering on v* tags: build, compute SHA-256 -> asr.mjs.sha256, create a Forgejo Release via POST /api/v1/repos/:o/:r/releases, upload asr.mjs + .sha256 + install.sh + install.ps1 as assets. Add scripts/install.sh and scripts/install.ps1 that pin a version (or latest), download from Forgejo Releases, verify SHA-256 before installing, and write a launcher.

Acceptance: 'pnpm build' emits packages/cli/dist/asr.mjs that runs 'node dist/asr.mjs --help'; install.sh aborts on a deliberately wrong asr.mjs.sha256 and succeeds on a matching one (verified in a shell test); release.yml lints/validates as a Forgejo Actions workflow. No GitHub release path.

title: Story: CLI publish — asr publish [--watch], status, submissions, local validation + content classification
description:
Epic: asr-klk
Spec: specs/cli-integration.md#commands; DESIGN.md#content-classification; specs/api.md#post-submissions

Goal: Implement 'asr publish [<dir>]' to zip the skill dir and POST it to /submissions, returning the submission id; validate manifest.yaml locally (against the @asr/core schema) and classify content (any file outside the content-only whitelist => requires_approval) to fail fast before upload. 'asr publish --watch' streams status until terminal. 'asr status <submission-id>' fetches current status; 'asr submissions' lists the caller's submissions. Requires an authenticated principal (or dev-mode skip).

Acceptance: against a stubbed submission API, 'asr publish ./md-skill' returns 201 and prints {id,status}; an invalid manifest fails locally before any HTTP call; 'asr publish --watch' on a code skill streams status transitions to a terminal state; 'asr status <id>' and 'asr submissions' render the documented JSON. Covered by integration tests.

title: Story: CLI install/update/remove/list — agent detection, lockfile, hash verification
description:
Epic: asr-klk
Spec: specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets; specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload

Goal: Implement 'asr install <owner/skill>[@version]' (refuses yanked; --global, --agent claude|codex|both), 'asr update [<owner/skill>]', 'asr remove', 'asr list'. Detect target agent (explicit --agent -> .claude/ or .codex/ present -> both) and install to .claude/skills/{name}/ or .codex/skills/{name}/ (project) or ~ scope. Maintain .agent/asr.lock.json (version, content-hash, install timestamp, source URL). Follow the 302 download redirect to the generic package URL and verify the zip's canonical SHA-256 against the registry contentHash, aborting with version.hash.mismatch on mismatch.

Acceptance: against a stubbed registry + package URL, 'asr install owner/skill' writes files under the detected scope and records an asr.lock.json entry; installing a tampered zip (wrong hash) aborts and emits version.hash.mismatch to stderr; installing a yanked version is refused; 'asr update' bumps to latest non-yanked and prints a diff summary; 'asr list' shows project+global installs. Covered by integration tests.

title: Story: CLI auth — asr login device-code flow, token storage, whoami/logout, dev-mode skip
description:
Epic: asr-klk
Spec: specs/cli-integration.md#authentication, #device-code-flow-rfc-8628, #token-storage, #dev-mode; DESIGN.md#cli-authentication

Goal: Implement the OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant (RFC 8628) against Entra ID in packages/cli. 'asr login' prints the verification URL + user code, polls the token endpoint (5s interval, max 15m, configurable via ASR_DEVICE_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS/ASR_DEVICE_POLL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS, doubling to 30s on rate-limit), then caches access+refresh tokens in the OS keyring (keytar on macOS/Windows, libsecret/0600 file fallback on Linux). 'asr whoami' shows identity + roles; 'asr logout' clears cached tokens; access-token expiry triggers silent refresh then falls back to device code. When ASR_URL is non-HTTPS (localhost) auth is skipped entirely.

Acceptance: against a mock Entra token endpoint, 'asr login' completes and reports 'Logged in as <user> (roles: ...)'; 'asr whoami' prints the same identity; 'asr logout' removes the cached token so 'asr whoami' reports signed-out; with ASR_URL=http://localhost:* no token is requested or stored. Covered by integration tests with a stubbed device-code server.

title: Story: Record published version (skill_versions row + .publish-record.json)
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
Spec: specs/versioning.md#invariant-every-published-change-is-a-versioned-commit-in-the-repo; specs/versioning.md#storage; specs/versioning.md#git-history-as-source-of-truth

Goal: On a successful publish (consumed by the Phase 1 publish path asr-6hl), write the queryable skill_versions row (content_hash, submission_id, published_by, approved_by, pr_number, merge_commit, scan_report_id) and commit .publish-record.json into the merge commit containing the canonical hash, scan report id, approver, and Flowcraft run id — so a version is independently verifiable from Git even if SQLite is wiped.

Acceptance: integration — after a version publishes, a skill_versions row exists with the merge_commit and content_hash, and git show <tag>:skills/<owner>/<name>/.publish-record.json returns JSON containing the same canonical hash plus scan report id, approver, and run id.

title: Story: Content-hash dedup and blocked-hash rejection on submission
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
Spec: specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash; specs/versioning.md#version-rules; specs/versioning.md#re-publish-after-yank

Goal: On submission, compute the canonical hash and enforce: return 409 if the hash already exists on any prior submission (including rejected), and reject (4xx with reason) if the hash is present in blocked_hashes regardless of source/severity. This implements security must-have: rejected/yanked content cannot be re-published under a new version.

Acceptance: integration — submitting content whose canonical hash matches a prior submission returns 409; submitting content whose hash is in blocked_hashes returns a 4xx naming the blocking reason/source; submitting novel content is accepted and proceeds.

title: Story: Versioning storage migration (skill_versions, version_diffs, blocked_hashes)
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
Spec: specs/versioning.md#storage

Goal: Add a forward-only SQLite migration (using the framework from asr-7jd) creating skill_versions (PK skill_name,version; content_hash, submission_id, pr_number, merge_commit, scan_report_id, yanked_at/by/reason), version_diffs (PK submission_id; diff_json, risk CHECK low/medium/high), and blocked_hashes (PK content_hash; source CHECK rejected/yanked/incident), plus idx_versions_hash, idx_versions_pub, and the partial idx_versions_yanked.

Acceptance: vitest — running migrations on a temp DB creates all three tables with the documented columns and indexes; a duplicate (skill_name, version) insert is rejected by the primary key; an invalid risk/source value is rejected by the CHECK; re-running migrations is a no-op.

title: Story: Semver version-format and version-rule validators in @asr/core
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
