Spec: specs/versioning.md#version-format; specs/versioning.md#version-rules

Goal: Add strict semver 2.0 helpers in @asr/core backed by a single pinned semver (^7.6.0) dependency shared across all packages: parse/validate MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH(-prerelease), compare, gt (no-downgrade), and rsort. Expose validators the submission API uses to enforce the version rules: valid format, no downgrade vs current published, correct pre-release ordering. (Duplicate-name@version and blocked-hash rules are enforced in their own stories against storage.)

Acceptance: vitest in @asr/core — an invalid version string is rejected; gt("1.0.0","1.1.0") is false so a downgrade is flagged; 1.0.0-beta.1 orders before 1.0.0; rsort returns descending semver order; exactly one semver version is resolved across the workspace.

title: Story: Canonical SHA-256 content-hash primitive in @asr/core
description:
Epic: asr-cg4
Spec: specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash

Goal: Implement canonicalHash(files): string in @asr/core/hash, computing a deterministic SHA-256 over a canonical representation of a skill's files: entries sorted by UTF-8 path, each contributing path + mode (0644/0755) + fixed mtime (2000-01-01T00:00:00Z) + uncompressed size (uint64 BE) + SHA-256 of content. Excludes .DS_Store, __MACOSX, .git/, and classifier-stripped files. This is the version-identity primitive consumed by Phase 1 publish, dedup, and the blocked-hashes table.

Acceptance: vitest in @asr/core — two file sets with identical content but different insertion order and differing real mtimes produce the same hash; adding a .DS_Store/__MACOSX entry does not change the hash; flipping one content byte changes it; pnpm --filter @asr/core test green.

title: Story: Per-skill workflow mutex + crash-resume
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/workflow.md#per-skill-mutex; specs/workflow.md#crash-recovery-resume; specs/security.md#race-condition-prevention

Goal: Add the pending_versions and publish_locks tables and enforce the per-(skill_name,version) mutex inside the same transaction as the submissions insert (duplicate -> 409 version_in_progress), the per-skill advisory publish lock before merge, and resumeWorkflows() on service startup that re-enters every non-terminal submission into the Flowcraft engine. Compute nodes carry idempotency keys (submissionId,nodeName,attempt) and treat Forgejo 409 as already-done.

Acceptance: an integration test shows two concurrent submissions for the same skill+version return 201 then 409 version_in_progress, and a test that restarts the workflow mid-pipeline shows resumeWorkflows() drives the surviving submission to a terminal state without duplicate Forgejo branches.

title: Story: Compliance approval UI (review queue + approval detail decision)
description:
Epic: asr-921
Spec: specs/web-ui.md#approval-detail-reviewid; specs/web-ui.md (routes /review, /review/:id); specs/web-ui.md#acceptance-tests-e2e

Goal: Build the React routes /review (approval queue, Compliance/Admin only) and /review/:id (Diff/Dependencies/Permissions/Scan/Audit tabs + sticky decision panel). The decision panel calls POST /approve and POST /reject (reason required for reject) and disables both buttons when submitter sub == reviewer sub. Routes are role-gated client-side and re-gated by the API.

Acceptance: Playwright E2E asserts the Approve/Reject buttons are disabled when submitter sub == reviewer sub (web-ui.md acceptance test #4), and that approving a pending submission removes it from the /review queue.

title: Story: docker-compose dev stack (Forgejo + API + Web)
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/deployment.md#development-docker-compose, #first-time-setup-dev; deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml

Goal: A docker-compose dev stack bringing up Forgejo (codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:15), the submission API (Node 22, AUTH_MODE=mock), and the web SPA on a shared network with documented env/ports (Forgejo 3000, API 3001, Web 5173) so the e2e smoke can run.

Acceptance: `docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d` brings all three services healthy; `pnpm test:e2e` (scripts/e2e-docker.mjs) passes against the stack.

title: Story: Registry read API — list, detail, download
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/registry-api.md#get-skills, #get-skillsownername, #download (GET /skills/:owner/:name/v/:version/download)

Goal: Read-side endpoints serving published skills: GET /skills (paginated list with query params), GET /skills/:owner/:name (detail incl. versions), and the version download endpoint proxying the Forgejo generic package.

Acceptance: after a skill publishes, curl GET /skills lists it; GET /skills/:owner/:name returns detail with >=1 version; the download endpoint streams the artifact with the documented content-type; integration test green.

title: Story: SKILL.md manifest parser in @asr/core
description:
Epic: asr-dft
Spec: specs/types.md#skill-manifest; code: packages/core/src/parser.ts

Goal: Parse a SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter + markdown body) into a validated SkillManifest with field-level errors. Used by the submission flow to extract owner/name/version/metadata for the MD-only path.

Acceptance: vitest in @asr/core parses a fixture SKILL.md into the expected SkillManifest and rejects a frontmatter-missing fixture with a descriptive error; `pnpm test` green.

title: Epic: Audit Trail Integrity (HMAC chain + git-tag anchoring)
description:
Cross-cutting capability: tamper-evident audit trail (specs/audit.md). Spans Phase 1 (schema) through Phase 4 (anchoring).

Goal: Every workflow action emits a verifiable, tamper-evident audit event; the chain is externally anchorable.

In scope:
- audit_events schema + closed action enum (specs/audit.md#schema, #event-types-closed-enum)
- audit.emit single chokepoint over the closed enum
- HMAC hash chain + chain verification (specs/audit.md#hash-chain, #chain-verification)
- HMAC KeyRing rotation (specs/audit.md#hmac-key-management)
- External anchoring to a signed Git tag, at least hourly (specs/audit.md#external-anchoring)
- Per-skill / per-user views + read-side authorization (specs/audit.md#per-skill--per-user-views, #read-side-authorization)
- Retention vs GDPR handling (specs/audit.md#retention--gdpr)

Out of scope (other epics): Phase 1 owns the submissions table + migration framework; this epic owns audit_events and the chain. Workflow nodes (Phase 2) call audit.emit but the emit contract lives here.

Success criteria: chain-verification passes over a sequence of emitted events; tampering with one row fails verification; an hourly job anchors the chain head to a signed Forgejo Git tag.

Depends on: Phase 1 (core types + SQLite migration framework).

title: Epic: Versioning & Content Integrity (semver, content hash, yank)
description:
Cross-cutting capability: the versioning invariant and content integrity (specs/versioning.md).

Invariant: every published change is a new semver version landing as a Git commit + tag + skill_versions row + .publish-record.json + audit entry + marketplace-sync run. No in-place mutation.

In scope:
- Strict semver version format + version rules (specs/versioning.md#version-format, #version-rules)
- Canonical SHA-256 content hash over a canonical zip (specs/versioning.md#canonical-content-hash)
- Rejected-content blocklist keyed by canonical hash (SPEC.md security must-have #8)
- skill_versions storage + update flow + re-approval matrix (specs/versioning.md#storage, #update-flow, #re-approval-matrix)
- VersionDiff type + diff presentation (specs/versioning.md#versiondiff-canonical-type, #diff-presentation)
- Latest-version resolution + concurrent-submission handling (specs/versioning.md#latest-version-resolution, #concurrent-submissions)
- Yank flow + endpoint + re-publish after yank (specs/versioning.md#yank-flow-security-incident-response)
- CLI contract for version/diff/yank (specs/versioning.md#cli-contract)

Out of scope (other epics): Phase 1 publishes the first version using the content-hash primitive; the advanced diff/yank surface is this epic. Audit emission lives in the Audit epic.

Success criteria: two uploads of identical content produce the same canonical hash; a blocklisted hash is rejected; a yank marks a version unavailable and records a new audit-tracked event; VersionDiff renders between two versions.

Depends on: Phase 1 (core types + Forgejo client + schema).

title: Epic: MCP Server Interface (agent-facing registry tools)
description:
Cross-cutting capability: the MCP server that exposes the registry to Claude Code / Codex as tools (specs/mcp.md).

Goal: An MCP client can authenticate and call registry tools (search/get/install metadata) over the documented transport.

In scope:
- MCP transport + server bootstrap (specs/mcp.md#transport)
- MCP authentication (Entra token validation on the MCP surface) (specs/mcp.md#authentication)
- Tool inventory + tool definitions (specs/mcp.md#tool-inventory, #tool-definitions)
- Error envelope + rate limiting + telemetry (specs/mcp.md#error-envelope, #rate-limiting, #telemetry)
- Client configuration docs (specs/mcp.md#client-configuration)

Out of scope (other epics): underlying Registry read API (Phase 1 owns GET /skills etc.; MCP calls it), marketplace repo + asr CLI (Phase 3), versioning semantics (Versioning epic).

Success criteria: an MCP client configured per specs/mcp.md authenticates and lists/searches skills via MCP tools, receiving the documented error envelope on failure.

Depends on: Phase 2 (Entra ID validation + a published-skill read path).

title: Epic: Phase 4 — Hardening & Production (Azure + SLA)
description:
Phase 4 of the ASR plan (SPEC.md#phase-4-hardening--production-week-78).

Goal: Production-ready on Azure Container Apps with SLA discipline.

In scope:
- Remaining scanners enabled (Veracode optional) (specs/security-scanning.md)
- SLA timeouts + notification emails (specs/workflow.md)
- Azure Container Apps deployment via Bicep templates, ACR mirroring, SQLite-on-Azure-Files nobrl mount (specs/deployment.md)
- Penetration testing of the zip upload path (process checklist, not a feature)
- Compliance officer documentation

Out of scope (other epics): foundational pipeline (Phases 1-2), CLI/marketplace (Phase 3), version diffing/yank flow internals (Versioning epic), git-tag audit anchoring job (Audit epic).

Success criteria: the full stack deploys to Azure Container Apps from Bicep; NODE_ENV=production + AUTH_MODE=mock fails to boot; SLA timeout fires a notification; a documented pen-test checklist for the zip path exists and passes.

Depends on: Phase 3.

title: Epic: Phase 3 — CLI & Registry Distribution (asr CLI + marketplace)
description:
Phase 3 of the ASR plan (SPEC.md#phase-3-cli--registry-integration-week-56).

Goal: External agents (Claude Code, Codex) discover and install skills via a marketplace repo and the asr CLI.

In scope:
- Marketplace Git repo sync on publish (specs/cli-integration.md)
- asr login device code flow (specs/cli-integration.md, specs/security.md)
- asr CLI install/publish/search commands, tsup-bundled single ESM (DESIGN.md)
- Forgejo generic package registry wiring for skill artifacts
- CLI distribution via Forgejo Releases

Out of scope (other epics): MCP server protocol/tools/auth (MCP epic — parallel distribution surface), SLA/notifications/Azure (Phase 4), versioning yank CLI contract (Versioning epic owns the version-diff/yank semantics; CLI surfaces them).

Success criteria: after a skill publishes, the marketplace repo gains a new entry; asr login completes a device-code flow against Entra; asr install pulls the artifact from the Forgejo generic package registry.

Depends on: Phase 2 (Entra ID + publish path must exist).

title: Visual: Browse tag filter chips are not interactive
description: Flow: Landing / browse. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: tag filter chips are visible and work per visual review prompt and specs/web-ui.md; empty state should include a clear filters action. Actual: skill tags render as passive span elements only; clicking 'security', 'review', 'docs', or 'writing' does not filter or update selected-chip state. Search works separately, as shown in .playwright-mcp/landing-search-after.png. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry renders tags with <span className='tag'> and has no selected tag/filter state or chip button handlers.

title: Visual: Browse screen uses legacy product branding instead of asr
description: Flow: Landing / browse. Viewport: 1280x800. Expected: specs/web-ui.md acceptance says the product name renders as lowercase 'asr' across all screens; no legacy/alternate branding should be visible. Actual: header logo alt text is 'Skill Registry', document title is 'Agent Skill Registry', and hero H1 reads 'Agent Skill Registry'. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web/src/App.tsx and index.html still use pre-ASR display copy/logo alt text instead of the canonical product name.
close_reason: Duplicate of open bead asr-2pf (browse branding does not use asr product name)

title: Visual: skill detail install snippet uses obsolete asr add command
description:
Flow: skill detail modal opened from browse at 1280x800.
Expected: install snippet matches specs/web-ui.md and specs/cli-integration.md: asr install owner/skill.
Actual: modal renders `asr add asr/skills-registry/security-review`; the CLI spec lists install, not add, and the browse detail shape includes owner/repo/name rather than the documented owner/name route.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/skill-detail-modal.png
Root cause hypothesis: seed API content and copyInstallCmd in packages/web/src/App.tsx still use an older CLI verb.

title: Visual: browse branding does not render product name as asr
description:
Flow: landing / browse at 1280x800 and 390x800.
Expected: product name renders as asr across screens, per specs/web-ui.md acceptance test 8.
Actual: the header/logo alt text and hero render Skill Registry / Agent Skill Registry instead of asr. This makes screenshots diverge from the required product branding.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/landing-before.png, .playwright-mcp/landing-mobile.png
Root cause hypothesis: browse header/hero copy and logo alt text still use older registry naming instead of the product name token.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-2pf (browse branding does not use asr product name)

title: Visual: mobile browse has horizontal overflow
description:
Flow: landing / browse at 390x800 mobile viewport.
Expected: no horizontal scroll; topbar/search should fit inside viewport and sidebar/navigation should collapse per specs/web-ui.md.
Actual: document scrollWidth is 449 while innerWidth is 390. The search field protrudes past the right edge in the captured mobile screenshot.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-mobile.png
Root cause hypothesis: header/container/search widths are fixed for desktop and do not constrain or wrap at narrow widths.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-n61 (browse header overflows on mobile)

title: Visual: browse header overflows on mobile
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 390x844. Expected: no horizontal scroll; header content should fit or stack within the viewport. Actual: the search wrapper extends beyond the right edge in the accessibility snapshot (container box x=231 width=218 on a 390px viewport, ending at x=449), producing horizontal overflow risk. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-mobile-390x844.png. Root cause hypothesis: mobile header keeps logo and search side-by-side without constraining search width or stacking.

title: Visual: browse screen missing filter chips
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800. Expected: search bar plus filter chips that can be toggled, per visual review checklist. Actual: search works, but no filter chips are rendered anywhere on the browse screen, so the filter-chip interaction cannot be exercised. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-1280x800-before.png and .playwright-mcp/landing-search-security-after.png. Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry implements only the search input and tag display inside cards; there is no filter chip state or UI.

title: Visual: browse branding does not use asr product name
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800. Expected: product name renders as 'asr' across screens and legacy/alternate product names are absent. Actual: header alt text and hero use 'Skill Registry' / 'Agent Skill Registry', and hero copy says 'Works with Office Companion', which reads like legacy branding instead of asr. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-browse.png. Root cause hypothesis: browse copy/logo alt text has not been updated to the ASR naming rules from specs/web-ui.md.

title: Visual: browse page has horizontal overflow on mobile
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 390x844. Expected: no horizontal scroll on narrow widths; cards and header fit within viewport. Actual: document scrollWidth is 449px while clientWidth is 390px, producing horizontal overflow. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/08-mobile-landing.png. Root cause hypothesis: fixed/min widths or container padding in the browse header/skill cards exceed the viewport.
close_reason: Duplicate of existing open bead asr-p5t; both cover browse mobile horizontal overflow at 390px viewport.

title: Visual: mock auth banner missing on browse screen
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800. Expected: dev/mock mode shows a persistent banner such as 'Mock auth: <roles>' per specs/api.md and specs/web-ui.md so screenshots cannot be confused with prod. Actual: browse screen has no mock auth banner; only /review shows 'Mock auth: Compliance'. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-browse.png. Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry header does not render the mock auth banner or shared auth chrome.

title: Visual: browse header causes horizontal scroll on mobile
description: Flow: Landing/browse responsive check at 390x844 viewport. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-mobile-390x844.png. Expected: no horizontal scroll at narrow widths and header/search controls should collapse or wrap within the viewport. Actual: documentElement.scrollWidth is 449 while window.innerWidth is 390; the search box extends off-screen, causing horizontal overflow. Root cause hypothesis: the browse header keeps logo and a max-width search control in a single flex row without a mobile breakpoint equivalent to the review topbar.

title: Visual: unknown skill path shows browse instead of error state
description:
Flow: Empty + error states at /skills/does-not-exist, 1280x800.
Expected: A missing skill or unknown detail URL should render a graceful 404/inline error state with retry or return navigation, never silently show unrelated data.
Actual: /skills/does-not-exist renders the normal browse registry with two skills and no indication that the requested skill route failed or is unsupported.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/07-unknown-skill-path.png
Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx routes every path other than /review to BrowseRegistry; there is no detail route or not-found route.

title: Visual: mock auth banner missing on browse
description:
Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800.
Expected: Dev mock mode is clearly labelled across the SPA so browse users do not confuse the session with production auth.
Actual: /review shows "Mock auth: Compliance", but / shows no mock identity banner or auth state indicator.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/01-browse-home.png
Root cause hypothesis: BrowseRegistry renders a separate header from ReviewDashboard and does not include the mock-auth-banner element or shared auth shell.

title: Visual: mock auth banner is absent from browse
description:
Flow: Auth mock mode at 1280x800 and 390x844. Loaded / with local dev stack in AUTH_MODE=mock.
Expected: dev/mock identity is clearly labelled in the SPA on core screens so it cannot be confused with production auth; login button should be hidden or labelled Mock.
Actual: /review shows Mock auth: Compliance, but / has no mock/dev identity banner at all. The browse page appears production-like while still using mock auth/API data.
Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/01-landing-before.png and .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/05-approval-dashboard-before.png
Root cause hypothesis: mock-auth-banner is implemented only inside ReviewDashboard, not in shared app chrome/BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-kea; same missing mock auth/dev banner on browse.

title: Visual: mobile browse header causes horizontal scroll
description:
Flow: Landing/browse at 390x844. Loaded / and evaluated layout width.
Expected: no horizontal scroll on narrow widths; header/search should wrap or resize within the viewport.
Actual: document scrollWidth is 449px with a 390px viewport. The header search control extends beyond the right edge in the accessibility snapshot.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/08-mobile-landing-before.png; eval: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/mobile-landing-eval.json
Root cause hypothesis: header container keeps a fixed row layout/search width at mobile sizes instead of stacking or using min-width: 0/flexible constraints.
close_reason: Duplicate visual-review beads from current run; canonical existing issues are asr-9i0, asr-1x1, asr-abd, and asr-4si respectively.

title: Visual: missing skill route renders browse page instead of 404
description:
Flow: Missing skill/error state at 1280x800. Opened http://localhost:5173/skills/does-not-exist.
Expected: a nonexistent skill URL shows a graceful not-found/error state with recovery action, not a blank screen or unrelated page.
Actual: the app silently renders the browse registry page with no 404, no missing-skill message, and no retry/back affordance.
Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524-154412/07-missing-skill-route-before.png
Root cause hypothesis: App.tsx only checks window.location.pathname === /review and sends every other route to BrowseRegistry.
close_reason: Duplicate visual-review beads from current run; canonical existing issues are asr-9i0, asr-1x1, asr-abd, and asr-4si respectively.

title: Visual: product name does not render as asr
description: Flow: Landing and detail, viewport 1280x800. Expected: product name renders as asr across screens, with legacy/incorrect names absent. Actual: header image alt is Skill Registry and hero/title render Agent Skill Registry; no visible asr product name appears. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-browse.png. Root cause hypothesis: web branding copy/logo assets predate the asr naming requirement.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-752; same branding/product-name issue.

title: Visual: mobile header creates horizontal overflow
description: Flow: Landing/browse on narrow viewport 390x844. Expected: no horizontal scroll; header/search should wrap or resize within the viewport. Actual: document scrollWidth is 449px on a 390px viewport and the search field visibly spills past the right edge. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/07-landing-mobile.png. Root cause hypothesis: header flex row keeps logo and max-width search side-by-side without mobile wrapping or min-width constraints.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-4si; same mobile header horizontal overflow at 390px viewport.

title: Visual: landing page filter chips are missing
description: Flow: Landing/browse, viewport 1280x800. Expected: search bar visible and filter chips available/interactable for browsing skills. Actual: search works, but no filter chip controls render; only static tag pills inside skill cards are present and they are not filter controls. Screenshots: .playwright-mcp/01-landing-browse.png and .playwright-mcp/02-search-filtered.png. Root cause hypothesis: browse UI implements text search only; filter chip state and controls are not built.

title: Visual: mobile header causes horizontal scroll
description: Flow: Landing/browse at 390x844 mobile viewport. Expected: no horizontal scroll; header logo and search should fit within the viewport or collapse responsively. Actual: document scrollWidth is 449px for a 390px viewport, and the search wrapper extends past the right edge. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/mobile-landing.png. Root cause hypothesis: the header container keeps logo and a fixed/min-width search layout in one row without a mobile breakpoint.

title: Visual: missing graceful 404 route state
description: Flow: Empty/error state at 1280x800, direct navigation to http://localhost:5173/skills/does-not-exist. Expected: route-level 404 with an inline error message and recovery action, not a silent fallback. Actual: the URL remains /skills/does-not-exist but the UI renders the generic browse landing page with no not-found message, retry, or navigation recovery. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/error-404.png. Root cause hypothesis: the SPA has no client router or not-found route, so every path falls through to App.tsx browse content.

title: Visual: mobile landing has horizontal overflow
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 390x844. Expected: no horizontal scroll on narrow viewports and sidebar/topbar/search should collapse responsively below 900px. Actual: documentElement.scrollWidth is 449 while innerWidth is 390, and the search field extends past the right edge. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524/landing-mobile-390x844.png. Root cause hypothesis: fixed-width topbar/search layout is not constrained for mobile and there is no drawer/sidebar responsive treatment.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-lxt; this run measured innerWidth=390 and documentElement.scrollWidth=449. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524/landing-mobile-390x844.png.

title: Visual: product branding uses PwC/Skill Registry instead of asr
description: Flow: Landing / browse at 1280x800 and 390x844. Expected: product name renders as 'asr' across screens, with no legacy/vendor branding. Actual: topbar shows PwC logo + 'Skill Registry', page title/H1 says 'Agent Skill Registry', and body copy references Office Companion. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524/landing-before-1280x800.png and .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524/landing-mobile-390x844.png. Root cause hypothesis: web shell is still using legacy/placeholder branding assets and copy instead of ASR design tokens/content.
close_reason: Duplicate of asr-wib; this run captured fresh evidence at .playwright-mcp/visual-review-20260524/landing-before-1280x800.png and landing-mobile-390x844.png.

title: Visual: product branding does not render as asr
description: Flow: Landing/browse and all tested routes at 1280x800. Expected: product branding renders as asr per review brief, with no legacy or unrelated brand treatment. Actual: header alt text says Skill Registry, the hero says Agent Skill Registry, and the header mark visually reads like an unrelated PwC-style logo. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-browse-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: packages/web public logo and App.tsx copy still use generic/placeholder registry branding instead of the asr product identity.

title: Visual: mobile header causes horizontal overflow
description: Flow: Landing/browse at 390x800 mobile viewport. Expected: no horizontal overflow; header/search content should fit or stack cleanly. Actual: document scrollWidth is 449px while viewport is 390px, with the search box clipped off the right edge. Screenshot: .playwright-mcp/landing-mobile-before.png. Root cause hypothesis: header container keeps logo and search side-by-side with fixed/min widths that exceed narrow viewports; body overflow hides the overflow instead of resolving layout.

title: Add or configure @asr/core tests so pnpm test passes
description: pnpm test currently runs pnpm --filter @asr/core test, which invokes vitest run. Vitest exits with code 1 because packages/core has no test files matching the default include pattern. Add focused tests or configure passWithNoTests only if that is the intended policy.

title: Build /review/:id detail shell with Diff + Scan tabs
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md#approval-detail-reviewid (Diff + Scan tabs, header skill+version+risk); specs/registry-api.md:18 (GET /skills/:owner/:name/versions/:version/diff → VersionDiff); specs/types.md (ScanReport)

Context:
The `/review/:id` screen is where a reviewer inspects a submission before deciding. This task builds the detail shell: a header (skill name + version + risk badge) and the two decision-critical tabs — Diff (the VersionDiff for the version) and Scan (the ScanReport findings). The sticky decision panel is added separately (asr-0mf.6) and mounts into this shell.

Affected files:
- packages/web/src/routes/ReviewDetail.tsx (new — reads `:id` param; `useQuery` for the submission + its `VersionDiff` via `GET /skills/:owner/:name/versions/:version/diff`; tab strip with Diff and Scan panels; renders `<DecisionPanel/>` slot)
- packages/web/src/router.tsx (point `/review/:id` route element at `ReviewDetail`)

Change:
Implement `ReviewDetail`: header shows `manifest.name`, `manifest.version`, and a risk badge from `VersionDiff.riskAssessment`. A tab strip renders a Diff tab (lists `filesAdded`/`filesRemoved`/`filesModified` and `permissionsExpanded`) and a Scan tab (lists `ScanReport.findings` with severity). Types `VersionDiff` and `ScanReport` imported from `@asr/core`. Include loading skeleton + error card states.

Acceptance:
vitest with mocked fetch returning a `VersionDiff` (one entry in `filesModified`, `permissionsExpanded:true`) and a `ScanReport` (one `high` finding) renders the header version, a Diff tab showing the modified file path, and a Scan tab showing the finding message at severity "high".

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement the Approve/Reject buttons or mutations here (asr-0mf.6). Dependencies/Permissions/Audit tabs from web-ui.md may be stubbed/declared but are out of scope for this task's single acceptance. Use canonical @asr/core types only.

Depends on asr-0mf.3 (route gated by RequireRole) and asr-4ea (imports VersionDiff/ScanReport from @asr/core).

title: Build /review approval queue screen
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf
Parent epic:  asr-921
Spec ref:     specs/web-ui.md#approval-detail-reviewid (Approval queue screen); specs/api.md:13,160 (GET /submissions?status=pending); specs/types.md#submission

Context:
Compliance reviewers need a queue of submissions awaiting review. This is the `/review` screen: it lists pending submissions (skill name + version + risk + submitter) each linking to its `/review/:id` detail. It is the surface the story's "approving removes it from the queue" acceptance is measured against.

Affected files:
- packages/web/src/routes/ReviewQueue.tsx (new — `useQuery` calling `GET /submissions?status=pending`, renders a table of rows; loading skeleton + empty state per specs/web-ui.md#states-to-cover-everywhere)
- packages/web/src/router.tsx (point the `/review` route element at `ReviewQueue`)

Change:
Implement `ReviewQueue` using `@tanstack/react-query` `useQuery(['submissions','pending'], () => fetch('/submissions?status=pending'))`. Type the response rows as `Submission` imported from `@asr/core` (manifest.name, manifest.version, status). Each row links via react-router `<Link to={`/review/${id}`}>`. Render a skeleton while loading and "No submissions awaiting review" empty state.

Acceptance:
vitest with `fetch` mocked to return two pending `Submission`s renders exactly two rows each containing the skill name + version and an anchor whose href is `/review/<id>`; mocking an empty array renders the "No submissions awaiting review" empty state.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement approve/reject mutations here (asr-0mf.6) or the detail tabs (asr-0mf.5). Do not paginate yet. Use the canonical `Submission` type from @asr/core — do not redefine it locally (CLAUDE.md hard rule).

Depends on asr-0mf.3 (route gated by RequireRole) and asr-4ea (imports Submission from @asr/core).

title: Add RequireRole route guard for /review routes
description:
Parent story: asr-0mf Parent epic: asr-921 Spec ref: specs/web-ui.md#routes (route protection: redirect to /login or /error?code=403); specs/web-ui.md (Roles column: /review requires Compliance/Admin) Context: The /review and /review/:id routes are Compliance/Admin only. Client-side gating is UX (the API re-gates), but the SPA must redirect users without the role rather than render the approval screens. A reusable guard keeps each protected route declarative. Affected files: - packages/web/src/auth/RequireRole.tsx (new — wrapper component taking `allowed: Role[]` and `children`; reads `useSession()`) - packages/web/src/router.tsx (wrap the `/review` and `/review/:id` elements in ``) Change: Implement `RequireRole({ allowed, children })`: if `session.roles` intersects `allowed`, render `children`; else render ``. Apply it around the two review route elements in router.tsx. Acceptance: vitest: with a mock session `roles:['Submitter']`, rendering `
secret
` inside a MemoryRouter does NOT show "secret" and navigates to `/error?code=403`; with `roles:['Compliance']` it shows "secret". Risks / out of scope: Client gating is not a security boundary — do NOT drop the server-side authorization assumption. No login redirect logic here (that is MSAL/asr-k4m); unauthenticated handling for prod is out of scope. Depends on asr-0mf.2 (uses useSession).

title: Add anchor scheduler (every 100 events or hourly)
description:
Parent story: asr-nm4
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#external-anchoring (line 152: "Every 100 events OR every hour (whichever comes first)")

Context:
Anchoring must happen on a bounded cadence so the unanchored tail of the chain is always small. This adds the trigger policy that invokes runAnchorOnce when either 100 new events have accumulated since the last anchor or an hour has elapsed -- whichever comes first -- without coupling that policy to the anchor body.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-scheduler.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-scheduler.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export shouldAnchor(state: { lastAnchorAt: number; lastAnchorEventCount: number; now: number; currentEventCount: number }, opts: { intervalMs: number; eventThreshold: number }): boolean returning true when (state.now - state.lastAnchorAt) >= opts.intervalMs OR (state.currentEventCount - state.lastAnchorEventCount) >= opts.eventThreshold. Export createAnchorScheduler(opts: { db; intervalMs?: number; eventThreshold?: number; now?: () => number; run?: typeof runAnchorOnce }) with defaults intervalMs=3_600_000, eventThreshold=100; it tracks lastAnchorAt/lastAnchorEventCount, polls on a timer, and calls run(...) when shouldAnchor is true, updating state from the run result. now()/run are injectable; start()/stop() manage the timer.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-scheduler.test.ts (pure, no real timer/Forgejo): shouldAnchor returns true when currentEventCount-lastAnchorEventCount===100 even with a small elapsed time; returns true when now-lastAnchorAt===3_600_000 with 0 new events; returns false when both are below threshold; with an injected run spy + now(), one scheduler tick invokes run exactly once when the threshold is crossed and updates lastAnchorEventCount; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure policy + timer only -- no Forgejo or DB writes here (runAnchorOnce, asr-nm4 job task, owns those). Multi-replica leader election (only one replica anchors) is out of scope (the SSE leader model, asr-tqo/Azure epic). Keep now()/run injectable so tests need no real clock.

title: Add runAnchorOnce job emitting audit.anchored
description:
Parent story: asr-nm4
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#external-anchoring (lines 152-158); audit action 'audit.anchored' (specs/audit.md line 74)

Context:
This is the body of the anchor operation: read the current chain head, build and sign the tag message, publish the signed tag via ForgejoClient, then record the anchor in the audit trail itself (audit.anchored) so anchoring is also tamper-evident. The scheduler (asr-nm4 scheduler task) decides WHEN to call this.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor.ts (new -- runAnchorOnce)
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor.test.ts (new -- dev-Forgejo integration)

Change:
Export async runAnchorOnce(db, forgejo: ForgejoClient, key: PrivateKey): Promise<{ tagName: string; eventCount: number } | null>. Steps: (1) eventCount = `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_events`; if 0, return null (nothing to anchor); read lastHash + hmac_key_id from `SELECT hash, hmac_key_id FROM audit_events ORDER BY rowid DESC LIMIT 1`; (2) tagName = `audit-anchor-${new Date().toISOString().replace(/[-:]/g,'').replace(/\.\d+Z$/,'Z')}`; (3) message = `lastHash=${lastHash}\neventCount=${eventCount}\nhmacKeyId=${hmacKeyId}`; (4) signature = await signAnchorMessage(message, key); (5) const { tagName: tn, commitSha } = await forgejo.createAnchorTag({ tag: tagName, message, targetSha: <repo head sha>, signature }); (6) emitAudit(db, { action:'audit.anchored', actor:'system', actorType:'system', detail:{ tag: tn, commitSha } }) inside a db.transaction. Return { tagName: tn, eventCount }.

Acceptance:
vitest integration packages/submission/src/audit/anchor.test.ts against the dev Forgejo (skipped if FORGEJO_BASE_URL unset): after seeding 3 audit events, runAnchorOnce creates a Forgejo tag whose name matches /^audit-anchor-\d{8}T\d{6}Z$/ and whose message contains the current lastHash and `eventCount=3`, and audit_events gains exactly one 'audit.anchored' row whose detail.tag equals the created tag name and detail.commitSha is set; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The 100-events-or-hourly trigger is the scheduler task (asr-nm4 scheduler) -- runAnchorOnce performs ONE anchor when called. Emit audit.anchored through emitAudit (the chokepoint), never a direct INSERT. Do not log the GPG signature. Resolving the repo head sha uses the ForgejoClient default branch; keep it to one call.

title: Add GPG anchor signer loaded from Key Vault env
description:
Parent story: asr-nm4
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#external-anchoring (lines 155-156); specs/audit.md#hmac-key-management (line 145)

Context:
The anchor tag must be signed with a dedicated GPG key whose private half lives only in Azure Key Vault and is loaded into the anchor job at runtime -- never written to logs. Isolating signing as a pure module keeps the private key out of ForgejoClient and lets the job (asr-nm4 job task) hand a finished signature to createAnchorTag.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-signer.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-signer.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add "openpgp":"^5.11.0" to packages/submission dependencies (if absent). Export async loadAnchorKey(env = process.env): Promise<PrivateKey> reading AUDIT_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY (ASCII-armored, base64 in env) and AUDIT_GPG_PASSPHRASE; throw Error('AUDIT_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY missing') if unset. Export async signAnchorMessage(message: string, key: PrivateKey): Promise<string> returning a detached ASCII-armored signature via openpgp.sign({ message: await openpgp.createMessage({ text: message }), signingKeys: key, detached: true }). The module MUST NOT console.log or include the armored private key / passphrase in any error (errors reference env var names only).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/anchor-signer.test.ts (test key generated in-test via openpgp.generateKey): signAnchorMessage('lastHash=deadbeef eventCount=3', key) returns a string beginning '-----BEGIN PGP SIGNATURE-----' that openpgp.verify accepts against the matching public key; loadAnchorKey({}) (no env) rejects with an error whose message mentions AUDIT_GPG_PRIVATE_KEY and contains no key material; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pulling the key from Azure Key Vault into the env var is the Azure epic's Key Vault->env projection (out of scope -- load from env here). Never log private-key/passphrase material. Publishing the tag is the ForgejoClient task; this module only produces a signature string.

title: Add createAnchorTag to ForgejoClient
description:
Parent story: asr-nm4
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#external-anchoring (lines 150-159); specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 64-88)

Context:
External anchoring writes a GPG-signed Git tag into the skills-registry Forgejo repo so any post-hoc tampering with the SQLite audit DB must also forge a tag on a protected ref. This adds the tag-creation primitive to ForgejoClient; the anchor job (asr-nm4 job task) supplies the signed tag body + message and ForgejoClient publishes the annotated tag object + ref via Forgejo's git-data API at /api/v1.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/forgejo.ts (add public method createAnchorTag to ForgejoClient)
- packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add async createAnchorTag(input: { tag: string; message: string; targetSha: string; signature?: string }): Promise<{ tagName: string; commitSha: string }>. Via this.upload: POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/tags with { tag, message, object: input.targetSha, type: 'commit', tagger: { name: 'asr-audit-anchor', email: process.env.AUDIT_ANCHOR_EMAIL ?? 'audit@asr.local', date: new Date().toISOString() } } (embed the detached ASCII-armored GPG signature into the message body when input.signature is set, per the annotated-tag object format); then POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/refs with { ref: `refs/tags/${input.tag}`, sha: <created tag-object sha> }. Return { tagName: input.tag, commitSha: input.targetSha }. Never log input.signature.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts with a mocked Forgejo (nock/msw on cfg.baseUrl): createAnchorTag({ tag:'audit-anchor-20260101T000000Z', message:'lastHash=ab eventCount=3', targetSha:'abc' }) issues a POST to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/tags then a POST to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/git/refs with ref 'refs/tags/audit-anchor-20260101T000000Z', and resolves to { tagName, commitSha:'abc' }; `rg -i 'github' packages/core/src/forgejo.ts` still returns no host references; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
NEVER reference github.com -- baseUrl is the Forgejo /api/v1 host. GPG signature *generation* is the signer task (asr-nm4 signer); this method only embeds/publishes a signature it is handed. Do not log signature or token values. Tag branch-protection interaction is out of scope.

title: Add no-PII guard to audit.emit detail
description:
Parent story: asr-28q
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#retention--gdpr (line 178); specs/audit.md#hash-chain (lines 91-114)

Context:
The GDPR design only works if PII never lands in audit_events.detail in the first place -- otherwise purging the principals table would still leave email/displayName recoverable from the immutable audit log. This adds a defensive runtime guard at the single audit chokepoint so a careless caller cannot persist PII into a detail JSON blob.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/emit.ts (add an assertNoPii(detail) check at the top of emitAudit, before any DB access)
- packages/submission/src/audit/emit.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add const PII_KEYS = ['email','displayname','name','givenname','surname','upn'] and a helper assertNoPii(detail: Record<string,unknown>) that throws Error(`audit detail must not contain PII key: ${k}`) when any top-level key of detail, lowercased, is in PII_KEYS. Call assertNoPii(input.detail) inside emitAudit immediately after the AUDIT_ACTIONS validation and before computing the hash / inserting the row, so a violation aborts with zero rows written.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/emit.test.ts: emitAudit(db, { action:'submission.created', actor:'sub-1', actorType:'user', detail:{ email:'x@y.z' } }) throws and SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_events stays 0; emitAudit with a PII-free detail ({ submissionId:'s1' }) succeeds and inserts one row; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Only top-level detail keys are checked (deep nested scanning is out of scope -- keep detail shapes flat per spec). Do NOT alter the hash payload or chain semantics (asr-5qw). The check is purely additive and must not change behaviour for PII-free callers. Resolving sub->email on read is asr-xdi.

title: Add principals upsert/get/purge helpers
description:
Parent story: asr-28q
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#retention--gdpr (lines 178-179)

Context:
Audit rows store only the opaque `sub` as actor; human-readable email/displayName live solely in the principals table and are resolved at query time. This task adds the write/read/purge surface: upsert on every authenticated action, lookup for the view endpoints (asr-xdi), and a GDPR Article 17 purge that erases PII without deleting any audit row or breaking the hash chain.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/principals.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/principals.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export upsertPrincipal(db, p: { sub: string; email: string; displayName: string }): void -- INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(sub) DO UPDATE SET email=excluded.email, display_name=excluded.display_name, last_seen=<now>; set first_seen on insert only, last_seen always (ISO 8601 UTC). Export getPrincipal(db, sub: string): { email: string; displayName: string } | null. Export purgePrincipal(db, sub: string): boolean -- DELETE FROM principals WHERE sub=? returning rowsAffected>0; it MUST NOT touch audit_events.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/principals.test.ts (after runMigrations + AUDIT_HMAC_* set): upsertPrincipal then getPrincipal returns the email/displayName; a second upsert with a new email overwrites it while first_seen is unchanged; after emitting two audit events under that sub and calling purgePrincipal(sub), getPrincipal returns null, SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_events is unchanged, and verifyChain(db, ring).valid === true; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
purgePrincipal deletes ONLY the principals row -- never audit_events (immutability is a SOC2/SOX requirement, spec line 177). Read-side joining of actor->email in the audit view endpoints is asr-xdi, not here. Monthly partitioning / immutable-blob archival is asr-mfv (Phase 4), out of scope.

title: Add 0005 principals migration
description:
Parent story: asr-28q
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#retention--gdpr (lines 175-180)

Context:
GDPR Article 17 requires PII (email, display name) to be purgeable, but SOC2/SOX require audit_events to be immutable and never deleted. The resolution is a separate principals table mapping the opaque Entra `sub` to email/displayName; purging a principal removes PII recoverability while the audit chain (which stores only `sub` as actor) stays intact. This migration creates that table; the helpers (asr-28q upsert/purge task) and read-side resolution (asr-xdi) consume it.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0005_principals.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/index.ts (import + push the 0005 entry after 0004, ascending id order)
- packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0005_principals.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export a Migration { id: 5, name: 'principals', up } whose up runs CREATE TABLE principals(sub TEXT PRIMARY KEY, email TEXT, display_name TEXT, first_seen TEXT NOT NULL, last_seen TEXT NOT NULL) plus CREATE INDEX idx_principals_email ON principals(email). In migrations/index.ts import the 0005 entry and push it after 0004 (asr-a03.1), keeping ascending id order. No FK to audit_events: principals is intentionally decoupled so it can be purged without touching the chain.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/migrations/0005_principals.test.ts: after runMigrations(db), db.pragma('table_info(principals)') includes columns sub, email, display_name, first_seen, last_seen with sub as the primary key (pk=1); db.pragma('index_list(principals)') contains idx_principals_email; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Migration numbering is one global sequence: 0001=submissions, 0002=scan_results, 0003=workflow_locks, 0004=audit_events (asr-a03.1) -- this is 0005, do not renumber or collide. Do NOT add a FK from principals to audit_events (decoupling is the whole point -- principals must be purgeable independently). Upsert/purge helpers and read-side join are separate tasks.

title: Add 5xx error-rate log-query alert rule
description:
Parent story: asr-b4q
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#monitoring (lines 315-320); specs/deployment.md#infrastructure-bicep (lines 103-130, Log Analytics)

Context:
Error rate is not a first-class Container Apps metric, so the ">1% 5xx" SLO is enforced as a scheduled log-query alert over the container/ingress logs that the managed environment already ships to Log Analytics (asr-5v0.1). A forced 5xx burst must page operators.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/alerts.bicep (add the scheduled query rule + reference the Log Analytics workspace)

Change:
Add a `Microsoft.Insights/scheduledQueryRules@2023-03-15-preview` alert against the Log Analytics workspace (asr-5v0.1) whose Kusto query computes the ratio of 5xx responses to total requests for the api app over a 5-minute window and fires when > 1%. Wire it to the action group created in asr-b4q.2. Parameterize the workspace resource id.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/alerts.bicep` succeeds; in a deployed environment, generating a burst of 5xx responses on the api raises this error-rate alert (visible via `az monitor scheduled-query list` and an alert firing in Log Analytics).

Risks / out of scope:
Container stdout/stderr → Log Analytics routing is provided by the managed environment's appLogsConfiguration (asr-5v0.1); do not redefine the workspace here, only reference it. Metric alerts (latency, restarts) are asr-b4q.2.

title: Add latency and replica-restart metric alert rules
description:
Parent story: asr-b4q
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#monitoring (lines 315-320); specs/deployment.md#infrastructure-bicep (lines 103-172)

Context:
Operators must be paged on latency regressions and crash loops. These two conditions map cleanly to Container Apps platform metrics, so they are expressed as Azure metric alerts scoped to the api container app, routed to a shared action group.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/alerts.bicep (new)

Change:
Create deploy/azure/alerts.bicep declaring a `Microsoft.Insights/actionGroups@2023-01-01` and two `Microsoft.Insights/metricAlerts@2018-03-01`: (1) P95 response latency > 2s over a 5-minute window on the api containerApp, (2) replica restart count > 0 (RestartCount) over a 5-minute window. Both reference the action group; parameterize the api containerApp resource id and the alert severity.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/alerts.bicep` succeeds; after deploy, `az monitor metrics alert list -g $RG` returns two rules whose conditions are the >2s latency threshold and the replica-restart threshold.

Risks / out of scope:
The error-rate (>1% 5xx) alert is a log-query alert handled in asr-b4q.3 (different resource type). Reuse, do not duplicate, the action group there. No health probes here (asr-b4q.1).

title: Add api and forgejo Container App health probes
description:
Parent story: asr-b4q
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#monitoring (lines 315-320); specs/deployment.md#container-apps (lines 193-225); specs/api.md (line 56, GET /health)

Context:
Container Apps must restart unhealthy replicas and only route traffic to ready ones. The api exposes GET /health (api.md line 56; route aligned by asr-mcx.6) and Forgejo exposes GET /api/v1/version (deployment.md line 320); wiring these as probes is the baseline of production self-healing.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (add health probes to the api and forgejo containerApp resources)

Change:
On the api containerApp (asr-rkr.1), add a `probes` array with a Liveness and a Readiness HTTP probe targeting path `/health` on port 3001 (the canonical liveness route, NOT `/healthz`). On the forgejo containerApp (asr-rkr.4), add a Liveness HTTP probe targeting `/api/v1/version` on port 3000. Set sensible initialDelaySeconds/periodSeconds/failureThreshold values.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` succeeds and the compiled ARM template's api containerApp `template.containers[0].probes` includes a `Liveness` probe whose httpGet path is `/health`; the forgejo containerApp has a Liveness probe whose path is `/api/v1/version`.

Risks / out of scope:
Use `/health` (not the story's `/healthz`) — that is the spec/aligned route (asr-mcx.6). Do not change ingress, scaling, or env vars. Alert rules and log routing are separate tasks (asr-b4q.2/.3).

title: Add malicious-zip fixtures and extractor rejection suite
description:
Parent story: asr-l3m
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#zip-upload-hardening (lines 11-83); implementation sketch (lines 28-83)

Context:
A checklist is only trustworthy if the controls are continuously proven. This task adds malicious zip fixtures and a test suite asserting extractSafe (asr-crh.1) rejects every one, turning the pentest checklist into an executable regression gate.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/zip/extract.malicious.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/test/fixtures/zip/buildMaliciousZips.ts (new; generates fixtures with yazl)

Change:
Using `yazl` (already a devDep from asr-crh.1) generate in-test fixtures for each attack class: a `../../etc/passwd` traversal entry; a path deeper than maxDepth; a symlink entry (external attrs `0xa000`); entries whose declared uncompressedSize totals beyond maxUncompressedBytes; more than maxFiles entries; a filename longer than maxPathLen; and a control-char/RTL-override (U+202E) filename. For each, assert `extractSafe(fixture, tmpDir, limits)` rejects with the matching error string from extract.ts ('path traversal', 'max depth', 'symlink rejected', 'uncompressed size limit', 'max files', 'path too long', 'illegal chars'). Use extractSafe's optional `limits` arg to trip caps cheaply.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/submission vitest run extract.malicious` passes with every implemented attack class rejected and at least one named assertion per class listed in docs/pentest-zip-path.md.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not modify extract.ts (asr-crh.1) — this is a test-only task exercising existing controls; if a control is missing (e.g. polyglot magic-byte sniff, duplicate-entry detection), file a follow-up and mark that row "pending" in the doc rather than weakening the suite.

title: Write docs/pentest-zip-path.md attack-class checklist
description:
Parent story: asr-l3m
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security.md#zip-upload-hardening (lines 11-83); SPEC.md#security-posture (lines 78-110)

Context:
The upload/extract path is the highest-risk untrusted-input surface in the system. A repeatable, reviewer-facing checklist ties each known zip attack class to the exact yauzl control that must reject it and to a named regression test, so a reviewer or pentester can confirm coverage at a glance.

Affected files:
- docs/pentest-zip-path.md (new)

Change:
Create docs/pentest-zip-path.md with a table of six attack classes — path traversal (Zip Slip), zip bombs (compression-ratio + total-size caps), symlink/hardlink entries, polyglot files (magic-byte vs extension), nested archives, and oversized/duplicate entries. Each row maps to the specific control in specs/security.md#implementation-sketch (cite the rejecting check, e.g. the `path traversal` startsWith guard at security.md lines 70-71, the `maxUncompressedBytes` cap at lines 78-79, the symlink `0xa000` check at lines 74-75, the `maxPathLen`/illegal-chars checks at lines 65-66) and names the regression test case proving it (the suite added in asr-l3m.2). Include a "how to run" line invoking that vitest suite. Where a MUST-HAVE control (polyglot magic-byte sniffing, duplicate-entry detection) is not yet implemented in extractSafe (asr-crh.1), mark the row "pending" with a follow-up note rather than omitting it.

Acceptance:
docs/pentest-zip-path.md exists, contains exactly the six attack classes above, each row cites a specs/security.md control line and a named test case (or "pending"), and a reviewer can follow the "how to run" line to execute the suite.

Risks / out of scope:
Documentation only — no code. The actual fixtures + assertions are asr-l3m.2; keep the named test cases here in sync with that suite. Do not claim controls that do not exist in extractSafe.

title: Replace remaining latest tags with pinned scanner versions
description:
Parent story: asr-eh0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#scanner-docker-image (lines 121-161, esp. line 17 "pinned versions"); #build--push (lines 154-160)

Context:
Reproducible, supply-chain-safe builds require every tool reference to be a validated pin, never a rolling tag. asr-st5.1 introduced the image with placeholder pins; this task finalizes the resolved pins (including the Veracode layer added in asr-eh0.1) and records them so future rebuilds are auditable.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile (replace any remaining ':latest'/'@latest'/'releases/latest' with resolved version pins/digests)
- deploy/docker/scanner/README.md (record each resolved pin and image digest)

Change:
Audit the Dockerfile for `:latest`, `@latest`, and `/releases/latest/` and replace each with a concrete pin: gitleaks `zricethezav/gitleaks:<v>`, the Trivy installer version, `foxguard@<v>`, the Opengrep release asset for a fixed tag (not `/latest/`), and the Veracode CLI version (asr-eh0.1). Record the resolved version + digest of each in README.md.

Acceptance:
`grep -REn ':latest|@latest|releases/latest' deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile` returns no matches, and deploy/docker/scanner/README.md lists a pinned version for gitleaks, trivy, foxguard, opengrep, and veracode.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not alter tool invocation flags or the orchestrator. Verify each pin actually builds before recording it. Custom rule authoring is out of scope.

title: Wire VERACODE_POLICY through runScanner env pass-through
description:
Parent story: asr-eh0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#veracode-integration-details (lines 522-533); environment variables (lines 93-98)

Context:
The host-side runScanner already forwards VERACODE_API_KEY_ID/SECRET into the scanner container (asr-st5.3) but not VERACODE_POLICY, and nothing asserts the "skip cleanly when unset, still return a verdict" contract at the runner boundary. This task completes the env wiring and locks the behaviour with a test.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/scan/runScanner.ts (add VERACODE_POLICY to the container env pass-through)
- packages/submission/src/scan/runScanner.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `VERACODE_POLICY` to the `-e` env list passed to `docker run` alongside the existing `VERACODE_API_KEY_ID`/`VERACODE_API_KEY_SECRET` (asr-st5.3). Forward each only when present in the service env. Confirm the returned ScanReport's `toolResults` reflects veracode `skipped:true` (from the orchestrator) when creds are absent and that a verdict is still returned.

Acceptance:
vitest case `runScanner > veracode env pass-through` using the injected container-spawn stub: with VERACODE_* unset, no `VERACODE_*` `-e` flags are passed and the returned report has a defined `verdict` with `toolResults.veracode.skipped===true`; with all three set, the spawn args include `-e VERACODE_API_KEY_ID`, `-e VERACODE_API_KEY_SECRET`, and `-e VERACODE_POLICY`.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not weaken the signature/verdict cross-check in runScanner (asr-st5.3). The orchestrator-side skip logic is asr-eh0.2; this task only forwards env and asserts the boundary contract.

title: Fix runVeracode verb and clean skip in orchestrator
description:
Parent story: asr-eh0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#veracode-integration-details (lines 522-561); specs/security-scanning.md#development-mode (lines 579-600); environment variables (lines 93-98)

Context:
The in-container orchestrator must call Veracode with the documented CLI verb and the configured policy, and must skip Veracode cleanly (recording it as skipped, not failed) whenever credentials are absent — the default in dev and any non-enterprise deploy.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/scanner/scan-orchestrator.mjs (fix runVeracode)

Change:
In `runVeracode`: return immediately with a skipped marker when `process.env.VERACODE_API_KEY_ID` is unset so the emitted `toolResults.veracode = { exitCode: 0, findingCount: 0, skipped: true }` and no veracode findings are added (dev-mode, lines 586-588). When set, invoke the documented primary verb `veracode static scan <SCAN_DIR> --format sarif --output <sarif>` (lines 545, 558) plus `--policy ${process.env.VERACODE_POLICY || 'default'}` (env line 97), then parse the SARIF via the shared parseSarif.

Acceptance:
A node smoke test of scan-orchestrator.mjs against a clean dir: with VERACODE_API_KEY_ID unset, the emitted report's `toolResults.veracode.skipped===true` and no veracode findings appear; with VERACODE_API_KEY_ID/SECRET set and the `veracode` binary stubbed on PATH, the orchestrator invokes it with args beginning `static scan` (assert via a recording stub).

Risks / out of scope:
Keep field names byte-identical to the canonical ScanReport (asr-st5.2). Do not import @asr/core in this in-container file. Host-side env pass-through (VERACODE_POLICY into the container) is asr-eh0.3. Do not change the gitleaks hard-block verdict logic.

title: Add pinned Veracode CLI install to scanner Dockerfile
description:
Parent story: asr-eh0
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#tier-3--optional-enterprise-configured-via-env (lines 33-37); specs/security-scanning.md#cli-installation-in-scanner-dockerfile (lines 534-540)

Context:
Veracode is the optional Tier-3 enterprise scanner. The Phase-2 scanner image (asr-st5.1) deliberately did NOT bake it in; this Phase-4 task adds the Veracode CLI to the image so enterprise deployments can enable it via env, while builds without Veracode credentials still succeed.

Affected files:
- deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile (add the Veracode CLI install layer)

Change:
Add the documented Veracode CLI install (security-scanning.md lines 534-540) as its own RUN layer: `curl -fsS https://tools.veracode.com/veracode-cli/install | sh && mv veracode /usr/local/bin/veracode || true`, pinned to a specific CLI version where the installer accepts a version arg. The layer must be build-safe (`|| true`) so an image build never fails when the Veracode endpoint is unreachable. Veracode runs only at scan time when credentials are present (orchestrator task asr-eh0.2).

Acceptance:
`docker build -t asr-scanner:veracode -f deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile deploy/docker/scanner` succeeds with no network credentials, and the Dockerfile contains a Veracode install layer (`grep veracode-cli/install deploy/docker/scanner/Dockerfile` matches). When the endpoint is reachable, `docker run --rm --entrypoint sh asr-scanner:veracode -c 'command -v veracode'` exits 0.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not add VERACODE_* secrets to the image or run a scan at build time. Do not change the four Tier-1/Tier-2 tool layers (asr-st5.1). The runVeracode invocation logic is asr-eh0.2; pin-auditing the whole Dockerfile is asr-eh0.4.

title: Wire notifier into workflow transitions
description:
Parent story: asr-qdh
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#workflow-integration (lines 500-512); specs/security-scanning.md#verdict-actions (lines 514-520)

Context:
With transport + mailer in place, the workflow transitions must fire the right email to the right principal: questionnaire ready → submitter, scan verdict=review_required → compliance reviewer, approve/reject → submitter. This is the user-visible payoff of the story.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.ts (call notify() at the questionnaire-ready, post-scan, and review-decision transitions)
- packages/submission/src/workflow/approvalPipeline.notify.test.ts (new)

Change:
At the questionnaire HITL entry, notify the submitter `questionnaire_ready`. After the scan node computes the verdict (asr-82o.2), when `verdict==='review_required'` notify the assigned compliance reviewer `scan_review_required` (verdict-actions table, lines 514-520). At the review decision, notify the submitter `approved` or `rejected`. Resolve each recipient from the submission record (submitter) and the review assignment (reviewer); thread the shared transport (createTransport) and PUBLIC_BASE_URL through the pipeline dependencies.

Acceptance:
Integration test `pipeline notify > review_required emails exactly the reviewer` drives a submission to scan verdict=review_required with an InMemoryTransport and asserts exactly one captured message, addressed to the reviewer, whose body contains the submission deep link and no other principal's PII.

Risks / out of scope:
SLA-expiry/escalation emails are sent from the SLA job (asr-h4i.2/.3), not here. Do not block a workflow transition on email failure — send is best-effort and logged. Do not add new audit actions for "email sent".

title: Add PII-safe notification templates and mailer
description:
Parent story: asr-qdh
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#workflow-integration (lines 500-512); specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry (lines 214-222)

Context:
Given a transport, the service needs PII-safe message bodies with a working deep link back to the submission. Each email goes to exactly one principal and must never embed any other principal's address or PII (a reviewer email must not leak the submitter's identity beyond what the workflow requires).

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/notify/mailer.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/notify/templates.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/notify/mailer.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add `export type NotifyEvent = 'questionnaire_ready' | 'scan_review_required' | 'approved' | 'rejected' | 'sla_extended' | 'sla_escalated'`. In templates.ts add `export function render(event: NotifyEvent, ctx: { submissionId: string; baseUrl: string }): { subject: string; body: string }` building the deep link `${baseUrl}/submissions/${submissionId}` and a per-event subject/body. In mailer.ts add `export async function notify(transport: EmailTransport, to: string, event: NotifyEvent, ctx): Promise<void>` that renders and sends to the single recipient. The body must contain only `to` (no other addresses) plus the link.

Acceptance:
vitest case `mailer > review email carries the link and only the reviewer address` — `await notify(mem, 'reviewer@example.com', 'scan_review_required', { submissionId:'s_1', baseUrl:'https://asr.example' })` results in `mem.sent[0].body` containing `https://asr.example/submissions/s_1` and not containing any '@' address other than 'reviewer@example.com'.

Risks / out of scope:
Recipient resolution (who is submitter/reviewer/admin for a given submission) belongs to the wiring task (asr-qdh.3) — `notify` takes an explicit `to`. Keep bodies plain text or simple HTML; no attachments. Do not include submitter PII in reviewer mail.

title: Add pluggable email transport with in-memory mock
description:
Parent story: asr-qdh
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/security-scanning.md#workflow-integration (lines 500-512); specs/submission-package.md#environment (lines 150-176)

Context:
Several workflow events must reach humans by email (questionnaire ready, scan needs review, approval/rejection, SLA expiry/escalation). Prod uses real SMTP/Graph; dev and tests must capture mail in-process with zero external calls. This task adds the transport seam so every later notifier call is testable and prod-swappable.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/notify/transport.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/notify/transport.test.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/env.ts (add NOTIFY_TRANSPORT + PUBLIC_BASE_URL to the zod schema, asr-6ax.2)

Change:
Add `export interface EmailMessage { to: string; subject: string; body: string }` and `export interface EmailTransport { send(msg: EmailMessage): Promise<void> }`. Add `export class InMemoryTransport implements EmailTransport` exposing `readonly sent: EmailMessage[]` and pushing each message. Add `export function createTransport(env): EmailTransport` selecting by `NOTIFY_TRANSPORT`: `'memory'` (default in dev/test) → InMemoryTransport; `'smtp'`/`'graph'` → the prod transport (a thin nodemailer/Graph wrapper behind the interface). Extend the zod env schema (asr-6ax.2) with `NOTIFY_TRANSPORT` and `PUBLIC_BASE_URL`.

Acceptance:
vitest case `transport > memory transport captures sent mail` asserts createTransport({ NOTIFY_TRANSPORT:'memory' }) returns an InMemoryTransport and after `await t.send({to,subject,body})` `t.sent` has length 1 with the same fields.

Risks / out of scope:
No real network/SMTP wiring here — the prod transport is behind the interface and may throw "not configured" until a later prod task fills it. Templates and event→recipient logic are the next task (asr-qdh.2). Do not import workflow nodes here.

title: Add compliance-review escalate-to-admin SLA branch
description:
Parent story: asr-h4i
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry (lines 214-222); specs/workflow.md#separation-of-duties (lines 224-227)

Context:
A compliance review left untouched for 30 days must not silently auto-reject a possibly-fine submission; the policy first escalates to an admin and grants a 7-day extension, only auto-rejecting if the admin also lets it lapse. This closes the review branch of the SLA sweep started in asr-h4i.2.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/slaTimeout.ts (extend runSlaSweep with the review branch)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/slaTimeout.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In `runSlaSweep`, when a submission's stage is `review` and past its deadline: if not already extended, send the notifier (asr-qdh) an `sla_escalated` mail to the admin recipient, persist the one-time 7-day extension (computeDeadline(stage, enteredAt, true)), and return it under a new `escalated` array; if already extended and still past the extended deadline, deliver the reject HITL signal and emit `workflow.review.rejected` via audit.emit (asr-5qw.2) with `detail.reason='timeout'`. Choose escalate vs auto_reject via `nextAction('review', alreadyExtended)` (slaPolicy.ts).

Acceptance:
Integration test `slaTimeout > escalates a stale review then auto-rejects` with a controlled clock: a review entered 31 days ago → first sweep returns it under `escalated`, the mock notifier captured one admin `sla_escalated` mail, and the deadline moved out 7 days; advancing past the extended deadline → next sweep auto-rejects it and emits an audit event `action==='workflow.review.rejected'` with `detail.reason==='timeout'`.

Risks / out of scope:
Escalation is a notification + extension only — there is NO `workflow.review.escalated` audit action (the enum at specs/audit.md lines 43-76 is closed); do not invent one. Admin recipient resolution (which principal is "admin") comes from config/env, not a new role lookup here. Questionnaire/confirmation branches (asr-h4i.2) are unchanged.

title: Add ACR build and Container App roll steps to deploy workflow
description:
Parent story: asr-fup
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#cicd-forgejo-actions (lines 289-301)

Context:
After login, the workflow must build the service images in ACR keyed by the commit
SHA and roll the api and web Container Apps to the new image, giving a one-push
deploy.

Affected files:
- .forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml (edit; from asr-fup task 1)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:289-301

Change:
Append two steps after login: "Build images in ACR" — derive
GIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD), run `az acr build --registry $ACR --image
asr-api:$GIT_SHA ./deploy/docker/api` and the asr-web equivalent (./packages/web);
"Roll Container Apps" — `az containerapp update --name api --resource-group $RG
--image $ACR.azurecr.io/asr-api:$GIT_SHA` and the web equivalent.

Acceptance:
The workflow still parses as valid YAML; grep confirms two `az acr build` steps
(asr-api, asr-web) and two `az containerapp update` steps (api, web), all using
$GIT_SHA derived from `git rev-parse`; no `GITHUB_SHA` and no github.com reference.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not use GITHUB_SHA (Forgejo "no GitHub" rule) — derive the SHA via git rev-parse.
Ordering: build must run before roll. The login step (task 1) must already exist.

title: Add Forgejo deploy workflow with service-principal az login step
description:
Parent story: asr-fup
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#cicd-forgejo-actions (lines 263-288)

Context:
Deploys are driven by Forgejo Actions on push to main. azure/login@v2 isn't on
Forgejo runners, so we az login with a service-principal JSON from the secret
AZURE_CREDENTIALS_JSON (Forgejo forbids generic names like GITHUB_TOKEN /
AZURE_CREDENTIALS) and shred the file immediately after login.

Affected files:
- .forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml (new)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:267-288

Change:
Create .forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml: name Deploy, on.push.branches [main], one job
`deploy` (runs-on ubuntu-latest) with steps: (1) actions/checkout@v4; (2) "Azure
login (CLI)" — env AZURE_CREDENTIALS_JSON from secrets, write it to /tmp/sp.json,
`az login --service-principal` reading clientId/clientSecret/tenantId via `jq -r`,
then `shred -u /tmp/sp.json`.

Acceptance:
The workflow parses as valid YAML
(`python3 -c 'import yaml;yaml.safe_load(open(".forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml"))'`
exits 0); it contains on.push.branches [main], an `az login --service-principal`
step using AZURE_CREDENTIALS_JSON, and `shred -u /tmp/sp.json`; grep finds no
`GITHUB_TOKEN`, no exact `AZURE_CREDENTIALS`, and no github.com reference.

Risks / out of scope:
Build and roll steps are the next task. Do not name the secret AZURE_CREDENTIALS or
use GITHUB_TOKEN. Always shred the SP JSON after login.

title: Point forgejo Container App image at ACR mirror
description:
Parent story: asr-1h5
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#forgejo-image-mirror (lines 182, 313)

Context:
Once the Forgejo image is mirrored into ACR, the forgejo Container App must pull
$ACR.azurecr.io/forgejo:15 instead of codeberg, completing the removal of the
cross-internet dependency on scale-out and restart.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep (edit; forgejo app added in asr-rkr task 4)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:182, 313

Change:
Set the forgejo containerApp image to '${acrLoginServer}/forgejo:15' (param
acrLoginServer, e.g. '$ACR.azurecr.io') and add the ACR as a
configuration.registries[] entry on the app so it authenticates to pull. Ensure no
codeberg reference remains in the prod app definition.

Acceptance:
`az bicep build --file deploy/azure/containerapps.bicep` exits 0; `grep -ri codeberg
deploy/azure/` returns no matches and the forgejo app image resolves to
'${acrLoginServer}/forgejo:15'.

Risks / out of scope:
Dev docker-compose keeps pulling codeberg directly — do NOT change
deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml. The image tag stays ':15'.

title: Add az acr build script for asr-api and asr-web images
description:
Parent story: asr-1h5
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#cicd-forgejo-actions (lines 289-294)

Context:
Prod service images (asr-api, asr-web) are built in ACR rather than pushed from a
laptop. Keying tags by git SHA makes every deploy traceable and rollbackable. The
deploy workflow (asr-fup) calls the same commands; this script makes them runnable
standalone too.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/scripts/acr-build-services.sh (new)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:289-294

Change:
Create deploy/azure/scripts/acr-build-services.sh: bash with `set -euo pipefail`,
require ACR (exit non-zero if unset), derive GIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD),
then `az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image "asr-api:${GIT_SHA}" ./deploy/docker/api`
and `az acr build --registry "$ACR" --image "asr-web:${GIT_SHA}" ./packages/web`.

Acceptance:
`bash -n deploy/azure/scripts/acr-build-services.sh` exits 0; grep confirms two
`az acr build` lines (asr-api and asr-web) both using ${GIT_SHA}; shellcheck clean.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not reference GITHUB_SHA or any github.com URL — derive the SHA via git
rev-parse. Container App rollout is out of scope (owned by asr-fup).

title: Add az acr import script mirroring Forgejo image into ACR
description:
Parent story: asr-1h5
Parent epic:  asr-mfv
Spec ref:     specs/deployment.md#forgejo-image-mirror (lines 303-313)

Context:
Pulling codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:15 from inside Azure on every cold start or
scale-out adds a cross-internet dependency. Mirroring the image into ACR removes it.
This script is meant to run on a daily schedule.

Affected files:
- deploy/azure/scripts/acr-mirror-forgejo.sh (new)
- spec source: specs/deployment.md:307-311

Change:
Create deploy/azure/scripts/acr-mirror-forgejo.sh: shebang '#!/usr/bin/env bash',
`set -euo pipefail`, require the ACR env var (exit non-zero with a message if unset),
then run `az acr import --name "$ACR" --source codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:15
--image forgejo:15 --force` and echo a success line. Mark the file executable.

Acceptance:
`bash -n deploy/azure/scripts/acr-mirror-forgejo.sh` exits 0 and
`grep -q 'az acr import .*--source codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:15 .*--image forgejo:15'`
on the file succeeds; shellcheck reports no errors.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not hardcode the ACR name. Scheduling the daily run (cron/workflow) is out of
scope for this task. This script is the one sanctioned place codeberg may appear in
prod tooling.

title: Wire persona materialization into install orchestrator
description:
Parent story: asr-srq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#generated-skillmd-for-personas, #persona-references; specs/cli-integration.md#skill-installation-targets

Context:
This connects persona materialization to the real install path: when the installed skill's manifest is kind:persona, the installer must reject reference cycles, then write a GENERATED SKILL.md (inject or delegate) instead of copying the raw skill, into each detected agent dir. Plain skills keep the existing verbatim copy.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/install.ts (extend the asr-5wr.6 installSkill orchestrator)
- packages/cli/src/install.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In installSkill, after download/verify and before/while writing files: parse the manifest; if manifest.kind === 'persona', call assertNoReferenceCycles(manifest.name, ...) (asr-srq.3) resolving references from the downloaded bundle, build content via generatePersonaSkillMd(manifest, content, resolved) (asr-srq.2), and write that as SKILL.md into each agentSkillDir (asr-5wr.1) target; for kind:skill keep the verbatim copy. Lockfile recording (asr-5wr.5) is unchanged.

Acceptance:
