vitest (install.test.ts) with download/verify stubbed and a temp cwd: installing an inject persona writes a SKILL.md whose frontmatter has `when_to_use: always`; installing a delegate persona writes a SKILL.md with allowed-tools including `Agent`; installing a persona whose references form a cycle rejects the install with an invalid_manifest error and writes no files. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do not regress plain-skill install (kind:skill must still copy verbatim). Cycle rejection must happen before any file write (no partial install). Reference resolution uses the downloaded bundle only — no network refetch of referenced skills here.

title: Add persona reference cycle detection
description:
Parent story: asr-srq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#persona-references (lines 222-231: "the CLI rejects references that form a cycle (A -> B -> A) with invalid_manifest")

Context:
Delegate personas may reference other skills, which are inlined at materialization time. A reference cycle (A references B which references A) would recurse forever, so the CLI must detect and reject cycles up front with a typed invalid_manifest error before any files are written.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/persona-refs.ts (new — assertNoReferenceCycles)
- packages/cli/src/persona-refs.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add assertNoReferenceCycles(root: string, getReferences: (name: string) => string[]): void that performs DFS over the reference graph starting at root, tracking the current path; on revisiting a node already on the path it throws an Error with code/name 'invalid_manifest' and a message naming the cycle (e.g. "reference cycle: A -> B -> A"). Acyclic graphs return normally. Also export an InvalidManifestError class (or a tagged Error) so callers can branch on it.

Acceptance:
vitest (persona-refs.test.ts): with getReferences modeling A->B->A, assertNoReferenceCycles('A', ...) throws an error whose code/name is 'invalid_manifest'; with A->B->C (acyclic) it does not throw. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Graph traversal only — no manifest loading from disk, no SKILL.md generation. Self-reference (A->A) is also a cycle and must be rejected. Do not silently drop the cyclic reference; reject the whole install.

title: Add persona SKILL.md generator for inject and delegate modes
description:
Parent story: asr-srq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#generated-skillmd-for-personas (lines 182-215); specs/types.md#skill-manifest (kind/persona_mode/references, lines 33-36)

Context:
When `asr install` materializes a kind:persona skill it must synthesize SKILL.md from the manifest + content. inject personas become always-active skills; delegate personas become subagent launchers that can call Agent and inline their referenced skills into the subagent prompt. This task builds the generator (used by the install wiring task).

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/persona.ts (new — generatePersonaSkillMd)
- packages/cli/src/persona.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add generatePersonaSkillMd(manifest: SkillManifest, content: string, resolved?: Record<string, string>): string. inject mode (persona_mode 'inject' or default): YAML frontmatter with when_to_use: always, allowed-tools from mapTools(manifest.permissions/declared tools, 'claude'), user-invocable: true, disable-model-invocation: false, then content. delegate mode: frontmatter with allowed-tools including 'Agent' (union with mapTools result), argument-hint, when_to_use describing delegation, then a subagent launcher body that inlines each referenced skill (from `resolved`) into the prompt template. Import SkillManifest from @asr/core and mapTools from tool-mapping.ts (asr-srq.1).

Acceptance:
vitest (persona.test.ts): generatePersonaSkillMd for an inject persona yields frontmatter containing `when_to_use: always`; for a delegate persona with references ['code-review'] (resolved provided) the frontmatter allowed-tools includes `Agent` and the body inlines the code-review content. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Generation only — no fs writes, no cycle detection (separate task), no install wiring. Plain skills (kind:skill) are copied verbatim by the installer and must not pass through this generator.

title: Add tool-mapping.ts upstream-to-agent tool name mapper
description:
Parent story: asr-srq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#tool-filter-mapping (lines 217-219)

Context:
Personas may restrict tool access using upstream tool names inherited from prior platforms. When materializing a persona for Claude Code or Codex, those names must be translated to each agent's real tool identifiers, with a safe fallback for anything unmapped. This pure mapping module is consumed by the persona SKILL.md generator.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/tool-mapping.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/tool-mapping.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add tool-mapping.ts exporting mapTools(upstream: string[], agent: 'claude' | 'codex'): string[]. Maintain a documented mapping table from upstream names to Claude Code / Codex equivalents (e.g. file-read -> Read, file-write -> Write/Edit, shell -> Bash, web -> WebSearch/WebFetch, subagent -> Agent). Unmapped tool names translate to their closest pair: the file-oriented default set Read + Write + Bash (deduplicated, order-stable). Export the raw table for tests.

Acceptance:
vitest (tool-mapping.test.ts): mapTools(['file-read','shell'],'claude') returns ['Read','Bash']; every documented upstream key maps to a non-empty equivalent list; an unknown name like 'frobnicate' maps to ['Read','Write','Bash']; results are de-duplicated. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure data + function — no filesystem, no manifest parsing. Keep the table the single source for both agents (parameterize by agent), do not fork two copies. SKILL.md generation is a separate task.

title: Add publish-to-download integration test with hash equality
description:
Parent story: asr-p29
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownernamevversiondownload (lines 81-91); specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 168-180); specs/submission-package.md#testing

Context:
Proves the publish-to-download round trip end to end: a published version's download redirect points at a generic-package URL that actually serves the uploaded skill.zip, and the served bytes hash to the version's contentHash. This is the story-level acceptance for asr-p29 and the contract `asr install` relies on.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/test/integration/publish-download.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add an integration test (Forgejo testcontainer + temp SQLite) that publishes acme/x@1.0.0 (driving the publish path so publishArtifact runs), then GET /api/v1/skills/acme/x/v/1.0.0/download and asserts: status 302; Location matches `/api/packages/acme/generic/x/1.0.0/skill.zip`; following the Location returns 200 with the zip bytes; and the canonical SHA-256 of those bytes equals the version's contentHash recorded at publish.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/submission test:integration` runs publish-download.test.ts green: the 302 Location resolves to a 200 zip whose canonical SHA-256 equals contentHash.

Risks / out of scope:
Requires the Forgejo testcontainer with the generic package registry enabled. Do not assert against a mocked Forgejo — the point is the real upload+redirect chain. Yanked-download behavior (X-ASR-Yanked) is covered by asr-sm0/asr-4it, not here.

title: Wire asr token command with --export, --write-env, --once
description:
Parent story: asr-dul Parent epic: asr-klk Spec ref: specs/cli-integration.md#token-export-for-mcp (lines 77-87) Context: This wires the user-facing `asr token` command from the building blocks: --export prints the eval line, --write-env writes the 0600 file, --once prints a freshly minted short-lived token, and a bare invocation prints nothing and exits 64. It replaces the legacy registry-token-management `token ` command (index.ts:606-709) which is superseded by the new auth model. Affected files: - packages/cli/src/index.ts (replace the legacy `token` command at lines 606-709 with the new flag-driven command) - packages/cli/src/token-cmd.test.ts (new) Change: Register `program.command('token')` with options --export, --write-env , --once and no positional action. Behavior: resolve the cached token via getValidAccessToken() (asr-thq.7, with silent refresh). --export -> process.stdout.write(formatExportLine(token) + '\n'); --write-env

-> await writeEnvFile(p, token) (no stdout); --once -> process.stdout.write(await mintDerivedToken() + '\n'); no flag -> write a usage hint to stderr only and process.exit(64). Use token-export.ts (asr-dul.1) and mintDerivedToken (asr-dul.2). Acceptance: vitest (token-cmd.test.ts) invoking the action with stubbed getValidAccessToken/mintDerivedToken/writeEnvFile: --export writes an `export ASR_TOKEN=` line to stdout and exits 0; --write-env f calls writeEnvFile(f, token) and writes nothing to stdout; bare token writes nothing to stdout and exits 64. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes. Risks / out of scope: Removing the legacy `token ` (list/create/revoke) command is intentional — registry PAT management moves under the new auth model; do not keep both (the positional `` and the new flags collide). Do not print the token to stderr or logs.

title: Add mintDerivedToken API client for short-lived tokens
description:
Parent story: asr-dul
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#token-export-for-mcp (lines 83-87: "--once mints a derived, short-lived (5-minute) token via the API")

Context:
`asr token --once` provides a one-shot, 5-minute token for a single MCP invocation so the long-lived session token never reaches the child process env permanently. This task adds the API client call that mints that derived token, building on the authed api.ts client (asr-rtz.1).

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/api.ts (extend the asr-rtz.1 client)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/api.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add mintDerivedToken(): Promise<string> to api.ts that POSTs to /api/v1/auth/derived-token via apiFetch (auth attached unless dev mode) and returns body.token from the { token, expiresAt } response. Surface a typed error if the response is non-2xx (carrying body.error), mirroring postSubmission's error handling.

Acceptance:
vitest (__tests__/api.test.ts) against a stub server returning { token: "short.lived", expiresAt: <now+300s> }: mintDerivedToken() resolves to "short.lived" and sent an Authorization header when auth is enabled; a 401 surfaces an error exposing body.error. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The server-side /auth/derived-token endpoint is owned by the submission API (out of scope for this CLI task) — the test stubs it. Do not cache the derived token in the token store; it is single-use and ephemeral.

title: Add token-export module (export line and 0600 env file)
description:
Parent story: asr-dul
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#token-export-for-mcp (lines 71-87)

Context:
MCP clients read ${ASR_TOKEN} from the environment. To hand them the token without leaking it to shell history or screen recorders, the CLI emits a shell-escaped `export ASR_TOKEN=...` for eval, or writes a 0600 sourced env file. This task is the pure rendering/writing module the `asr token` command will call.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/token-export.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/token-export.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add token-export.ts exporting formatExportLine(token: string): string returning a single line `export ASR_TOKEN=<shell-escaped>` (single-quote escaping that handles embedded quotes), and async writeEnvFile(path: string, token: string): Promise<void> that writes that line to path and chmods it 0o600 (write with { mode: 0o600 } AND an explicit chmod to defeat umask). Neither function reads the token store — the token is passed in.

Acceptance:
vitest (__tests__/token-export.test.ts): formatExportLine("a'b") returns a line that, when eval'd, sets ASR_TOKEN exactly to a'b (assert the escaping); writeEnvFile to a temp path creates a file with mode 0600 (statSync().mode & 0o777 === 0o600) containing the export line. `pnpm --filter @asr/cli test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
No token retrieval, no API calls, no stdout printing here — pure formatting + file write. Do not log the token. The bare-command exit-64 behavior and command wiring are a later task.

title: Add marketplace sync integration test
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#sync-job (story acceptance); specs/submission-package.md#testing (integration against a forgejo testcontainer)

Context:
End-to-end proof that the marketplace stays in sync: publishing adds a plugin + marketplace.json entry, yanking updates/removes it, and a forced failure pages exactly once within the rate-limit window. This is the story-level acceptance signal for asr-3d8.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/test/integration/marketplace-sync.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add an integration test (Forgejo testcontainer + temp SQLite) that: (1) fires version.published for skill acme/x@1.0.0 and asserts a PR is opened on the marketplace repo adding plugins/x/.claude-plugin/plugin.json, plugins/x/.codex-plugin/plugin.json, plugins/x/skills/x/SKILL.md and a marketplace.json entry for x; (2) fires version.yanked for acme/x@1.0.0 and asserts the marketplace.json entry is removed/updated; (3) forces a sync failure and asserts marketplace_sync.failed is emitted once and the pager fires once within the hour. Forgejo only.

Acceptance:
`pnpm --filter @asr/submission test:integration` runs marketplace-sync.test.ts green: published opens the expected PR/files, yank updates the entry, and the forced failure pages exactly once.

Risks / out of scope:
Requires the Forgejo testcontainer harness; gate the test behind the integration project (test/integration), not the unit run. Do not assert on real on-call paging — stub the pager and assert call count.

title: Add marketplace_sync.failed emit with hourly per-skill rate limit
description:
Parent story: asr-3d8
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#sync-job (line 271: "emit marketplace_sync.failed and page on-call, rate-limited to one alert per hour per skill"); specs/audit.md (audit.emit usage)

Context:
When a marketplace sync fails the operator must be alerted, but a flapping skill must not page on-call every minute. This task wraps the sync with an audit event and a per-skill 1/hour paging rate limit so failures are observable without alert storms.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.ts (extend: wrap syncMarketplaceRepo with failure handling + in-memory rate limiter)
- packages/submission/src/jobs/marketplaceSync.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add runMarketplaceSync(skillName: string, deps): on syncMarketplaceRepo rejection, call audit.emit (asr-5qw.2) with action 'marketplace_sync.failed' and detail { skillName, error }, then invoke a pager only if the last page for that skillName was more than 3600s ago (Map<string, number> lastPagedAt keyed by skillName). Re-throw after recording so callers see the failure. The audit emit is unconditional; only the pager is rate-limited.

Acceptance:
vitest with audit.emit and the pager stubbed and syncMarketplaceRepo forced to reject twice within the hour for the same skill: marketplace_sync.failed is emitted twice (once per failure) but the pager is invoked exactly once. `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Use the closed AUDIT_ACTIONS enum — 'marketplace_sync.failed' must be a member (add it in @asr/core audit types if absent, but keep that change minimal). The rate-limit map is process-local (acceptable for single write replica); persistent dedupe is out of scope.

title: Add dev-mode MCP connectivity smoke test (initialize + tools/list)
description:
Parent story: asr-8pk
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#client-configuration (dev-mode block, lines 138-146); specs/mcp.md#authentication (dev mode skips validation, line 23); specs/mcp.md#transport (initialize, lines 5-10)

Context:
The dev-mode config block is only useful if it actually connects. This adds an automated smoke test proving that, against the app booted with AUTH_MODE=mock, a Streamable-HTTP MCP client can initialize (no auth header) and enumerate the registered tools — the same flow an operator follows with the dev-mode .mcp.json.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/test/integration/mcp-devmode.test.ts (new)

Change:
vitest integration: boot the Hono app (app.fetch via @hono/node-server) with AUTH_MODE=mock on an ephemeral port. Using `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client` with `StreamableHTTPClientTransport(new URL('http://localhost:<port>/mcp'))` and NO Authorization header, call `client.connect()` (the initialize handshake) then `client.listTools()`. Assert the handshake succeeds and the returned tool list includes at least 'registry_search' (registered by asr-76e.1). Tear down the server in afterAll.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/test/integration/mcp-devmode.test.ts: against the mock-auth dev server, an MCP client with no auth header completes initialize and listTools returns a set whose names include 'registry_search'; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test:integration` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Dev-only path: relies on AUTH_MODE=mock skipping token validation (specs/mcp.md line 23) — do not test the prod bearer path here. Requires the server bootstrap (asr-1c3.1) and at least one registered tool (asr-76e.1). Do not assert the full tool inventory (tools land across asr-76e/asr-3hs/asr-3po) — only that listTools is reachable and includes registry_search.

title: Write MCP client configuration documentation
description:
Parent story: asr-8pk
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#client-configuration (lines 113-146); token acquisition via asr login (specs/cli-integration.md; asr-dul)

Context:
An operator wiring an MCP client to the registry needs copy-paste configuration for each supported client. This ships the three canonical config blocks so Claude Code, Codex CLI, and a local dev client can all connect, with a pointer to how the bearer token is obtained.

Affected files:
- docs/mcp-clients.md (new)
- README.md (add a link to docs/mcp-clients.md under an MCP/clients section)

Change:
Create docs/mcp-clients.md with three fenced blocks copied verbatim from specs/mcp.md#client-configuration: (1) Claude Code `.mcp.json` (transport http, url .../mcp, headers Authorization: Bearer ${ASR_TOKEN}); (2) Codex CLI `~/.codex/config.toml` ([mcp_servers.skill-registry], transport http, url, bearer_token_env_var=ASR_TOKEN); (3) dev-mode `.mcp.json` (no auth header, http://localhost:3001/mcp). Add a short "Getting a token" note: ASR_TOKEN is produced by `asr login` (device-code flow, Phase 3 / asr-dul) and exported into the environment. Link the new doc from README.md.

Acceptance:
A reviewer diffs the three fenced config blocks in docs/mcp-clients.md against specs/mcp.md lines 117-146 and finds no content differences (the .mcp.json, config.toml, and dev-mode blocks are byte-identical), and README.md contains a working relative link to docs/mcp-clients.md.

Risks / out of scope:
Docs only — do not change the MCP server. Keep the dev-mode block auth-header-free (dev skips token validation per specs/mcp.md line 23). Do not document `asr login` internals here (that is asr-dul) — only reference it.

title: Wire rate limit and telemetry into the MCP tool invocation path
description:
Parent story: asr-3f8
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#rate-limiting (line 111); specs/mcp.md#telemetry (lines 148-153); specs/submission-package.md#module-layout (mcp/server.ts invocation wrapper, line 26)

Context:
The limiter (asr-3f8.1) and telemetry emitter (asr-3f8.2) are inert until every tool dispatch routes through them. This adds the single invocation chokepoint in the MCP server so all tools — current and future — are rate-limited and logged uniformly, without each tool task re-implementing it.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.ts (add a wrapToolHandler used when registering tools)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/server.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In server.ts add `export function wrapToolHandler(tool: string, handler: ToolHandler, deps: { limiter; logger; principalOf; sessionOf }): ToolHandler`. Per invocation the wrapper: resolves principalSub + sessionId from the session context (asr-xcs.2) and a fresh traceId (randomUUID); calls `deps.limiter.check(principalSub, tool)` and on `{ok:false}` logs outcome 'error' with code -32005 and throws `rateLimitedError(retryAfterSeconds)` (asr-3f8.1) BEFORE running the handler; otherwise times the handler and in a finally emits `logInvocation` (asr-3f8.2) with durationMs and outcome ('error' + the McpToolError.code when the handler throws). Route the existing register* tool bindings through wrapToolHandler inside createMcpServer.

Acceptance:
vitest integration: invoking one registered read tool 601 times as a single principal makes the 601st throw code -32005 with a numeric retryAfterSeconds; a captured telemetry record for one successful invocation has exactly the six telemetry fields and contains no tool input/output; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Limiter check happens BEFORE the handler runs (reject early); telemetry emits in a finally so errors are still logged. Do not change the limiter windows (asr-3f8.1) or the log shape (asr-3f8.2). Per-session principal/sessionId resolution must come from the asr-xcs.2 binding, never from tool input.

title: Add no-leak structured telemetry logger for MCP invocations
description:
Parent story: asr-3f8
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#telemetry (lines 148-153)

Context:
Each MCP tool invocation must emit exactly one structured log line for observability, but tool inputs and outputs may contain skill content and must never be logged. Isolating the emitter guarantees the six-field shape and the no-leak guarantee in one place that the invocation wrapper (asr-3f8.3) calls.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/telemetry.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/telemetry.test.ts (new)

Change:
Using pino (already a dep, submission-package.md line 90), export `interface InvocationLog { traceId: string; sessionId: string; principalSub: string; tool: string; durationMs: number; outcome: 'ok' | 'error'; code?: number }` and `function logInvocation(log: InvocationLog, logger = baseLogger): void`. It logs ONLY those fields (success: the six fields; error: the six fields plus the numeric MCP error `code`). The function must accept no inputs/outputs/results parameter at all, and on outcome 'error' it logs the code only — never a message string, stack, or exception object. Export the configured `baseLogger` (pino) so callers share one instance.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/telemetry.test.ts (pino destination stubbed to capture the JSON record): logInvocation for a success writes a record whose keys are exactly {traceId,sessionId,principalSub,tool,durationMs,outcome}; an error record additionally has a numeric `code` and still has no `stack`, `err`, `input`, `output`, or `result` key; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
The function signature must make leaking impossible — do not accept tool args/results as parameters. Do not implement rate limiting (asr-3f8.1) or the wrapper that calls this (asr-3f8.3). traceId/sessionId are supplied by the caller (the wrapper), not generated here.

title: Add per-principal MCP rate limiter
description:
Parent story: asr-3f8
Parent epic:  asr-2jw
Spec ref:     specs/mcp.md#rate-limiting (line 111: review_decision 60/min/sub); specs/registry-api.md#rate-limiting (authenticated 600 req/min/sub); specs/mcp.md#error-envelope (rate_limited -32005, line 65)

Context:
MCP tool invocations must be capped per principal to mirror the REST registry limits, with the only mutating tool (review_decision) held to 60/min/sub. Centralising the limiter as a pure module lets the invocation wrapper (asr-3f8.3) gate every tool uniformly and return a spec-correct rate_limited error with a retry hint.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/mcp/rateLimit.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/mcp/rateLimit.test.ts (new)

Change:
Implement a fixed-window per-(principalSub, bucket) counter. Export `type ToolClass = 'mutating' | 'read'` and `function toolClass(tool: string): ToolClass` returning 'mutating' for 'review_decision' else 'read'. Export `function createRateLimiter(now = () => Date.now())` returning `{ check(principalSub: string, tool: string): { ok: true } | { ok: false; retryAfterSeconds: number } }` with limits mutating = 60/60s, read = 600/60s (registry-api.md authenticated). On exceed return `{ ok:false, retryAfterSeconds }` = whole seconds until the current window rolls over. Export `function rateLimitedError(retryAfterSeconds: number)` returning `mcpError(MCP_ERROR.rate_limited, 'rate_limited', { retryAfterSeconds })` (asr-x16.1).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/mcp/rateLimit.test.ts (injected clock): the 61st review_decision call within one window for principal 'p1' returns { ok:false } with a numeric retryAfterSeconds in 1..60; a read tool allows 600 then blocks the 601st; advancing the injected clock past the window resets the count to allow again; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
In-process counters only (single write replica per submission-package.md#concurrency-model) — no Redis. Do not wrap the tool handlers here (that is asr-3f8.3). Keep the -32005 code and the retryAfterSeconds key exact (clients branch on them).

title: Add asr update with diff summary
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr update [<owner/skill>] — bump to latest non-yanked, print diff summary)

Context:
Users keep skills current: update one or all installed skills to the latest non-yanked version and see what changed. Builds directly on the install orchestrator (K10) and the lockfile (K9).

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts:357-419 (rewrite `update [slug]`)
- packages/cli/src/install.ts (add updateSkill helper reusing installSkill)
- packages/cli/src/install.test.ts (extend)

Change:
install.ts: `updateSkill(name?)`: for the named skill or every entry in the lockfiles, read the installed version, getSkillDetail -> latestVersion (non-yanked); if newer, installSkill at latestVersion and print `${owner}/${name}: ${old} -> ${new}`; if already latest print "up to date". index.ts wires `update [slug]` to it.

Acceptance:
vitest (install.test.ts) with an installed skill at 1.0.0 and the registry reporting latest 1.2.0: updateSkill('owner/skill') reinstalls 1.2.0 and prints "owner/skill: 1.0.0 -> 1.2.0".

Risks / out of scope:
Reuse installSkill (K10) — do not duplicate download/verify logic. Skip yanked latest (already enforced by K11). No interactive prompts.
acceptance_criteria: updateSkill reinstalls latest non-yanked and prints old to new diff

title: Add asr remove command
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr remove <owner/skill>)

Context:
Users must uninstall a skill: delete its files from each agent scope it was installed to and drop its lockfile entry. The legacy `remove <name>` (packages/cli/src/index.ts:446-465) uses the cursor/claude/project model and a bare name; the spec uses an owner/skill slug and the Claude/Codex model.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts:446-465 (rewrite `remove <slug>`)
- packages/cli/src/commands.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Rewrite `remove <slug>` to parse owner/name and, for each detectAgents() target (and both project + global when --global), rm -rf agentSkillDir(agent,name,{global}) and removeFromLock the entry. Print which scopes were cleaned; warn if nothing was installed.

Acceptance:
vitest (commands.test.ts) with a temp cwd containing an installed skill dir + lock entry: `remove owner/skill` deletes the .claude/skills/skill directory and removes the lockfile entry.

Risks / out of scope:
No registry call (purely local). Do not remove the legacy add/update commands here.
acceptance_criteria: asr remove owner/skill deletes the skill dir and removes the lock entry

title: Add asr list for installed skills (project + global)
description:
Parent story: asr-5wr
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr list — installed skills, project + global)

Context:
Users need to see what is installed and at which version/source. The legacy `list [repo]` (packages/cli/src/index.ts:132-183) conflates GitHub repo listing with installed listing; the spec's `asr list` lists installed skills from the lockfiles across both scopes.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts:132-183 (replace `list` to read installed from lockfiles)
- packages/cli/src/commands.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Rewrite `list` to read .agent/asr.lock.json for both project (cwd) and global (home) scopes via lockfile.ts getAllInstalled, printing for each: `${name}  v${version}  [${scope}]  <- ${sourceUrl||source}`. When none installed print "No skills installed."

Acceptance:
vitest (commands.test.ts) with two lockfile entries (one project, one global) stubbed: `list` prints both skills with their versions and a project/global scope tag.

Risks / out of scope:
Drop the GitHub repo-listing behavior of the old command (its listSkillsInRepo path) — that GitHub path violates project rules. Do not change the lockfile schema (K9 owns that).
acceptance_criteria: asr list prints project and global installed skills with versions

title: Add asr versions command marking yanked and latest
description:
Parent story: asr-8ce
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr versions); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsowername (versions[] yanked-marked); specs/versioning.md#latest-version-resolution

Context:
Users must see every version including yanked ones, with the installable latest highlighted, before pinning a version. The detail endpoint returns versions[] (yanked included and marked) plus a server-computed latestVersion (semver.rsort over non-yanked, non-rejected).

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (add a `versions <slug>` command)
- packages/cli/src/commands.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add commander command `versions <slug>`: split slug, call getSkillDetail(owner,name), iterate detail.versions printing each `${version}  ${publishedAt}`; append `(yanked: ${yankReason})` in red when version.yanked; mark the entry whose version === detail.latestVersion with a green "<- latest" suffix. Display newest-first by publishedAt.

Acceptance:
vitest (commands.test.ts) with getSkillDetail mocked to a detail whose versions include one yanked (1.1.0) and latestVersion 1.2.0: output marks 1.1.0 as yanked and tags 1.2.0 with "<- latest".

Risks / out of scope:
Do not use the separate /versions endpoint (listVersions excludes yanked, so the detail endpoint is the correct source for marking yanked). No diff rendering.
acceptance_criteria: asr versions marks a yanked version and tags the latest non-yanked one

title: Add asr info command for skill detail
description:
Parent story: asr-8ce
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr info); specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownername

Context:
Users need a detail view for a single skill before installing. No `info` command exists today. It renders the SkillDetail returned by GET /api/v1/skills/:owner/:name.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (add a new `info <slug>` command near the search command, around line 131)
- packages/cli/src/commands.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add commander command `info <slug>` that splits slug into owner/name (error on bad format), calls getSkillDetail(owner, name) from registry-client.ts, and prints: name+owner header, latestVersion, description, tags joined, downloadCount, and riskAssessmentLatest.

Acceptance:
vitest (commands.test.ts) with getSkillDetail mocked: `info owner/code-review` prints the latestVersion, description, and riskAssessmentLatest fields from the mocked SkillDetail to stdout.

Risks / out of scope:
No version-list rendering here (that is `asr versions`, K4). No manifest/artifact download.
acceptance_criteria: asr info owner/skill renders latestVersion, description and riskAssessmentLatest

title: Wire asr search to the registry read client
description:
Parent story: asr-8ce
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (asr search <query>); specs/registry-api.md#get-skills

Context:
`asr search` must query the private Registry API, but the current command (packages/cli/src/index.ts:102-131) searches GitHub via @asr/core's searchSkillRepos — a violation of the project's "never reference GitHub" rule and the wrong data source. This rewires it to the K1 registry read client.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/index.ts:102-131 (replace the `search <query>` action body)
- packages/cli/src/commands.test.ts (new)

Change:
Replace the search action to call searchSkills(query) from registry-client.ts and print one row per result: `${owner}/${name}  v${latestVersion}  (downloads ${downloadCount})` followed by the dim description. On empty items print "No skills found." Remove the GitHub searchSkillRepos call and the -t/--token GitHub option; an optional registry token override may be sourced from config.

Acceptance:
vitest (commands.test.ts) with searchSkills mocked to return two SkillSummary items: invoking the search action prints both `owner/name` rows with their latestVersion and downloadCount to stdout (assert via captured console.log).

Risks / out of scope:
Do not touch the `browse` command (index.ts:63-99) here. Do not remove searchSkillRepos from @asr/core (other legacy commands still import it until their own migration tasks).
acceptance_criteria: asr search foo prints owner/name rows with latestVersion and downloadCount

title: Wire asr status and asr submissions commands
description:
Parent story: asr-rtz
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#commands (lines 38-39); specs/api.md (GET /submissions, GET /submissions/:id)

Context:
Submitters need to check a submission and list their own without re-publishing. status fetches one submission detail; submissions lists the caller submissions.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/commands/submissions.ts (new)
- packages/cli/src/index.ts (register status and submissions)

Change:
Add commands/submissions.ts registering asr status <submission-id> (GET /submissions/:id via apiFetch, render id + current workflow phase + key fields) and asr submissions (GET /submissions scoped to the caller, render a table of id/status/createdAt). Register both in index.ts.

Acceptance:
vitest against a stub API: asr status <id> renders the returned submission detail JSON fields; asr submissions renders the list rows returned by the stub.

Risks / out of scope:
Read-only; depends on api.ts. Do not implement compliance approve/reject here (those are MCP/web flows).

title: Add silent access-token refresh accessor
description:
Parent story: asr-thq
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     specs/cli-integration.md#token-storage (line 69: silent refresh then fall back to device code)

Context:
Access tokens expire; commands should transparently refresh using the stored refresh token, and only re-trigger the device-code flow when refresh is impossible. This is the accessor every authed command calls before making a request.

Affected files:
- packages/cli/src/auth/session.ts (new: getValidAccessToken)
- packages/cli/src/__tests__/session.test.ts (new)

Change:
Add session.ts exporting getValidAccessToken(baseUrl): Promise<string>. Read stored tokens; if accessToken is unexpired (expiresAt - now > 60s) return it; else if a refreshToken exists POST the OAuth refresh grant to the token endpoint, persist the new tokens, and return the new access token; if refresh fails or no refresh token, throw an AuthRequiredError signalling the caller to prompt asr login.

Acceptance:
vitest against a stub token endpoint: an expired access token with a valid refresh token yields a refreshed access token that is persisted; an expired token whose refresh is rejected throws AuthRequiredError.

Risks / out of scope:
Depends on token-store and device-code. Do not auto-launch the device-code flow inside this accessor; only signal via the error so commands control UX.

title: Add scripts/install.ps1 with SHA-256 verification
description:
Parent story: asr-bl6
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     DESIGN.md#install-scripts -> scripts/install.ps1 (lines 74-108)

Context:
Windows users cannot run install.sh; they need a PowerShell installer with the same SHA-256 integrity guarantee before the asr.mjs bundle is trusted and a launcher shim is created.

Affected files:
- scripts/install.ps1 (new)

Change:
Add a PowerShell 5.1+/7+ script per DESIGN.md lines 76-108: resolve ForgejoUrl (ASR_FORGEJO_URL default), Repo org/aks, Version (args[0] default latest), Dest (ASR_INSTALL_DIR default $LOCALAPPDATA/Programs/asr). Build the asset base for latest vs pinned. Invoke-WebRequest asr.mjs and asr.mjs.sha256, compare Expected vs Get-FileHash SHA256 (lowercased), on mismatch Remove-Item both and throw, on match remove the .sha256 and write an asr.cmd shim (node "%~dp0asr.mjs" %*). ErrorActionPreference Stop.

Acceptance:
pwsh -NoProfile -File scripts/install.ps1 against a local fixture: on a matching hash it writes Dest/asr.cmd and completes; on a tampered asr.mjs it throws SHA-256 mismatch and removes the partial asr.mjs.

Risks / out of scope:
Must work on Windows PowerShell 5.1 (no PowerShell-7-only cmdlets). POSIX path handled by install.sh (separate task). CI on macOS may only static-lint this; full run is a Windows job.

title: Add scripts/install.sh with SHA-256 verification
description:
Parent story: asr-bl6
Parent epic:  asr-klk
Spec ref:     DESIGN.md#install-scripts -> scripts/install.sh (lines 39-72)

Context:
End users install the CLI by piping a published shell script that downloads the asr.mjs bundle from a Forgejo Release and verifies its integrity before trusting it. Supply-chain safety depends on the SHA-256 check aborting the install when the downloaded bundle does not match the published asr.mjs.sha256.

Affected files:
- scripts/install.sh (new)

Change:
Add a POSIX sh script per DESIGN.md lines 41-72: resolve FORGEJO_URL (ASR_FORGEJO_URL default), REPO org/aks, VERSION arg (default latest), DEST (ASR_INSTALL_DIR default $HOME/.local/bin). Build ASSET_BASE for latest vs pinned tag. curl -fsSL asr.mjs and asr.mjs.sha256, compare expected vs shasum -a 256 actual, on mismatch print to stderr, rm the partial files, exit 1. On match remove the .sha256, write a launcher (exec node "$DEST/asr.mjs" "$@"), chmod +x. No GitHub URLs.

Acceptance:
A shell test serving a fixture asr.mjs over a local file/HTTP path: with a matching asr.mjs.sha256 the script writes an executable $DEST/asr and exits 0; with a deliberately wrong asr.mjs.sha256 the script exits non-zero, prints SHA-256 mismatch to stderr, and leaves no asr.mjs in $DEST.

Risks / out of scope:
Use only POSIX sh constructs (no bashisms) so it runs under dash. Windows is handled by install.ps1 (separate task). Do not embed a hardcoded production Forgejo host beyond the documented placeholder default.

title: Wire update flow (resolve current, validate bump, persist diff, route) into POST /submissions
description:
Parent story: asr-ak7
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#update-flow (lines 73-88); specs/versioning.md#re-approval-matrix (lines 90-103); specs/api.md (409 not_greater)

Context:
For a submission of an existing skill the system must resolve the currently published version, reject downgrades, compute and persist the diff, and choose the approval path from the matrix — the behaviour that makes an md-only edit auto-approve while a code change forces compliance review. This composes the already-built primitives at the POST /submissions seam.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.ts (extend the asr-igs.3 POST handler)
- packages/submission/test/integration/submissions.updateFlow.test.ts (new)

Change:
In the POST handler, after extraction/hash, resolve current = resolveLatestVersion(db, manifest.name) (asr-nh4.1); if current present, run validateVersionUpgrade(manifest.version, current) (asr-iew.2) and on {ok:false} return apiError(c,409,'version_not_greater'|'invalid_version'). Build the prior VersionSnapshot from getSkillVersion(db, name, current) + its committed files (or null on first publish) and the incoming snapshot from the extracted files; diff = computeVersionDiff(prior, incoming) (asr-hbr.3); insertVersionDiff(db, submissionId, diff) (asr-ak7 repo); path = selectApprovalPath(diff) (asr-ak7 matrix). Persist the chosen path on the submission status_json (e.g. approvalPath) so the workflow (asr-82o/asr-6hl) branches on it. First publish (current undefined) keeps the existing classify-based path.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/test/integration/submissions.updateFlow.test.ts (app.request, temp sqlite seeded with acme/x@1.0.0 published): POSTing acme/x@1.1.0 whose only change is an edited SKILL.md persists a version_diffs row with risk 'low' and a submission whose status_json.approvalPath === 'auto-approve'; POSTing acme/x@1.1.0 that adds run.py persists risk medium|high and approvalPath 'full-review'; POSTing acme/x@0.9.0 returns 409 version_not_greater; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Compose existing primitives only — do not re-implement diff/risk (asr-hbr), the upgrade rule (asr-iew.2), the resolver (asr-nh4.1) or the diff store (asr-ak7 repo). The content-hash gate (asr-gh1.2) and soft lock (asr-nh4.3) are separate seams in the same handler — keep their ordering. Actually triggering each Flowcraft path (HITL vs compute) is the workflow story (asr-82o/asr-6hl); this task only resolves+validates+persists+selects and records approvalPath.
acceptance_criteria: vitest integration: submitting v1.1.0 over published v1.0.0 with only a markdown edit persists a version_diffs row risk='low' and selects the auto-approve path; a submission adding a code file persists risk medium/high and selects full-review; a v0.9.0 downgrade returns 409; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Add version_diffs insert/read repository to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-ak7
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#storage (version_diffs table, lines 162-170); specs/versioning.md#versiondiff-canonical-type (lines 105-135)

Context:
Each submission of an existing skill persists its VersionDiff so the compliance reviewer UI (specs/web-ui.md approval detail) and the audit trail can show exactly what changed. The 0005 migration (asr-efq.1) creates version_diffs; this is the write/read helper the update-flow handler calls.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/versionDiffs.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/versionDiffs.test.ts (new)

Change:
Import the Database type from ../index.js and VersionDiff from @asr/core (asr-dx6). Add `export function insertVersionDiff(db: Database, submissionId: string, diff: VersionDiff): void` running INSERT OR REPLACE INTO version_diffs (submission_id, from_version, to_version, diff_json, risk, computed_at) VALUES (?, diff.fromVersion||null, diff.toVersion, JSON.stringify(diff), diff.riskAssessment, diff.computedAt). Add `export function getVersionDiff(db: Database, submissionId: string): { diff: VersionDiff; risk: 'low'|'medium'|'high' } | undefined` selecting the row and parsing diff_json back to VersionDiff.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/versionDiffs.test.ts: after runMigrations + a submissions row, insertVersionDiff(db, id, sampleDiff) then getVersionDiff(db, id).diff deep-equals sampleDiff and .risk === sampleDiff.riskAssessment; getVersionDiff for an unknown id is undefined; an attempt to insert risk 'bogus' (constructing a bad diff) throws the CHECK constraint; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Storage helper only — computing the diff is asr-hbr.3 and wiring it into the handler is the asr-ak7 update-flow task. risk is taken from diff.riskAssessment (asr-hbr.2), not recomputed. Depends on asr-efq.1 (table) and asr-dx6 (VersionDiff type). PK is submission_id, so INSERT OR REPLACE makes re-computation idempotent.
acceptance_criteria: vitest: after a submissions row, insertVersionDiff(db, submissionId, diff, risk) then getVersionDiff(db, submissionId) round-trips the VersionDiff (from JSON) with the stored risk; an invalid risk throws (CHECK); pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Add selectApprovalPath re-approval-matrix function to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-ak7
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#re-approval-matrix (lines 90-103); specs/versioning.md#update-flow (lines 73-88)

Context:
The update flow must pick an approval path purely from the computed VersionDiff so the workflow can branch deterministically and the reviewer UI can explain "why re-approval". Centralising the matrix in @asr/core (pure, no IO) lets the submission handler and the workflow share one decision, mirroring assessRisk (asr-hbr.2).

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/versionDiff.ts (extend asr-hbr: add selectApprovalPath)
- packages/core/src/versionDiff.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `export type ApprovalPath = 'auto-approve' | 'full-review' | 'rescan-conditional'` and `export function selectApprovalPath(diff: VersionDiff): ApprovalPath`. Rules from the matrix, evaluated in order: if diff.permissionsExpanded || diff.manifestKindChanged || Object.keys(diff.dependenciesAdded).length>0 || any non-markdown file in filesAdded/filesModified || a new file extension introduced -> 'full-review'; else if Object.keys(diff.dependenciesChanged).length>0 (patch-bump) || (diff.permissionsBefore && !diff.permissionsExpanded && permissions narrowed) -> 'rescan-conditional'; else (markdown-only adds/edits, unchanged permissions) -> 'auto-approve'. Treat a path as markdown if it ends in .md/.markdown.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/versionDiff.test.ts: selectApprovalPath on a diff whose filesModified=['SKILL.md'] with no dep/permission change === 'auto-approve'; on a diff with filesAdded=['run.py'] === 'full-review'; on a diff with dependenciesAdded={'left-pad':'^1'} === 'full-review'; on a diff with only dependenciesChanged={'x':{from:'1.0.0',to:'1.0.1'}} === 'rescan-conditional'; on a diff with permissionsExpanded:true === 'full-review'; pnpm --filter @asr/core test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure function over VersionDiff (asr-dx6) — no DB, no workflow trigger. Reuse the diff fields produced by computeVersionDiff (asr-hbr.3); do not recompute the diff or risk here. Persisting the diff (version_diffs) and triggering the workflow path are separate asr-ak7 tasks.
acceptance_criteria: vitest core: selectApprovalPath(diff) returns 'auto-approve' for an md-only diff; 'full-review' for any code change / new dep / expanded permissions / kind change; 'rescan-conditional' for dependency patch bump or narrowed permissions; pnpm --filter @asr/core test passes.

title: Return 409 version_in_progress via pending-version soft lock in POST /submissions
description:
Parent story: asr-nh4
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#concurrent-submissions (lines 234-241); specs/workflow.md#per-skill-mutex (lines 189-213)

Context:
Two submissions for the same name@version must not coexist. The lock primitive exists (asr-fc6.2 acquire/release over pending_versions) but is not yet wired into the write path. This task takes the lock inside the submissions-insert transaction so a duplicate in-flight pair is rejected with 409 version_in_progress, while different versions of the same skill still proceed.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/submissions.ts (extend the asr-igs.3 POST handler)
- packages/submission/test/integration/submissions.softLock.test.ts (new)

Change:
In the POST /submissions handler, inside the same db.transaction that inserts the submission row, call acquirePendingVersion(db, manifest.name, manifest.version, submissionId) (asr-fc6.2) BEFORE insertSubmission; if it returns false, throw a sentinel the handler maps (outside the tx) to apiError(c, 409, 'version_in_progress', { details:{ name: manifest.name, version: manifest.version } }). On the successful publish/terminal path, releasePendingVersion(db, name, version) is called (or left for the workflow terminal step — reference asr-fc6 crash-resume). Place this check after the content-hash gate (asr-gh1.2) and the semver upgrade validation.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/test/integration/submissions.softLock.test.ts (app.request, temp sqlite): POSTing a valid md-only submission for demo@1.0.0 returns the created response and writes a pending_versions row; a second POST for demo@1.0.0 (while the first row exists) returns 409 with body.error 'version_in_progress'; a POST for demo@1.1.0 returns the normal created response; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
acquirePendingVersion must run inside the caller's insert transaction so it is atomic (asr-fc6.2 returns false on contention rather than throwing). Do not duplicate the lock primitive or open a nested transaction. The publish (different-version) merge-ordering lock is asr-fc6.3, not here. Release-on-terminal semantics belong to the workflow (asr-6hl.3 / asr-fc6); this task only adds the acquire + 409 mapping and may release on the synchronous reject path.
acceptance_criteria: vitest integration: a first POST /submissions for name@version succeeds; a concurrent second POST for the same name@version returns 409 version_in_progress; a POST for a different version of the same skill is accepted; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Resolve latest from skill_versions (excluding yanked) in GET /skills/:owner/:name
description:
Parent story: asr-nh4
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/registry-api.md#get-skillsownername (lines 59-77); specs/versioning.md#latest-version-resolution (lines 219-232)

Context:
The Phase-1 detail handler (asr-4it.2) computes latest from the submissions table and explicitly ignores yanks. The versioning epic must make GET /skills/:owner/:name authoritative from skill_versions so the resolved latest excludes yanked versions and the versions[] array carries yank state — the contract MCP and CLI depend on.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/registry.ts (extend the asr-4it.2 GET /:owner/:name handler)
- packages/submission/src/http/registry.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In the GET '/:owner/:name' handler, after loading the SkillDetail (asr-4it.1 getPublishedSkill), override version data from skill_versions: build versions[] from listVersions(db, name) (asr-nh4 resolver task) mapping each row to { version, contentHash: content_hash, publishedAt: published_at, yanked: row.yanked_at !== null } and set detail.latest = resolveLatestVersion(db, name). If resolveLatestVersion is undefined (all yanked / none published) keep the response but latest = null. Return the merged SkillDetail (still 404 via apiError when getPublishedSkill is undefined).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/http/registry.test.ts (app.request, temp sqlite seeded via skills + skillVersions repos): with acme/x rows 1.0.0, 1.1.0 published and 1.2.0 yanked, GET /api/v1/skills/acme/x returns 200 whose body.latest === '1.1.0' and body.versions contains an entry { version:'1.2.0', yanked:true }; GET for a missing skill still returns 404; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Extend the existing asr-4it.2 handler at the post-load seam — do not fork registryRoutes or change the list endpoint. Reuse resolveLatestVersion/listVersions (asr-nh4 resolver) — do not re-implement semver ordering. The download endpoint (asr-4it.3) and MCP yank rejection (asr-76e.3) consume this contract separately. Keep the route unauthenticated (asr-4it.2).
acceptance_criteria: vitest integration: with acme/x having published 1.0.0,1.1.0 and yanked 1.2.0, GET /api/v1/skills/acme/x returns 200 with latest==='1.1.0' and versions[] including the yanked one marked yanked:true; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Add resolveLatestVersion + listVersions to skill_versions repository
description:
Parent story: asr-nh4
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#latest-version-resolution (lines 219-232); specs/versioning.md#version-rules (yanked not latest, line 49)

Context:
There must be exactly ONE canonical "latest" resolver, reused by the read API (asr-4it.2 detail), MCP (asr-76e.2/.3) and CLI (asr-w51), so a yanked version can never regress to latest. Per the spec, ordering defers to semver.rsort (not SQL), so this reads candidate rows then sorts in JS via the @asr/core wrapper.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skillVersions.ts (extend asr-ai5.1)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skillVersions.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Import rsortVersions from @asr/core (asr-iew.1). Add `export function listVersions(db: Database, skillName: string): SkillVersionRow[]` (SELECT * FROM skill_versions WHERE skill_name=? — caller-visible incl. yanked). Add `export function resolveLatestVersion(db: Database, skillName: string): string | undefined`: select versions WHERE skill_name=? AND yanked_at IS NULL, map to version strings, return rsortVersions(versions)[0] (undefined if none). Ordering MUST come from rsortVersions, not SQL.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skillVersions.test.ts: after inserting skill_versions rows 1.0.0, 1.1.0, 1.2.0 then markVersionYanked on 1.2.0, resolveLatestVersion(db,'x') === '1.1.0' and listVersions(db,'x').length === 3; with all versions yanked resolveLatestVersion is undefined; resolveLatestVersion(db,'missing') is undefined; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Resolver/reader only — the GET handler wiring is asr-nh4 (detail task) and the 409 soft lock is the other asr-nh4 task. Defer ordering to rsortVersions (asr-iew.1); the SQL ORDER BY in the spec is illustrative only — do not implement semver ordering in SQL. Depends on asr-ai5.1 (skillVersions repo + markVersionYanked from asr-sm0.1 for the test).
acceptance_criteria: vitest: with skill_versions 1.0.0,1.1.0,1.2.0 where 1.2.0 yanked, resolveLatestVersion(db,name)==='1.1.0' and listVersions(db,name) returns all three with yanked flags; resolveLatestVersion for an unknown skill is undefined; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Implement POST yank endpoint with separation-of-duties and version.yanked emit
description:
Parent story: asr-sm0
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#endpoint (lines 198-205); specs/versioning.md#yank-flow (lines 188-196); specs/audit.md (version.yanked action)

Context:
The compliance-facing action that withdraws a published version. It must enforce separation of duties (a Compliance role whose sub differs from the version's published_by — mirroring the approve check asr-kkd.4), then atomically mark the version yanked, block its hash, commit YANKED.md to Git, and emit the version.yanked audit event so the action is tamper-evident.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/http/yank.ts (new — exports yankRoutes)
- packages/submission/src/index.ts (mount app.route('/api/v1/skills', yankRoutes) behind auth)
- packages/submission/test/integration/yank.test.ts (new)

Change:
Build yankRoutes = new Hono(). POST '/:owner/:name/versions/:version/yank' with body { reason: string (>=1), severity: 'low'|'high'|'critical' }: resolve the principal (Compliance role required, else apiError 403); load the version via getSkillVersion (asr-ai5.1); 404 if missing; if principal.sub === row.published_by -> apiError(c,403,'separation_of_duties'). In one db.transaction: markVersionYanked(db, name, version, { yankedAt, yankedBy: principal.sub, reason }) (asr-sm0.1); insertBlockedHash(db, { content_hash: row.content_hash, skill_name: name, version, blocked_at: now, blocked_by: principal.sub, reason, source: 'yanked' }) (asr-sm0.1); emitAudit(db, { action:'version.yanked', skillName:name, version, actor:principal.sub, actorType:'compliance', detail:{ reason, severity } }) (asr-5qw.2). After commit, await forgejo.commitFileToMain({ owner, name, path:'skills/'+owner+'/'+name+'/YANKED.md', content: Buffer.from('# Yanked '+version+'\n'+reason+'\n'), message:'yank '+name+'@'+version, idempotencyKey:'yank-'+name+'-'+version }) (asr-sm0.2). Return c.json({ yanked:true, blocked_hash: row.content_hash }, 201).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/test/integration/yank.test.ts (app.request, temp sqlite, mocked ForgejoClient + AUDIT_HMAC env): with acme/x@1.0.0 published_by 'alice', POST .../1.0.0/yank as Compliance 'carol' { reason:'leak', severity:'high' } returns 201 { yanked:true, blocked_hash } and afterwards getSkillVersion shows yanked_at set, getBlockedHash(row hash).source==='yanked', and resolveLatestVersion excludes 1.0.0; POST as Compliance 'alice' (==published_by) returns 403 separation_of_duties; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
DB writes + audit emit run in the caller's db.transaction (emitAudit must not open its own — asr-5qw.2); the Forgejo commit happens after the tx (network, idempotent). Reuse apiError (asr-igs.1) and the role/principal resolution from the approve path (asr-kkd.4). Re-publish refusal is already enforced by asr-gh1.2 via blocked_hashes — not duplicated here. The CLI surface (asr install refuses yanked, asr yank command) is asr-w51, not here.
acceptance_criteria: vitest integration: POST /skills/acme/x/versions/1.0.0/yank as a Compliance user != published_by returns 201 {yanked:true,blocked_hash}; the version is then yanked (getSkillVersion) and in blocked_hashes (source 'yanked') and excluded from resolveLatestVersion; a Compliance user == published_by returns 403; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Add ForgejoClient.commitFileToMain for marker commits (YANKED.md)
description:
Parent story: asr-sm0
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#yank-flow (step 7 YANKED.md commit, line 196); specs/git-integration.md#client-forgejoclient (lines 96-166); specs/versioning.md#invariant (no direct write to main, lines 5-7)

Context:
The yank flow must record a YANKED.md in Git so history reflects the action, and per the no-direct-write-to-main invariant that commit must still go through a PR + merge. publishMdOnly (asr-6hl.3) commits skill files via openSubmissionPR (keyed on a submission); a yank is not a submission, so ForgejoClient needs a small public helper to commit a single marker file through branch -> PR -> merge.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/forgejo.ts (add public commitFileToMain to ForgejoClient)
- packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `async commitFileToMain(input: { owner: string; name: string; path: string; content: Buffer; message: string; idempotencyKey: string }): Promise<{ sha: string }>`: createOrGetBranch('marker/'+idempotencyKey) (asr-she.2); putFile(branch, input.path, input.content, idempotencyKey) (asr-she.2); open a PR for that branch via the same upload-token POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls used by openSubmissionPR (asr-she.3) with title input.message; mergePR(prNumber) (asr-she.4, merge token) -> { sha }; deleteBranch(branch). Tolerate 409/405/404 like the other flows. Return { sha }.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/forgejo.test.ts with a mocked Forgejo (nock/msw on cfg.baseUrl): commitFileToMain({ owner:'acme', name:'x', path:'skills/acme/x/YANKED.md', content:Buffer.from('yanked'), message:'yank x@1.0.0', idempotencyKey:'yank-x-1.0.0' }) issues the branch POST, the contents PUT to .../YANKED.md, the pulls POST, and the merge POST via the MERGE-token client, returning the mocked merge_commit_sha; pnpm --filter @asr/core test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Reuse createOrGetBranch/putFile (asr-she.2), the PR-open path (asr-she.3) and mergePR/deleteBranch (asr-she.4) — do not duplicate Octokit setup (asr-she.1). The merge MUST use the merge token (this.merge), never the upload token. This helper commits exactly one file; multi-file commits stay with openSubmissionPR. No DB writes, no audit emit here.
acceptance_criteria: vitest core with mocked Forgejo: commitFileToMain({owner,name,path:'skills/acme/x/YANKED.md',content,message}) creates/gets a branch, PUTs the file, opens a PR and merges via the merge token, returning the merge sha; pnpm --filter @asr/core test passes.

title: Add yank persistence (markVersionYanked + insertBlockedHash) to @asr/submission
description:
Parent story: asr-sm0
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#yank-flow-security-incident-response (steps 1-2, lines 188-196); specs/versioning.md#storage (blocked_hashes, lines 173-181)

Context:
Yanking a version has two durable effects the endpoint composes: flag the skill_versions row yanked, and add its content hash to the permanent block list so it can never be re-published (the existing submission gate asr-gh1.2 then refuses it). These are the two write helpers; the endpoint (asr-sm0 handler task) calls them inside one transaction.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skillVersions.ts (extend asr-ai5.1: add markVersionYanked)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/versions.ts (extend asr-gh1.1: add insertBlockedHash)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/skillVersions.test.ts (extend)
- packages/submission/src/db/repositories/versions.test.ts (extend)

Change:
In skillVersions.ts add `export function markVersionYanked(db: Database, skillName: string, version: string, input: { yankedAt: string; yankedBy: string; reason: string }): boolean` running `UPDATE skill_versions SET yanked_at=?, yanked_by=?, yank_reason=? WHERE skill_name=? AND version=? AND yanked_at IS NULL` and returning info.changes === 1 (already-yanked or missing -> false). In versions.ts add `export function insertBlockedHash(db: Database, row: BlockedHashRow): void` running INSERT INTO blocked_hashes (content_hash, skill_name, version, blocked_at, blocked_by, reason, source) VALUES (?,...). Reuse the existing BlockedHashRow interface (asr-gh1.1). Neither opens its own transaction.

Acceptance:
vitest: in skillVersions.test.ts, after insertSkillVersion of a non-yanked row, markVersionYanked(db,'x','1.0.0',{yankedAt:'2026-01-01T00:00:00Z',yankedBy:'compliance',reason:'leak'}) returns true and getSkillVersion shows yanked_at/by/reason set; a second markVersionYanked returns false; in versions.test.ts, insertBlockedHash({content_hash:'sha256:ab',skill_name:'x',version:'1.0.0',blocked_at:'...',blocked_by:'compliance',reason:'leak',source:'yanked'}) then getBlockedHash(db,'sha256:ab').source === 'yanked'; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Two trivial writes used together for yank — no HTTP, no Forgejo, no audit emit here (the endpoint task composes them). markVersionYanked is idempotent (WHERE yanked_at IS NULL). Depends on asr-ai5.1 (skillVersions repo), asr-gh1.1 (versions repo + BlockedHashRow), asr-efq.1 (tables). Re-publish refusal itself is asr-gh1.2 (already gates on blocked_hashes) — not re-implemented here.
acceptance_criteria: vitest: after seeding a skill_versions row, markVersionYanked(db, name, version, {at,by,reason}) sets yanked_at/by/reason (verified via getSkillVersion) and returns true; a second call for a missing version returns false; insertBlockedHash(db,{...,source:'yanked'}) then getBlockedHash returns source 'yanked'; pnpm --filter @asr/submission test passes.

title: Implement computeVersionDiff over two skill snapshots in @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-hbr
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#versiondiff-canonical-type (lines 105-135)

Context:
Before classification the workflow needs a structured diff between the prior published version and the incoming one so it can branch on permissionsExpanded and persist a reviewer-facing record. This assembles the canonical VersionDiff from two snapshots and stamps risk via assessRisk.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/versionDiff.ts (extend: add VersionSnapshot + computeVersionDiff)
- packages/core/src/versionDiff.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add `export interface VersionSnapshot { version: string; contentHash: string; files: Record<string,string>; manifest: SkillManifest }` (files map path→blob SHA-256; SkillManifest from './types.js'). Add `export function computeVersionDiff(from: VersionSnapshot | null, to: VersionSnapshot): VersionDiff` populating: skillName=to.manifest.name; fromVersion=from?.version ?? ''; toVersion=to.version; fromContentHash=from?.contentHash ?? null; toContentHash=to.contentHash; filesAdded/Removed/Modified (compare path sets and blob hashes); dependenciesAdded/Removed/Changed (diff from/to manifest.dependencies, treating undefined as {}); permissionsBefore=from?.manifest.permissions ?? null; permissionsAfter=to.manifest.permissions; permissionsExpanded=isPermissionsExpanded(before, after); manifestKindChanged=(from!=null && (from.manifest.kind!==to.manifest.kind || from.manifest.persona_mode!==to.manifest.persona_mode)); computedAt=new Date().toISOString(); riskAssessment=assessRisk(thisDiff).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/versionDiff.test.ts: computeVersionDiff(null, snapshot) returns fromVersion '' and fromContentHash null with all files in filesAdded; a to-snapshot adding a dependency populates dependenciesAdded and yields riskAssessment 'high'; a to-snapshot widening permissions sets permissionsExpanded true; a markdown-only edit (one .md blob hash changed) yields filesModified=['SKILL.md'] and risk 'low'; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Operates on caller-provided blob-hash maps — does not read files or zips. Reuse isPermissionsExpanded (asr-hbr.1) and assessRisk (asr-hbr.2); do not duplicate that logic. VersionDiff type must already exist from asr-4ea — do not redefine it.

title: Add assessRisk VersionDiff risk classifier to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-hbr
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#versiondiff-canonical-type (lines 130-133 — risk auto-assessment rules)

Context:
Reviewers and the workflow need a single deterministic risk label for each version change. assessRisk is a pure function of an already-populated VersionDiff (type from @asr/core, asr-4ea), keeping the policy in one auditable place separate from diff construction.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/versionDiff.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/versionDiff.test.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (add `export * from './versionDiff.js';`)

Change:
Add `export function assessRisk(diff: VersionDiff): 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'` (import VersionDiff from './types.js'). high if any: dependenciesAdded non-empty, dependenciesChanged non-empty, permissionsExpanded true, manifestKindChanged true, or any filesAdded entry is non-markdown (path not ending .md/.markdown). medium if (no high trigger and) any filesModified entry is non-markdown (code edits within existing files). low otherwise (markdown-only changes with narrowed/unchanged permissions).

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/versionDiff.test.ts: a diff with only filesModified=['SKILL.md'] and permissionsExpanded=false → 'low'; a diff with dependenciesAdded={lodash:'^4'} → 'high'; a diff with permissionsExpanded=true → 'high'; a diff with filesModified=['src/run.ts'] and no deps/perms changes → 'medium'; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure function over the diff fields — do not recompute permissionsExpanded here (consume the boolean already on the diff). Markdown detection is by extension only. No file IO.

title: Add permissions-expansion comparator to @asr/core
description:
Parent story: asr-hbr
Parent epic:  asr-cg4
Spec ref:     specs/versioning.md#re-approval-matrix (lines 100-103 — permissions expanded vs narrowed); specs/types.md#skill-manifest (PermissionsManifest, lines 13-19)

Context:
Whether a new version expands the permissions manifest decides re-approval routing (expanded → always full compliance review) and feeds the VersionDiff risk score. This is a pure comparison over the canonical PermissionsManifest type so the workflow can branch on it before classification.

Affected files:
- packages/core/src/permissions.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/permissions.test.ts (new)
- packages/core/src/index.ts (add `export * from './permissions.js';`)

Change:
Add `export function isPermissionsExpanded(before: PermissionsManifest | null, after: PermissionsManifest): boolean` (import PermissionsManifest from './types.js'). Return true if before is null and after grants any capability; otherwise true if any of: network false→true; subprocess false→true; filesystem widens along none<read-own<read-write-own; networkHosts adds a host not previously allowed (treat absent/empty as no hosts); environment adds a var not present in before. Narrowing or unchanged → false.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/core/src/permissions.test.ts: enabling network (false→true) → true; widening filesystem 'read-own'→'read-write-own' → true; adding an environment var → true; removing a capability (network true→false) → false; identical manifests → false; before=null with a no-capability manifest (network false, subprocess false, filesystem 'none', environment []) → false; `pnpm --filter @asr/core test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Pure function only. Do not compute the rest of the VersionDiff here. Define the filesystem ordering exactly as none<read-own<read-write-own so future levels are easy to insert.

title: Emit key.rotated event on rotation
description:
Parent story: asr-tqo
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#hmac-key-management (line 147)

Context:
Rotation must itself be auditable and must add the new key to the ring so subsequent events use it while old events still verify under their stored hmac_key_id. A 'key.rotated' audit event records both old and new key ids.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/keyring.ts (add rotateKey helper)
- packages/submission/src/audit/keyring.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Add function rotateKey(db: Database.Database, keys: KeyRing, newId: string, newBytes: Buffer): void that, inside db.transaction: (1) keys.addKey(newId,newBytes); (2) emits emitAudit(db, { action:'key.rotated', actor:'system', actorType:'system', detail:{ oldKeyId: keys.activeId, newKeyId: newId } }, keys) BEFORE flipping active so the rotation event is signed by the OLD key per spec line 147; (3) sets keys.activeId = newId.

Acceptance:
vitest keyring.test.ts: after emitting one event under k1 then rotateKey(db, ring, 'k2', bytes2), audit_events contains a 'key.rotated' row with detail.oldKeyId==='k1', detail.newKeyId==='k2', signed under hmac_key_id 'k1'; the NEXT emitAudit carries hmac_key_id 'k2'; the original k1 event still verifies via ring.get('k1'); `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Multi-replica SSE refresh (spec line 148) is out of scope -- single-process rotation only. Do NOT purge old keys here (retention/purge is asr-28q). verifyChain consumption of unknown ids is asr-7gx.

title: Sign audit.emit via KeyRing active key
description:
Parent story: asr-tqo
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#hmac-key-management (lines 145-147)

Context:
audit.emit initially reads a single env key. To support rotation it must sign with the KeyRing's active key and stamp the row's hmac_key_id with the active id, so later verification resolves the right key per row. Replaces the single-env-key path from asr-5qw.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/emit.ts (edit emitAudit to accept/use a KeyRing)
- packages/submission/src/audit/emit.test.ts (extend)

Change:
Change emitAudit to take the KeyRing, e.g. emitAudit(db, input, keys: KeyRing = loadKeyRing()). Set hmacKeyId = keys.activeId and key = keys.get(keys.activeId) (throw if undefined). Keep all other behaviour identical (action validation, ulid id, prevHash read, computeHash, insert, caller-owned transaction). Remove the direct process.env.AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES read in favour of the KeyRing.

Acceptance:
vitest emit.test.ts: with a KeyRing whose activeId='k2', emitAudit stamps event.hmacKeyId==='k2' and event.hash === computeHash(event, keys.get('k2')); after addKey('k3',bytes3) and setting active to 'k3', the next emitted event carries hmacKeyId==='k3'; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Do NOT implement rotation event emission here (asr-tqo rotate task). Keep the caller-owns-transaction contract from asr-5qw. Existing emit tests must keep passing -- adjust the test KeyRing setup rather than changing chain semantics.

title: Add HMAC KeyRing loader
description:
Parent story: asr-tqo
Parent epic:  asr-62i
Spec ref:     specs/audit.md#hmac-key-management (lines 143-148)

Context:
Key rotation requires verifying old events against the key that signed them while signing new events with the current key. A KeyRing holds the active 32-byte key plus up to N previous keys and resolves a key by id. This is the indirection that lets audit.emit and verifyChain (asr-7gx) survive rotation.

Affected files:
- packages/submission/src/audit/keyring.ts (new)
- packages/submission/src/audit/keyring.test.ts (new)

Change:
Export interface KeyRing { activeId: string; get(id: string): Buffer | undefined; addKey(id: string, bytes: Buffer): void } and function loadKeyRing(env = process.env): KeyRing. loadKeyRing reads activeId from AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_ID and active key bytes from AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES (base64 -> Buffer; assert 32 bytes else throw). Support N previous keys (default N=3) via env vars of the form AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES_<id> (base64), held in a Map<string,Buffer>; get(id) returns the Buffer or undefined for unknown/purged ids. addKey(id,bytes) inserts a key so the rotate task (asr-tqo) can append the new active key.

Acceptance:
vitest packages/submission/src/audit/keyring.test.ts: with AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_ID='k1', AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES = base64 of a 32-byte key, and AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES_k0 set, loadKeyRing().get('k1') and .get('k0') return 32-byte Buffers, .activeId==='k1', and .get('nope') === undefined; a non-32-byte AUDIT_HMAC_KEY_BYTES makes loadKeyRing throw; `pnpm --filter @asr/submission test` passes.

Risks / out of scope:
Key Vault wiring (pulling secrets in prod) is out of scope -- load from env here; the Azure epic supplies the Key Vault -> env projection. Do NOT change audit.emit signing in this task (asr-tqo wire task). 'unknown key -> fail closed' is verifyChain's behaviour (asr-7gx), which calls get() and treats undefined as valid:false.

title: Story: Zip-upload-path penetration test checklist
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: specs/security.md (zip hardening, yauzl); SPEC.md#security-posture

Goal: Produce docs/pentest-zip-path.md: a repeatable penetration-test checklist for the upload/extract path covering path traversal, zip bombs (ratio + total-size caps), symlink entries, polyglot files, nested archives, and oversized/duplicate entries. Each case maps to the specific yauzl hardening control that must reject it and to a reproducing test fixture/case.

Acceptance: docs/pentest-zip-path.md exists, enumerates the six attack classes above, and each row links to a control in specs/security.md and a named test case; running that test suite shows every malicious fixture is rejected.

title: Story: Workflow notification email delivery (transitions + SLA events)
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: specs/workflow.md#timeout-and-expiry; specs/security-scanning.md#workflow-integration

Goal: Add an email notifier in packages/submission that sends on the key workflow events — questionnaire ready (submitter), scan complete needing review (compliance), approval/rejection (submitter), and SLA expiry/escalation — using a pluggable transport (real SMTP/Graph in prod, in-memory mock in dev/tests). Emails contain the submission deep link and never leak PII beyond the recipient's own address.

Acceptance: an integration test driving a submission to verdict=review_required captures exactly one reviewer email (mock transport) containing the submission link and no other principal's PII.

title: Story: Veracode Tier-3 optional enablement and scanner version pinning
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: specs/security-scanning.md#tier-3--optional-enterprise-configured-via-env; specs/security-scanning.md#veracode-integration-details

Goal: Production-harden the scanner image (built in Phase 2, asr-st5): replace every ':latest' / '@latest' tool reference with validated pinned versions, fix the Veracode CLI invocation to the documented verb, and wire VERACODE_API_KEY_ID/SECRET/POLICY through runScanner so Veracode runs only when credentials are present and is skipped cleanly otherwise.

Acceptance: building the scanner image uses only pinned versions (grep finds no ':latest'/'@latest'); with VERACODE_* unset the orchestrator omits veracode from tools_run and still returns a verdict; with mock creds set the veracode runner is invoked with the documented verb.

title: Story: Production monitoring, health probes, and alert rules
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: specs/deployment.md#monitoring

Goal: Wire the existing API /healthz (Phase 1) and Forgejo /api/v1/version as Container Apps liveness/readiness probes, route all container stdout/stderr to Log Analytics, and define alert rules for error rate > 1%, P95 latency > 2s, and replica restart count.

Acceptance: the api Container App template declares a health probe hitting /healthz; deployed alert rules are listed by 'az monitor metrics alert list' for the three conditions; a forced 5xx burst raises the error-rate alert in Log Analytics.

title: Story: Forgejo Actions deploy pipeline (ACR build + roll Container Apps)
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: specs/deployment.md#cicd-forgejo-actions

Goal: Add .forgejo/workflows/deploy.yml that, on push to main, logs into Azure with the az CLI using a service-principal JSON from secret AZURE_CREDENTIALS_JSON (Forgejo forbids GITHUB_TOKEN/AZURE_CREDENTIALS names), builds asr-api and asr-web in ACR keyed by the commit SHA, then rolls the api and web Container Apps to the new image. The SP JSON is shredded after login.

Acceptance: the workflow file passes a YAML/action lint; a dry-run (act or documented manual trace) shows the four steps (checkout, az login via AZURE_CREDENTIALS_JSON, az acr build x2, az containerapp update x2) execute in order with no GitHub references.

title: Story: Forgejo image ACR mirror and service image build/push
description:
Epic: asr-mfv
Spec: specs/deployment.md#forgejo-image-mirror; specs/deployment.md#cicd-forgejo-actions

Goal: Remove the cross-internet pull on scale-out by mirroring codeberg.org/forgejo/forgejo:15 into ACR via a daily 'az acr import', and build/push the service images (asr-api, asr-web) into ACR via 'az acr build'. The Forgejo Container App must reference $ACR.azurecr.io/forgejo:15, not codeberg.
